author: John Banister
description: Family tree visualization with GEDCOM/Gramps import and Canvas/PDF/Markdown export. Charts, maps, timelines, citations, fictional calendars. For genealogists, worldbuilders, and TTRPG campaigns. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: banisterious
manifest.description: Family tree visualization for genealogists and worldbuilders. Interactive charts, geographic maps, evidence tracking, GEDCOM support, and fictional calendars.
manifest.name: Charted Roots

name: Charted Roots

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
A worldbuilding-and-maps release: a new marriage type field, friendlier place-type hierarchy editing, and a cluster of map and Entity Profile fixes for mixed real-world/fictional vaults. Also bumps bundled dompurify to clear a fresh security advisory.

## New

- **Marriage type field** ([#628](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/628)): record the type of union (Common-law marriage, Cohabitation, Domestic (civil) partnership, Putative marriage, Concubinage, or any custom value) per spouse. Picked in the person modal as a preset or free text, mirrored to both partners, and shown on Dynamic Timeline marriage rows. New "Show marriage type" setting, on by default.

## Improved

- **Place type hierarchy: insert above an occupied level** ([#734](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/734)): placing a type onto a level another already holds now offers a choice, keep them tied or insert above and push the lower types down, so adding a type above the current top is a single action instead of a manual renumber. The per-row Hide and Reset controls are now compact eye and revert-arrow icons.
- **Editing a person ensures `cr_type: person`** ([#744](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/744)): saving in the Edit Person modal fills in a missing `cr_type: person`, so a note that picked up a `cr_id` but skipped "Add essential person properties" gets tagged correctly. An existing type is never overwritten.
- **Entity Profile memberships sorted by start date** ([#743](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/743)): memberships now order by earliest start year (era-aware), undated last.

## Fixed

- **Fictional events no longer appear on the real-world map** ([#747](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/747)): in a mixed vault, events tied to pixel-mapped fictional places leaked onto the real-world map and clustered at 0,0. Each map now draws only the locations in its own coordinate system (markers, places, heat map, and paths).
- **Entity Profile place category shows its label** ([#745](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/745)): the place category heading showed the raw id (e.g. `historical`) instead of "Historical".
- **Map data failures show the actual error** ([#746](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/746)): a failed map refresh now reports the specific error instead of a generic message.

## Security

- **dompurify -> 3.4.11** (transitive via jspdf), clearing.

---

This is a stable release on the v0.22.x line. 1569 tests across 139 suites pass.

Thanks to @Vericia, @DigitalDreamn, @doctorwodka, and @tenephor for the reports and feature requests behind this release.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.73

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Reporter-driven patch fixing two regressions and a crash from the 0.22.71 cohort, plus place type refinements and a dependency security update.

### Fixed
- **Organization members are listed again** when the member's person note is identified only by `cr_id` (no explicit `cr_type`) - a 0.22.71 regression that silently dropped them (#742).
- **Timeline no longer crashes** ("e.trim is not a function") when a note mixes an inline `events:` array with a bare-number date like `born: 1850` (#741).
- **Parent place dropdown** shows the type's display name (not the raw id) for the highest-ranked type (#739).

### Changed
- **Place type reordering** now swaps just the two levels, preserving intentional ties (State/Province, etc.) and gaps; the Customize/Edit actions are now gear/pencil icons (#734 follow-up).

### Security
- Updated the bundled **dompurify to 3.4.10** (via jspdf).

### Stability
No breaking changes. Tests: 1533 passing across 134 suites.

Thanks to @doctorwodka, @tenephor, and @DigitalDreamn for the reports.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.72

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Place type management and consistent display names. The Place type manager gains up/down reordering for categories and the types within them, plus smarter hierarchy-level defaults; several surfaces that showed a raw internal id now show the proper display name; and an organization-membership data-integrity bug is fixed.

### Added
- **Smarter hierarchy ranks for place types** (#734) - each category gets a "+ Add type" button that pre-selects the category and defaults the new type one level deeper than the deepest existing one, plus up/down arrows to reorder types within a category.
- **Reorder place type categories** (#733) - move-up/down controls let any category, custom or built-in, sit anywhere in the list, including above the built-ins.

### Changed
- **Quick level presets** in the place type modal now have breathing room from the text above them (#728).

### Fixed
- **Adding an organization member no longer silently fails** when the person note has a foreign `type` property (e.g. `type: character`) (#738).
- **Organization and source types** now show their display name in the Entity Profile pane instead of the raw id (#735).
- **Place types** show their display name across the Place Statistics card, Places list, and Parent place dropdown (#732).
- **Universe dynamic blocks** show the type's display name instead of its internal id (#731).
- **Statistics date range** splits universe-less fictional dates into their own "Uncategorized" bucket so they don't skew the real-world span (#719).

### Stability
Continues the 0.22.x stability window; no breaking changes. Tests: 1526 passing across 134 suites.

Thanks to @DigitalDreamn and @prayidae for the reports that drove this release.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.71

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Fictional-worldbuilding correctness and entity linking. Calendarium calendars now appear throughout Charted Roots rather than on a single screen, and the integration's settings move next to the calendars they control. Linking a person's place no longer spawns a duplicate note when a place's display name differs from its filename; a marriage location can be unlinked; and the place picker shows each place's parent hierarchy so same-named places can be told apart. The Statistics date range is now era-aware and grouped per universe, so fictional BBY/ABY dates no longer pollute the span, and years before a calendar's earliest era read sensibly. Orphan-organization detection also catches parents referenced only by another organization. **1493 tests passing across 129 suites.**

### Changed

- **Calendarium integration settings moved next to the calendars they control**: The "Calendarium integration" and "Sync Calendarium events" options now live in Settings -> **Fictional date systems** (previously under Advanced -> Integrations), directly above the list where imported Calendarium calendars appear. Toggling the integration now refreshes that list in place. Follow-up to [#725](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/725).

### Fixed

- **Fictional years before a calendar's earliest era now read sensibly instead of as a bare negative** ([#729](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/729)): A date that falls before every era a calendar defines (common when a calendar has only forward eras and no "before"-style era) used to render as a raw signed number (e.g. a Gaean Reach range showing `-29-1538 GR`). It now reads relative to the earliest era, e.g. `29 before GR`. Applies anywhere canonical years are displayed, including the Statistics date range and the map time slider. Surfaced from #719.

- **Statistics date range is now era-aware and grouped per universe** ([#719](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/719)): The Entity overview's "Date range" read the leading four digits of each date, so a fictional `8082 BBY` was treated as the year 8082 and pooled with real-world dates into a nonsensical span. Years are now resolved through the same era-aware machinery the timeline uses (signed canonical years), and the range is grouped per universe. Real-world dates show a plain Gregorian span, fictional universes show an era-correct one (e.g. `8082 BBY-23 ABY`), and a mixed vault lists each separately instead of conflating them. A purely real-world vault is unchanged. The same per-universe range now also feeds the Collections and folder-statistics views. Split from #714; raised in discussion #712 by [@DigitalDreamn](https://github.com/DigitalDreamn).

- **Orphan organizations now include parents referenced only by another organization** ([#708](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/708)): An organization named as another org's `parent_org` (Settings -> "Parent organization") but with no note of its own wasn't listed under Orphan organizations, so it couldn't be created/adopted from there. Parent references are now scanned too, alongside event, person, and membership references. Reported by [@DigitalDreamn](https://github.com/DigitalDreamn).

- **Calendarium calendars now appear in Charted Roots** ([#725](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/725)): With the Calendarium integration set to "read", Calendarium calendars weren't showing in the event modal's date-system dropdown or other menus. The bridge that reads Calendarium only populated its API reference on one code path (the Control Center's Date Systems card), so every other place that asked for calendars saw none. It now reads the Calendarium API wherever it's needed, so calendars appear consistently, and the bridge logs what it finds (or why it found nothing) for easier diagnosis. Note: recognizing Calendarium event dates (`fc-date`) on timelines is a separate switch. Enable **Sync Calendarium events** as well. Reported by [@Fatebreak](https://github.com/Fatebreak).

- **Place linking no longer creates a duplicate note when a place's name differs from its filename** ([#724](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/724)): Linking a person's birth, death, or marriage place to a note filed under a disambiguating filename (for example `Essex-MA-USA.md` with `name: Essex`) created a new `Essex.md` and linked that instead of the existing note. The wikilink builder resolved place (and source) references by looking only for a *person* with the matching id (a regression from #559), so the place's id never resolved and it fell back to filename matching, which a diverging name can't satisfy. It now resolves by the correct note type, so the link lands on the existing place. Two related gaps are fixed alongside: a linked **marriage location can now be unlinked** from the Person modal (the field was link-only), and the **place picker shows each place's parent hierarchy** (e.g. "Massachusetts, United States") so two same-named places can be told apart. Reported by [@tenephor](https://github.com/tenephor).
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.70

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Date handling and event ordering. GEDCOM import now understands many common date formats it used to drop silently, flagging anything ambiguous in the import preview. Computing sort order no longer reports false cycles for reciprocal before/after pairs, and a real loop now opens an inspectable result you can click through to fix each culprit. Fictional/era dates keep month and day, the Statistics date range counts death years, and timeline place context uses a place's name rather than its filename. The Person modal's extended name fields fold into a collapsible section, and the Control Center's orphan organization and universe rows lay out correctly. **1476 tests passing across 128 suites.**

### Added

- **Inspectable result after "Compute sort order"** ([#723](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/723)): When Compute sort order hit a before/after loop, its only feedback was a transient notice. The named events (added in [#721](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/721)) scrolled away and weren't actionable, so fixing a cycle meant hunting down each event by hand. The operation now opens a small result dialog whenever there are cycles or errors: it shows the updated count (or a "nothing to update" state) and lists the events that couldn't be ordered as clickable links that open the note, so you can jump straight to each culprit and fix its "Occurs before/after". A hint notes that opening a note closes the dialog and that re-running Compute shows the list again. The common quick-success case keeps the lightweight toast. Split from [#721](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/721) ([@doctorwodka](https://github.com/doctorwodka)).

### Changed

- **Extended name fields tucked into a collapsible "Extended name options" section** ([#717](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/717)): After the name-prefix/suffix/surname-particle fields were added, the Add / Edit Person modal felt cramped and pushed the non-name options further down. Those fields (along with nickname, given name, surname(s), and (when editing) maiden and married names) now live in a collapsible **Extended name options** section, collapsed by default, leaving just the baseline **Name** field up top. Mirrors the existing "Step & adoptive parents" container. Requested by [@doctorwodka](https://github.com/doctorwodka).

### Fixed

- **Fictional/era dates keep month and day precision** ([#722](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/722)): An era date with month or day precision such as `DE 1264-08-15` was parsed down to just its year, so two events sharing an era-year could only be ordered by a raw-string comparison, which mis-sorts across eras (a December date landing before a March date that shared the same canonical year). The parser now records the month and day, and the event sort orders by them within the same year; negative-era years like `EP -01-12` keep both the negative year and the month. Surfaced while investigating #721 ([@doctorwodka](https://github.com/doctorwodka)).

- **Event sort no longer reports a phantom cycle for reciprocal before/after, and names the events in a real cycle** ([#721](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/721)): When two events expressed the same ordering from both sides; one set to "Occurs before" the other, the other set to "Occurs after" the first. **Compute sort order** double-counted that single constraint and wrongly reported the pair as an unorderable cycle, leaving their sort order stale. Reciprocal relationships now order correctly. And when there genuinely is a before/after loop, the notice (and console) now lists the event titles involved instead of only a count (so you can find the culprit) replacing the modal's misleading "see the console for details". Reported by [@doctorwodka](https://github.com/doctorwodka).

- **Timeline place context uses the place's name, not its filename** ([#720](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/720)): The Dynamic Timeline Block's place-context hierarchy (e.g. "Born in London, England") built its leaf segment from the place wikilink's target (the file name) rather than the place note's display name. Vaults that disambiguate same-named places with distinct filenames (two "Essex" notes filed as, say, `Essex-EssexCo-MA-USA` and `Essex-EssexCo-Ontario-Canada`) saw the filename in the timeline instead of "Essex", which made the feature unusable for duplicate place names. The leaf now uses the resolved place's name, so distinctly-filed notes that share a name render correctly. Reported by [@tenephor](https://github.com/tenephor).

- **Statistics Dashboard date range now counts death years** ([#714](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/714)): The "Date range" line in the Entity overview anchored each person on a single year (their birth, or their death only if no birth was recorded) so death dates were dropped whenever a birth year existed. The range understated the period the collection covers and could collapse to a single point like "1900-1900" for someone who lived 1900–1990. It now spans the earliest and latest of all birth *and* death years, so that person reads "1900-1990 (90 years)". The shared analytics also feed the Control Center stats tab, reports, and collections view. Reported by [@DigitalDreamn](https://github.com/DigitalDreamn).

- **Orphan organization and universe rows now lay out correctly** ([#708](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/708)): The rows in the Control Center's **Orphan organizations** and **Orphan universe values** cards relied on layout classes that were never defined, so the name, reference count, and **Create note** button ran together with no spacing and the buttons didn't line up. The rows now use a proper flex layout. Name and count spaced out, buttons right-aligned.

- **Data Quality ops no longer mishandle parenthetical name disambiguators** ([#715](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/715)): People disambiguated with a balanced parenthetical in the note title (for example `Jon Smith (son of Robert)`) were misread by two batch operations. "Remove placeholder values" flagged such references for deletion (its malformed-link check matched any link ending in `)`), and "Normalize name formatting" suggested re-casing the annotation to `(Son Of Robert)`. Balanced parentheticals are now recognised as valid disambiguators and left untouched by both. Reported by [@Vericia](https://github.com/Vericia).

- **GEDCOM import no longer silently drops common date formats** ([#716](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/716)): Several widely-used date formats imported blank with no warning — full month names (`27 June 1885`), month and year (`October 1848`), qualifiers with a trailing period or synonym (`Abt. 1809`, `About 1870`, `Circa`), `DD/MM/YYYY` slash dates, and dates carrying an event label (`Bapt 18 Dec 1690`, `Buried 11 April 1758`). These now parse correctly. Dates that are genuinely ambiguous (`05/06/1990`, read as day/month), recovered from an event label, or unparseable are listed in the import preview's warnings panel so you can review them before importing, instead of disappearing. Reported by [@oliverclock](https://github.com/oliverclock).
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.69

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Feature release centered on Organizations, with a timeline enhancement and an Obsidian 1.13.1 fix alongside. You can now create an organization inline from the Manage memberships modal and adopt orphaned organizations (names referenced by events or people that never got a note) from the Control Center, bringing Organizations to parity with Universes. The Person modal gains optional name-prefix, name-suffix, and surname-particle fields, and the Dynamic Timeline Block's place context becomes depth-configurable, up to the full location hierarchy. The settings tab, which rendered blank on Obsidian 1.13.1, is also restored. **1420 tests passing across 122 suites.**

### Added

- **Timeline place context can show the full location hierarchy** ([#705](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/705)): Choose how many parent levels to append. Set **Place context depth** under Settings -> Timeline -> Event display (1 = immediate parent; 0 = full hierarchy), or override per block with `place_context: <number>` / `place_context: full`. Long places wrap so deep hierarchies don't overflow. Requested by [@tenephor](https://github.com/tenephor), with suggestions from [@DigitalDreamn](https://github.com/DigitalDreamn).

- **Create an organization from the Manage memberships modal** ([#710](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/710)): The organization dropdown now offers **+ New organization**, so you can create one without leaving the modal. Continues [#700](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/700). Requested by [@DigitalDreamn](https://github.com/DigitalDreamn).

- **Name prefix, suffix, and surname-particle fields in the Person modal** ([#709](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/709)): The Add / Edit Person modal gains optional **Name prefix** (Dr., Rev.), **Name suffix** (Jr., III), and **Surname prefix** (von, de la) inputs, mapping to `name_prefix` / `name_suffix` / `surname_prefix`. Stemmed from discussion [#706](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/discussions/706) ([@Vericia](https://github.com/Vericia)).

- **Adopt orphaned organizations from the Control Center** ([#708](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/708)): The Organizations tab now lists **Orphan organizations** (names referenced by events/people with no note) each with **Create note** (plus **Create all**). Creating the note links existing references and backfills the new id onto referencing person notes. Mirrors the Orphan universe values feature. Requested by [@DigitalDreamn](https://github.com/DigitalDreamn).

### Fixed

- **Settings tab no longer renders blank on Obsidian 1.13.1**: The tab hosted its UI through a render callback that relied on a container argument 1.13.1 stopped providing, so it came up empty on that version. It now renders into the documented setting element; 1.13.0 and earlier are unchanged.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.68

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Small reporter-driven patch alongside a codebase-wide type-checking pass. Family Chart highlight dimming no longer lets the connector lines show through dimmed cards, and the relationships filter now labels user-created relationship categories instead of leaving them blank. Under the hood, the TypeScript codebase is now fully type-checked with zero errors and the type-check gates CI; a sweep that also turned up and fixed a couple of latent bugs. **1397 tests passing across 121 suites.**

### Fixed

- **Highlight dimming no longer shows connector lines through dimmed cards** ([#670](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/670)): The previous fix dimmed non-matching cards by making them translucent, so the connector lines behind them showed through. Dimmed cards now stay fully opaque (dimmed with a filter), so the links behind them are hidden again. Reported by [@DigitalDreamn](https://github.com/DigitalDreamn).

- **Custom relationship categories are labelled in the relationships filter** ([#707](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/707)): In the filter's "By category" dropdown, a category you created yourself appeared as a blank entry (clickable but unlabelled) because the label came from the built-in list only. Custom category names now resolve correctly, in both the Relationships pane and the Control Center Relationships tab. Reported by [@DigitalDreamn](https://github.com/DigitalDreamn).

- **"Find related research" recognizes the open person note** ([#704](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/704)): Running the command while viewing a person note didn't detect it as a person, so it always fell through to the person-picker prompt instead of using the note you were already on. It now uses the active note when it is a person.

- **GEDCOM-X export reports the correct exported count** ([#704](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/704)): The export completion notice always read "0 people exported" because it counted from the wrong object; the exported file itself was correct. The notice now reports the actual numbers written.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.67

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Timelines, organizations, and the Family Chart.

### Added
- **Personal events appear on timelines** (#692): a person's inline events list (residence, occupation, immigration, and the like) showed on Maps but never on the Dynamic Timeline Block. Those events now appear on the timeline too, interleaved with births, deaths, and family events in chronological order, and era-aware for fictional calendars.
- **Living-status control in the person modal** (#698): marking a person living or deceased used to mean hand-editing a property, or turning on privacy protection to reveal a hidden override. A Living status dropdown (Automatic / Living / Deceased) now sits under the death fields in both the create and edit person modals, always available.
- **Timelines can show place context** (#701): timeline rows can append a place's parent location, so an event reads "Born in London, England" instead of the ambiguous "Born in London". The parent comes from your place note hierarchy. It is off by default; turn it on under Settings (Timeline, Event display), or per block with `place_context: true`.

### Changed
- **Family Chart focus outline width is adjustable** (#689): the focus (root person) outline shares the connector-line accent colour, so at a fixed width it could still be easy to miss. Its width is now adjustable from 2 to 10 pixels (default 4) through the Style Settings plugin.
- **"Manage memberships" on a person with none prompts you to add one** (#700): choosing Manage memberships on a person who belongs to no organizations used to dead-end with a notice. It now opens the Add membership dialog directly, so you can add their first membership without detouring through the Organizations tab (and it offers to create an organization if none exist).

### Fixed
- **Highlight dimming no longer washes out connector lines** (#670): with Highlight Groups active, the layer that dims non-matching cards also fell across the connector lines routing into them, greying them. Dimming now applies to the card content alone, so the lines keep their full colour.
- **Timeline context events span a person's whole life** (#699): the historical-context lifespan margin was measured only against a person's own milestones, so context during a family-event-heavy period, or the later life of someone with no recorded death, could be dropped. The window now reflects the whole life: family events widen it, a person with no death date extends to their latest family event (or, when marked living, their latest context event), and a recorded death caps it.
- **Organization member lists honour `sort: date`** (#702): the members block accepted sort: date but always fell back to sorting by name. Members now sort by their membership start date, earliest first, undated last, with name as a tiebreak, and fictional BBY/ABY dates order by true chronology.
- **Custom relationships mapped to parent or spouse keep their own type** (#703): a custom type mapped to "parent" or "spouse" was written using the built-in parents/spouse properties, discarding the custom name. The custom field is now preserved (a "Creator" relationship writes creator, for example) and the chart still reads it as a parent or spouse edge.

Thanks to @DigitalDreamn, @doctorwodka, and @tenephor for the reports.

**1393 tests passing across 120 suites.**
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.66

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Timelines, the Family Chart, and import.

### Added
- **Large-import warning before importing** (#688): the import wizard's preview now shows how many notes an import will create and warns when that total is large enough to slow Obsidian down (around 10,000+), suggesting you narrow the file first. The warning appears before anything is written, for every import format.

### Changed
- **Timelines show event icons by default** (#691): the Event type display setting now defaults to "Icon with label" instead of "Text label", so timelines, canvas event nodes, and maps show event icons out of the box. Existing vaults are migrated once on upgrade; if you prefer text only, set it back in Settings and it sticks.
- **Family Chart focus outline is more visible** (#689): the focus (root person) outline is drawn slightly thicker so it stands apart from the connector lines, which share the accent colour. At the old width the default purple accent in dark mode could be easy to miss.

### Fixed
- **Timeline context notes work with fictional calendars** (#693): a context note added to a Dynamic Timeline Block displayed its events only when the dates were four-digit years. Lines dated in a fictional era (like "BBY 32" or "32 BBY") were skipped, so a fictional context note showed nothing. Context dates now understand era abbreviations (either order), short years, and ranges.
- **Mixed-era timelines sort and filter correctly** (#695): a timeline mixing eras such as BBY and ABY ordered events by the bare year number, so an "ABY 40" event could sink below a "BBY 10" one, and context events were kept or dropped by digit size rather than real distance. Ordering and the context margin now follow true chronology. Pure-BBY timelines also read birth-at-top now, like real-world dates.
- **Event icons appear consistently on timelines** (#691): in text mode, a person's own events showed icons only when the timeline also contained a family event, while context and family rows always did. Event icons are now all-or-nothing per timeline, controlled solely by the Event type display setting.
- **Family Chart no longer labels unmarried co-parents as spouses** (#694): with kinship labels on, two people who share a child but were never married were labelled "Spouse". Only people who actually have a spouse relationship are labelled now; an unmarried co-parent connector carries no label.
- **Children added from a parent's Edit person modal link back to the parent** (#697): creating or linking a child through a person's Edit person modal listed the child on the parent but left the child without a parent link. The reverse link is now written on each child when the parent is saved, so you no longer need the Data Quality bidirectional fix afterward.
- **Family Chart highlight dimming no longer clips connector lines on Circle cards** (#670): with Highlight Groups active and the Circle card style, the dim layer on non-matching cards was sized to a bounding box that overhung the round card and faintly dimmed nearby connector lines. The dim layer now matches the card's circle.

Thanks to @DigitalDreamn, @doctorwodka, and @inerlogic for the reports.

**1361 tests passing across 117 suites.**
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.65

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Family Chart, migration statistics, and GEDCOM import.

### Fixed
- **Family Chart focus indicator is visible again** (#689): the outline marking the focused (root) person no longer borrows the card's text colour (which last release darkened) so it stays clearly visible. It uses your theme accent color in the chart and in exports.
- **Family Chart highlight groups work in a pop-out window**: with the chart in its own window, Highlight Groups had no effect (cards neither dimmed nor highlighted). The highlight step was looking for cards in the main window; it now applies within the chart's own window.

### Changed
- **Migration routes now count each move, not just the net journey** (#684): "Top migration routes" (Statistics Dashboard and Places) counts each move a person made instead of only their first-to-last journey. A family that relocates together is counted on the shared leg even when members were born in different places, a round trip contributes both legs, and intermediate stops appear. The migration rate and moved totals are unchanged.
- **GEDCOM import consolidates variant spellings of the same place** (#687): one place that appeared under several `PLAC` forms (e.g. "Montréal, Québec, Canada" / "Montreal, QC, Canada") now folds into a single note instead of one per spelling. Canadian province abbreviations expand like US states already did, and accent/case/spacing variants consolidate with the discarded forms kept as historical names. It won't merge two places that only share a name (Quebec the province and Quebec the city stay separate).
- **Historical place names are stored as flat properties** (#687): `historical_names` is now a flat list (with an optional `historical_name_periods` list) instead of a nested array of objects, which Obsidian flagged. Existing notes still load, and **Flatten nested properties** can convert older notes in bulk.

Thanks to @tenephor, @DigitalDreamn, and @inerlogic for the reports.

**1326 tests passing across 113 suites.**
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.64

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Follow-up release: import fidelity for same-name relatives, a few Family Chart and Edit Person fixes, and tidier crop storage.

### Added
- **Burial date field in the Create/Edit Person modal** (#682): the person modal now has a "Burial date" field alongside birth and death, writing the `burial_date` property (previously only set by import or by hand). Clearing it removes the property.

### Fixed
- **GEDCOM and GEDCOM-X imports keep generational name suffixes** (#685): relatives who share a given name and surname and differ only by a suffix (Sr. / Jr. / III) no longer collide to one base name and get numbered. The suffix is folded into the name, so each person stays distinct (`John Smith Jr.`, `John Smith III`).
- **Unlinking a birth or death place in the Edit Person modal now removes it on save** (#680): clearing a person's birth or death place without choosing a replacement no longer leaves the old wikilink (and its `_id`) behind.
- **Migration analysis follow-ups** (#643): the Places-tab statistics card now uses the same event-driven analysis as the Statistics Dashboard; a sub-place destination counts toward the place that contains it; and the dashboard refreshes on its own after you add or edit a movement event.
- **Family Chart highlight groups no longer paint dimmed cards under the connector lines** (#670): dimming now uses an overlay instead of lowering the card's opacity, avoiding the stacking-context quirk that pushed dimmed cards below the lines on Linux and macOS.

### Changed
- **Image crop regions are stored as flat properties** (#683): crops now use flat parallel arrays (`media_crop_image`, `media_crop_x`, ...) instead of a nested YAML array, clearing the Data Quality nested-property warning. Existing notes still render, setting a crop migrates that note, and **Flatten nested properties** converts the rest in bulk.

Thanks to @tenephor, @inerlogic, and @DigitalDreamn for the reports.

**1289 tests passing across 111 suites.**

releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.61

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Family Chart card-rendering pass, plus follow-ups to the v0.22.59 data-integrity and export work.

## Family Chart

- **Long names wrap on Rectangle, Compact, and Mini cards** ([#671](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/671)) - Names wider than the card were cut off at the right edge. A name that overflows now wraps at a word boundary onto a second line, and cards grow in height to fit, so multi-word names stay readable. (A single word longer than the whole card can still be trimmed; the wider Compact style or "Split given/surname" both help there.)
- **Circle cards widen to fit long names and detail fields** ([#669](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/669)) - Circle used a fixed width, so longer names and the optional detail lines (nickname, title, occupation) ran past the card edge. Circle cards now measure their longest line and widen to fit it, and spacing adjusts so wider cards don't overlap.
- **Rectangle cards are more compact with clearer spacing** ([#669](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/669)) - The default card used a large avatar and a wide body, so a spouse couple read as one block. Rectangle cards now use a smaller avatar and tighter dimensions, with a wider minimum gap so spouse and sibling cards stay distinct.
- **Circle avatar clipping no longer relies on a partially-supported CSS feature** ([#669](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/669)) - The round-avatar clip moved from a CSS clip-path property to an equivalent SVG clip path. No visible change, and it scales more reliably across avatar sizes.
- **Highlight Groups keep dimmed cards above the connector lines** ([#670](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/670)) - With Highlight Groups active, dimmed cards could be painted beneath the connector lines on some platforms. The card layer is now isolated so every card, dimmed or highlighted, stays above the lines.
- **Pastel theme uses readable card text in dark mode** ([#672](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/672)) - Under a dark theme the Pastel preset drew white labels on its light pastel cards. Because the pastel colors are light in both modes, Pastel now uses dark card text in dark mode too.

## Data Quality

- **"Repair misaligned children" fixes both parents of a shared child in one pass** ([#666](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/666)) - When two parents shared the same children, the tool could repair one and leave the other, either because its two lists happened to stay the same length or because it was simply missing a child the first parent recovered. A broken child link can also mask a real child. The detector now treats a broken link as a repair trigger and reconciles the co-parent of every recovered child in the same run.

## Timeline export

- **Person-filtered exports no longer overwrite each other** ([#657](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/657)) - A timeline export took its file name from the title alone ("Event Timeline"), so exporting a second person collided with the first. The export now folds the filtered person into the title and file name (for example, "Event Timeline - Ahsoka Tano").

---

**1176 tests passing across 98 suites.**

Thanks to @DigitalDreamn and @tenephor for the reports and test cases.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.60

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Focused follow-up to v0.22.58: one relationship data-integrity fix and two Family Chart rendering fixes.

## Relationships

- **Misaligned children lists no longer mislabel or drop relationships** ([#666](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/666)) - A parent stores its children as two parallel lists, the names and their hidden IDs, which must line up one-for-one. If an ID went missing, the lists fell out of step and the next edit could silently re-pair a child's name with the wrong note or drop a reference. This is now prevented on both the edit and rename paths, and a new "Repair misaligned children" tool in the Data Quality tab rebuilds affected notes from each child's own parent links, recovering dropped children and cleaning up the misleading links, with a preview before anything is written.

## Family Chart

- **"Circle" card style shows connection bubbles and the photo placeholder again** ([#669](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/669)) - Circle was drawn with a different renderer that never produced the connection-indicator bubbles or the no-photo placeholder. It now renders a round avatar, ringed in the gender color with the name centered below, on the same renderer the other styles use, so bubbles, placeholders, and branch expand/collapse all behave consistently across themes.
- **Warning callouts before batch repairs are readable in every theme** - The "back up your vault" notice could render warning-colored text on a warning-colored background and disappear in some themes; it now uses the standard text color.

---

**1162 tests passing across 96 suites.**

Thanks to @DigitalDreamn for the reports and test cases.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.59

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
A reporter-driven follow-up to v0.22.57. Several fixes from that release worked on the surface they were tested against but missed a parallel render or save path; this group closes those gaps, alongside a Family Chart theme fix and a new opt-in timeline toggle.

## New

- **"Show sibling's marriages" timeline toggle** ([#661](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/661)) - A new opt-in toggle adds a sibling's marriage to the focal person's Dynamic Timeline, so you can see their age at a sibling's wedding. It joins the existing toggles for parents' and children's marriages, covers biological and adopted siblings, and is off by default.

## Fictional dates

- **A universe's default calendar drives how its dates are read** ([#650](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/650)) - Fictional dates in a note assigned to a universe now resolve through that universe's chosen default calendar, and a custom calendar is preferred over a built-in that shares its era abbreviations (e.g. `BBY`/`ABY`). The "Systems in use" breakdown credits the right calendar.

## Events

- **The Create/Edit Event modal saves the Organizations field** ([#659](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/659)) - Entering organizations and saving now persists them on both create and edit, and editing an event pre-fills the field. The organization "Events" section shows each event's type by its proper name and icon.
- **Person-filtered Canvas and Excalidraw timeline exports return the right events** ([#657](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/657)) - Exporting a person-filtered timeline to Canvas or Excalidraw no longer reports "No events to export after filtering" when the preview shows events; all formats now filter consistently.
- **Event tables and timelines show participant and place aliases** ([#658](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/658)) - The dockable Events view, the Control Center timeline tables, the person-filter dropdowns, and the person-profile timeline place line now show aliases instead of raw `Filename|Alias` text, while still linking to the correct note.

## Profiles and views

- **Event profile header shows the event type's name** ([#665](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/665)) - The Entity Profile heading lists an event's type by its catalog name (built-in or custom) instead of the raw internal slug.
- **Data Quality results refresh after an edit** ([#664](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/664)) - After filling in missing data, the Data Quality view and its Refresh button no longer keep reporting the item as missing; it refreshes once the note's metadata has actually been re-read.

## Family Chart

- **Connector lines stay visible in the High Contrast theme** ([#668](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/668)) - The lines between cards no longer vanish in High Contrast; line color is now derived to stay visible against the chart background, while the other themes keep the line color they had.

---

**1145 tests passing across 95 suites.**

Thanks to @DigitalDreamn and @doctorwodka for the reports and test cases.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.58

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
A reporter-driven cohort closing 11 issues from community threads spanning fictional-date handling, the Events tab statistics, the Entity Profile views, and organization linking. Three new capabilities land alongside a batch of fixes for fictional-date counting and parsing, the timeline export, the cross-tab "Manage media" action, & several Control Center papercuts.

## New

- **Link organizations to events** ([#659](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/659)) - Event notes can now reference organizations through an `organizations` wikilink array, and those events appear in the "Events" section of each linked organization's profile. The Create/Edit Event modal has a new "Organizations" field for setting the links.
- **"Referenced events" on the Source profile** ([#654](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/654)) - A source note's profile view now lists the event notes that cite it, alongside "Referenced facts" and "Media." Each entry links through to the event.
- **"Dissolved" field on the Organization modal** ([#649](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/649)) - The `dissolved` property was already supported by templates and the profile view; the Create/Edit Organization modal now has a field for it, right under "Founded."

## Fictional dates

- **Negative years and decade notation parse and sort correctly** ([#655](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/655), [#660](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/660)) - Negative fictional years (`EP -18`) were rejected and sorted as broken text; they now parse to a negative canonical year and sort earliest-first. Decade notation (`EP 30s`) is supported, and month/day suffixes no longer corrupt a negative year. This corrects era display, ages, and statistics for these dates too.
- **Statistics and Timeline Export count fictional dates correctly** ([#648](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/648)) - The Events tab counters skipped approximation markers, month/day forms, and death-only fictional dates, and an all-fictional vault could read "0 dated." Both now route through the shared date parser.
- **"Systems in use" attributes dates to the correct custom calendar** ([#650](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/650)) - A custom calendar that reused a built-in's era abbreviations (e.g. `BBY`/`ABY`) is now credited correctly instead of being shadowed by the built-in.

## Entity profiles

- **Event profile shows the participant's alias** ([#658](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/658)) - Aliased participant links now display the alias instead of the raw wikilink text, while still navigating to the correct note.

## Events tab and Control Center

- **Timeline export "Filter by person" matches the selected person** ([#657](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/657)) - The filter no longer collapses to "0 events"; counts and date range update as expected.
- **"Manage media" works across all entity tables** ([#656](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/656)) - The action now opens the media manager from the People, Places, Organizations, and Events tabs and the Media Gallery, instead of silently doing nothing.
- **Statistics recompute once the metadata cache settles** ([#651](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/651)) - The Statistics card no longer shows a stale low count right after launch or a batch of edits; it recomputes when the index settles.
- **"View full statistics" surfaces the dashboard in front of the modal** ([#645](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/645)) - The link now closes the Control Center first, so the dashboard isn't opened behind it.

---

Stability window holding on the v0.22.46 anchor; this group is reporter-driven UX fixes and additive features with no data-loss-class changes. **1106 tests passing across 88 suites.**

Thanks to @doctorwodka and @DigitalDreamn for the reports and test cases.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.57

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Small reactive patch closing one reporter-driven Events tab counting bug and stripping a dynamic-script-loading code path out of jspdf's bundled output that the v0.22.55 Community Plugins automated review flagged in error.

- **Events tab Statistics card: fictional-date count now includes event notes** ([#644](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/644)): The "Fictional dates" subsection's "X notes use fictional date systems" line only counted person notes with fictional `born`/`died`, never event notes' `date` field, despite the label saying "notes" generically. So a vault with 19 fictional-dated events and 9 fictional-dated persons read as "9", and a vault with 3 fictional-dated events and 0 fictional-dated persons read as "0". The count now reflects both kinds of notes (each fictional-dated event contributes one-for-one), and "Systems in use" picks up event-detected systems automatically. Reported by [@doctorwodka](https://github.com/doctorwodka).
- **Strip jspdf's `pdfobjectnewwindow` output mode from the bundle**: jspdf's `output('pdfobjectnewwindow')` dynamically loads `pdfobject.min.js` from a CDN by creating a `<script>` element. It's the only `createElement('script')` call in jspdf's source and the only code path of its kind in Charted Roots' bundled `main.js`. The plugin only ever calls `save()` (Family Chart export, Tree wizard PDF output), so the case body is unreachable, but the v0.22.55 Community Plugins automated review flagged it anyway. A new `patch-jspdf.js` postinstall script replaces the case body with a `throw`, preserving the switch shape so jspdf still parses cleanly while dropping the dynamic script element creation from the bundle entirely.

**1064 tests passing across 82 suites.**
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.56

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Security-only patch addressing all 18 dependency advisories surfaced by the v0.22.54 Community Plugins automated review.

- **`jspdf` 3.0.4 -> 4.2.1** - clears 10 advisories including Path Traversal in the Node `fs` build, HTML Injection in `output` methods, PDF Injection in the `AcroForm` module, DoS via malformed BMP / GIF dimensions, stored XMP metadata injection, race conditions in `addJS`, and PDF Object Injection via free-text color.
- **`dompurify` 3.3.0 -> 3.4.7** (transitive via jspdf) - clears 8 advisories including XSS, prototype pollution, `FORBID_TAGS` bypasses via `ADD_TAGS` function predicates, and `SAFE_FOR_TEMPLATES` bypasses in `RETURN_DOM` mode.

Upstream confirmed no breaking API changes through the 3.x and 4.x majors; jsPDF 4.0's only behavioral change is `fs` access restriction in the Node build, which doesn't apply to Obsidian's renderer context.

**No application-code changes.** PDF export verified end-to-end locally for both (Family Chart export and Tree wizard PDF output).

**1064 tests passing across 82 suites.**

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Reactive patch closing a cluster of issue reports, and the project's first external code contribution. **1060 tests passing across 82 suites.**

## Changed

- **Adoption events on family timelines now use the adoption icon** ([#632](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/632)): v0.22.52 ([#627](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/627)) gave the focal person's own `Adopted` row the `heart-handshake` icon in warm orange, but the adoption events on the family timeline surfaces (the adoptive parent's `Adopted {name}` row, and the adopted-sibling and adopted-grandchild `Adoption of {name}` rows) still showed the generic `users` icon. They now resolve through the built-in `adoption` event type, so the same icon and color appear across every Dynamic Timeline surface. The adopted child's birth row keeps the generic icon, since it is a birth rather than an adoption. Raised by [@DigitalDreamn](https://github.com/DigitalDreamn).

## Fixed

- **Family Chart: custom-relationship overlay self-heals if it draws before cards settle** ([#615](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/615)): On some setups the overlay line's top endpoint could detach from its source card after a refresh, floating partway down toward the next card. The overlay waits for card positions to stabilize before drawing, but if the chart's entrance animation briefly stalls mid-flight, that wait can fire against an intermediate position. Fix adds a backstop: shortly after drawing, it checks whether any card moved since, and if so re-runs the stability wait and redraws once the cards settle, so a premature draw repairs itself. Reported by [@doctorwodka](https://github.com/doctorwodka).

- **"Not a duplicate" dismissals now persist across sessions** ([#633](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/633)): In the duplicate-detection modal, marking a pair "Not a duplicate" only removed it from the current scan, so the same pair reappeared on the next scan. Dismissed pairs are now saved and filtered out of future scans, and a new "Clear dismissed" button (shown only when dismissals exist) lets you undo a stale dismissal. Reported by [@Darcylynn](https://github.com/Darcylynn).

- **Calendar view filters events by year as well as month** ([#634](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/634)): Navigating to a specific month and year (for example February 1850) showed events from every year that shared that month, so a person born in February 2010 appeared on February 15, 1850. The calendar now matches both month and year. This was the project's first external contribution, by [@MohammadYusif](https://github.com/MohammadYusif) in [#636](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/pull/636), reported by [@Darcylynn](https://github.com/Darcylynn).

- **Merge Duplicate Places: Help link points at the current wiki**: The Help link in the modal header pointed at an outdated URL and returned a 404. It now links to the current wiki. Reported by [@Darcylynn](https://github.com/Darcylynn).
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.53

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Small reactive patch closing two reported UI papercuts. **1048 tests passing across 79 suites.**

## Fixed

- **Dynamic Timeline Block: the focal person's own `Adopted` event now renders with an icon** ([#627](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/627)): The adoptive parent's `Adopted {name}` row already showed the generic family-event `users` icon, but the focal person's own `Adopted` row showed no icon at all. The renderer's icon resolution calls `getEventType('adoption', ...)` for the focal-own row, and the built-in catalog had no entry with that id, so the lookup returned undefined and the renderer fell through to a placeholder span. The fix adds `adoption` as a built-in vital event type alongside birth / death / marriage / divorce (icon `heart-handshake`, warm orange). Side benefit: every other surface that consults the catalog (event timelines, map controller, Create/Edit Event modal, GEDCOM mapping) now picks up the new entry automatically. Reported by [@doctorwodka](https://github.com/doctorwodka), confirmed by [@DigitalDreamn](https://github.com/DigitalDreamn).

- **Edit Person modal now recognizes single-letter sex markers and writes the canonical GEDCOM-aligned form** ([#629](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/629)): Profile View writes single-letter sex markers (`M` / `F` / `X` / `U`); Edit Person previously wrote word forms (`male` / `female` / `nonbinary` / empty), so a person saved via Profile View as `sex: F` opened in Edit Person with the dropdown grayed as "unrecognized". The fix normalizes the loaded sex value through the existing value-alias service, so all known shapes (word forms, GEDCOM markers, aliases like `nb` / `enby` / `intersex`) converge on the canonical M/F/X/U set before populating the dropdown. The dropdown's options also flip to marker values with display labels (Male / Female / Non-binary / Unknown), gaining explicit `X` and `U` options the previous version lacked. Existing word-form notes auto-convert on next save via Edit Person. Reported by [@doctorwodka](https://github.com/doctorwodka).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Reactive patch closing three reporter-driven fictional-date and timeline-ordering papercuts on the heels of v0.22.50, plus one audit Phase 5 cleanup. **1023 tests passing across 77 suites.**

## Changed

- **Internal: Control Center mobile-mode check drops the body-class fallback** (audit Phase 5): `isMobileMode()` now returns `Platform.isMobile` directly; v0.22.49's `MobileClassManager` made the body-class belt-and-suspenders redundant.

## Fixed

- **Fictional-era dates with an inline approximation marker now parse correctly** ([#624](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/624) follow-up): The v0.22.50 fix missed the actual data shape: `born: DE ~310` (inline tilde between era and year). The approximation stripper now also handles inline markers, so `DE ~310`, `DE c. 1264`, and `DE circa 310` parse cleanly with the era preserved. Reported by [@doctorwodka](https://github.com/doctorwodka).

- **Per-person Dynamic Timeline Block now respects `before`/`after` event ordering** ([#625](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/625)): The v0.22.45 [#569](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/569) `sort_order` values were ignored when breaking same-year ties, so a same-year pair with `after: [[Event B]]` rendered in insertion order rather than B-then-A. The comparator now consults sort_order between the year compare and the rawDate tiebreak. Latent since v0.22.39. Reported by @doctorwodka, confirmed by @DigitalDreamn.

- **Fictional-era dates with an ISO-style month/day suffix now preserve the era prefix on display** ([#626](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/626)): `adoption_date: DE 1264-08-15` rendered as a plain `1264` on the Dynamic Timeline Block. The fictional parser already stripped trailing `T HH:MM:SS` time suffixes; now also handles `-MM-DD`. Age math was unaffected. Spotted during #624 follow-up verification.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.51

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Three reporter-driven items plus the first three audit Phase 4b stylesheet migrations. Family Chart fixes for wikilink text-field rendering and bare-year age calculation, adopted sibling/grandchild adoption events now always-on, and mobile-class consolidation across three view stylesheets. **986 tests passing across 76 suites.**

## Changed

- **Dynamic Timeline Block: adopted-sibling and adopted-grandchild adoption events are now always-on** ([#623](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/623)): The v0.22.49 events were gated on the `Show adopted children's births` toggle. They now match the adoptive parent's always-on `Adopted {name}` event ([#396](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/396)). Adoption is a family event the focal canonically experienced, independent of the user's broader adopted-births preference. Reported by [@DigitalDreamn](https://github.com/DigitalDreamn) as a #621 follow-up.

- **Internal: Phase 4b mobile-migration first batch** (`map-view.css`, `family-chart-view.css`, `profile-view.css`): Three view stylesheets migrate from media-query-only to dual-path mobile coverage, consuming v0.22.49's `MobileClassManager` classes for phones where Obsidian's media queries don't fire reliably. Existing media queries stay for narrow desktop / tablet leaves. The view-local `cr-map-view-phone` and `cr-profile--mobile` classes retire onto the global ones. User-visible side effect: phones in landscape now get the compact mobile layouts on the Map view's world-map-preview and the Family Chart's info panel. Verified on Android via `chrome://inspect`.

## Fixed

- **Bare 1-3 digit year strings now parse as years across the date pipeline** ([#624](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/624)): The date parser required 4-digit values, so a frontmatter `birth_date: 310` (3-digit canonical year in a custom fictional-era calendar) was unparseable. When paired with an era-prefixed event date like `adoption_date: DE 1264`, the renderer's safety-net dropped the age annotation rather than risk a wrong number across mismatched eras. Fix accepts any whole-string digit-only input as a year; substring matching is preserved for `"March 12, 1942"`, and the whole-string anchor keeps `"5 Jan"` and `"1900s"` from matching accidentally. Reported by [@doctorwodka](https://github.com/doctorwodka).

- **Family Chart: wikilink-shaped text fields now render the alias instead of the raw `[[…]]` markup** ([#622](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/622)): The card display for `religion`, `alt name`, `nickname`, `title`, `occupation`, and `caste` rendered raw wikilink markup when those fields were set to a wikilink. A guarded helper now strips brackets and collapses pipe-form to the alias only when the input is fully bracket-wrapped, so plain text containing `/` or `|` (e.g., `occupation: "Cook/Server"`) passes through unchanged. The info panel's birth/death place display now uses the same helper. Reported by [@doctorwodka](https://github.com/doctorwodka).
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.50

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Continuation of the reporter cycle plus a tranche of audit-plan progress. Adoption events on sibling/grandparent timelines, era-aware twin tiebreak fix, settings reorganization, a long-deferred sanitizer relaxation, and some mobile groundwork. **931 tests passing across 73 suites.**

## Added

- **Adoption events on the sibling and grandparent Dynamic Timeline Blocks** ([#621](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/621)): The adoption event itself (previously emitted only on the adoptive parent's timeline as `Adopted {name}`) now also renders on the focal person's sibling timeline (`Adoption of {name}`) and grandparent timeline (`Adoption of {name}`), extending the v0.22.48 #618 birth-event coverage in parallel. Gated on the same `Show adopted children's births` toggle. Both new labels are customizable under `Settings -> Timeline -> Timeline labels`. Distinct from the adoptive parent's `Adopted {name}` label so the reader can tell which surface the event is on at a glance. Reported by [@DigitalDreamn](https://github.com/DigitalDreamn).

## Changed

- **Timeline settings now live in their own top-level section** (was under Advanced): The three Timeline subsections (Timeline layout, Timeline labels, and Family events on timelines) move from `Settings -> Advanced` to a new `Settings -> Timeline` top-level section, placed right after `Dates & validation` so the temporal-display settings cluster together. No setting names or values change, only the location.

- **Filenames preserve parentheses and curly braces** ([#506](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/506)): The shared name-sanitization helper used by all importers and entity-creation flows previously stripped `( )`, `[ ]`, and `{ }` from filenames on the assumption all six were wikilink-breaking. Empirical evidence proved that only square brackets actually break wikilinks (they are the delimiters themselves). The character class now preserves `( )` and `{ }` while still stripping `[ ]` and the filesystem-illegal set. User-facing effect: creating a universe named `Star Wars (AU)` now produces a file with parens preserved in the basename. Closes [#506](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/506).

- **Internal: Phase 4a mobile groundwork**: New `MobileClassManager` infrastructure attaches `cr-mobile` / `cr-desktop` / `cr-phone` / `cr-tablet` classes to each registered view's container based on `Platform.is*` flags. The audit plan's Phase 4b (per-component CSS migration from `@media (max-width: 768px)` to class-scoped selectors) consumes these classes; this release lays the infrastructure. All 15 registered views call into the manager via a new `registerCRView` factory wrapper. `Mobile layout` section in `docs/developer/coding-standards.md` documents the pattern.

- **Internal: extract `shouldUseSubmenu()` helper** for the `Platform.isDesktop && !Platform.isMobile` dual check that was duplicated across three sites. No behavior change.

## Fixed

- **Dynamic Timeline Block: same-year twins now order correctly in descending eras** ([#609](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/609) follow-on): The v0.22.48 #609 fix added a raw-date lex-compare tiebreak that put the firstborn at the top of a twin pair, correct for ISO dates (chronological = oldest at top) but era-blind for descending eras (BBY, BCE, etc.) where the year sort already produces "newer at top". The comparator now detects descending-era rawDates via the `DateService` and inverts the tiebreak direction so same-year twins follow the same direction as their year neighbours. Reported by [@DigitalDreamn](https://github.com/DigitalDreamn) as a follow-up to the v0.22.48 fix.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Reporter-driven release built around v0.22.47 verification feedback. New Dynamic Timeline coverage toggles, timeline correctness fixes, and a Family Chart kinship-label fix. **917 tests passing across 72 suites.**

## Added

- **Children's marriages on the parent's Dynamic Timeline Block** ([#607](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/607)): New `Show children's marriages` toggle under `Settings -> Advanced -> Family events on timelines` (default off). Covers biological, adopted, and step children. Renders the spouse's name and (when set) the marriage location. Customizable label template with `{name}` and `{spouse}` placeholders.

- **Parent's marriages on the child's Dynamic Timeline Block** ([#608](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/608)): Symmetric companion to #607. New `Show parent's marriages` toggle (default off) renders stepparent acquisitions (a bio parent's remarriage) and adoptive-couple marriages. Skips the bio-pairing marriage since that's implicit in the existing parent links.

## Fixed

- **Dynamic Timeline Block: same-year twins now sort by birth-time suffix** ([#609](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/609)): The timeline sorted entries by year alone, leaving same-year twins in arbitrary insertion order. The sort now applies the same year-then-raw-date tiebreak as the v0.22.46 sibling-sort consolidation, so timestamped twins (`BBY 29 T20:03:04` vs `BBY 29 T20:08:15`) order firstborn before secondborn deterministically.

- **"Show adopted children's births" toggle now governs the sibling + grandparent surfaces** ([#618](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/618)): The toggle was honored on the adoptive parent's timeline but adopted siblings and adopted grandchildren leaked through as plain `Birth of {name}` entries elsewhere. Both walks now respect the toggle; when on, adopted relations get a distinct label (`Birth of adopted sibling {name}` / `Birth of adopted grandchild {name}`) so the relationship is visible at a glance.

- **Custom relationship `Maps to: Father` / `Maps to: Mother` now writes the gendered parent scalar** ([#616](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/616)): The Father / Mother dropdown options fell through to a flat custom-property write rather than the canonical `father:` / `mother:` scalars, so Canvas Family Tree, Family Chart, the Relationship Calculator, and the v0.22.47 conflict guard never saw the relationship. Now routes through `addParentRelationship`, picking up the conflict-guard prompt and sex-mismatch warning for free.

- **Family Chart "Show kinship labels": Parent label aligns with each child's card + renders on stable positions** ([#619](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/619)): Two-part fix. Labels previously landed at the link path's arc-length midpoint (which for displaced children falls on the horizontal trunk, not the visible "line down"); they now anchor a fixed offset above each child's card edge. Initial-render scheduling now polls for card-position stability instead of using a fixed `setTimeout(1500)`, eliminates the mid-animation snapshot that left the bottom label overlapping its card until manual refresh.

A focused fix release driven by reporter feedback on the v0.22.46 [Discussion #602](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/discussions/602) thread, plus a few quick-win polish items. **906 tests passing across 71 suites.**

## Fixed

- **Per-field conflict guards on parent scalar writes** ([#606](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/606)): Adding a custom relationship type with `Maps to: Parent` no longer silently overwrites an existing `father` / `mother`. A confirmation modal now surfaces with `Cancel` and `Replace <name>` buttons; on confirmed replace, the previous parent's `children` array is also cleaned up. Reported by [@DigitalDreamn](https://github.com/DigitalDreamn).

They now render as a small text node directly below the left spouse card. Multi-marriage stacks order by spouse index. Reported by [@DigitalDreamn](https://github.com/DigitalDreamn).

- **Fictional-era dates accept the ISO 8601 time suffix** ([#590](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/590) follow-up): `born: BBY 29 T20:03:04` (and the no-space variant) now parses correctly, with the era prefix preserved on display. The time component is used as the sibling-sort tiebreak per the v0.22.46 design; display surfaces strip the time for cleaner reading. The Edit Person modal's Birth date hint now mentions twin-disambiguation support. Reported by [@DigitalDreamn](https://github.com/DigitalDreamn).

- **Family Chart custom-relationship overlay polish** ([#591](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/591) follow-up): Three smaller issues on a pure vertical adoptive chain. Bow direction is now stable across renders (no flipping between initial draw and refresh); bezier endpoints are clipped to card boundaries instead of sitting inside the cards; sag scales with chord verticality so vertical chords through stacked cards have enough clearance to bow around them. The post-refresh endpoint-drift symptom from the same report was not reproducible locally and is deferred. Reported by [@doctorwodka](https://github.com/doctorwodka).

- **Custom relationship type editor: Maps-to dropdown now reveals when "Include on family trees" toggles on**. The toggle handler couldn't override the initial `.crc-hidden` class via inline `style.display`; it now toggles the class directly.

## Changed

- **Internal: drop vestigial `break-inside: avoid` from timeline-callout list items.** Vestigial after the v0.22.46 multicolumn -> Grid migration. Closes the last CSS-lint warning the scanner was flagging.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.47

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Charted Roots v0.22.46

A bug-fix-heavy release driven by post-v0.22.45 reporter feedback, with two architectural themes folded in.

## Highlights

**Bug fixes from #602 and direct reporter follow-ups**

- **Family Chart custom-relationship overlay** rendering reliably (#591): the curve no longer clips through cards on near-vertical chords, and the post-refresh shrink to a stub is fixed.
- **Gramps import shared-event handling** respects role (#601): events you participated in as Informant/Witness no longer attribute their date/place to your own person note.
- **Family Chart Person details pane** actually closes on the X button (#604), and stops auto-opening on chart load.
- **Family Chart sibling sort** now matches the other four rendering surfaces — fictional-era-aware via `DateService`, with the same twin/triplet ISO 8601 time tiebreak as the rest of the cluster (#605).

**Timeline coverage expansion (#582-#585, from @DigitalDreamn)**

Four new opt-in toggles on the dynamic Timeline block: children's deaths on parent's timeline, stepparent's deaths on stepchild's timeline, sibling deaths on the person's timeline, and grandchildren's births on the grandparent's timeline. Each filtered to events that occurred while the focal person was alive.

**Events timeline filters (#515)**

Universe, place, and date-range filters on the Events tab Timeline card and the dockable Events sidebar, gated behind a "More filters" disclosure. The dockable sidebar persists all six filter values across Obsidian restarts.

**Sibling sort: ISO 8601 time tiebreak for twins (#590)**

When siblings share a birth date, the sort now uses an ISO 8601 time component (`1985-04-12T03:42`) as a deterministic tiebreaker. Side effect: siblings within the same calendar year also now sort by month and day. Applied across all five sibling-sort sites in the codebase.

**Event sort: point events before range events on tied start dates (#569 follow-up)**

When two events share a start date and one has `date_end` (range) while the other doesn't (point), the point event now sorts first. Closes the display-order gap on the v0.22.45 auto-compute work.

**Scanner CSS cleanup**

Timeline-callout multi-column layout migrated from CSS multicolumn to CSS Grid, clearing the last partially-supported CSS warning the Community automated review was flagging on v0.22.45.

## Reporters and contributors

Thanks to [@doctorwodka](https://github.com/doctorwodka), [@DigitalDreamn](https://github.com/DigitalDreamn), and Tiberius (on the Obsidian Forum) for the careful reports and verification cycles that drove this release.

## Full changelog

See [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md#0.22.46---2026-05-17) for the complete entry.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.46

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
**Multi-theme release: bundle hygiene + sibling-sort consolidation arc + community-reported bug fixes.**

## What's new

**Event picker now has a person filter ([#581](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/581))** - The Relative ordering event picker (Create / Edit Event modal, the "Add" button on the After or Before fields) gets a new "Person:" dropdown alongside the existing Type filter. Source values are deduplicated from each event's participant fields. Reduces scrolling when multiple people have similarly-titled events. Suggested by [@DigitalDreamn](https://github.com/DigitalDreamn).

**Event ordering takes effect immediately on save ([#569](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/569) follow-up)** - Saving an event with `before` / `after` constraints now recomputes `sort_order` automatically in the background, so the Events tab and Profile View Events section reflect the narrative order right away. Previously the constraints had to be materialized by running the "Compute sort order" command manually first.

## Fixes

**Add Custom Relationship modal honors the gender-neutral parent setting ([#579](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/579), [#580](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/580))** - Adding a parent through the custom-relationship modal now writes to the `parents` array when the "Enable gender-neutral parent property" setting is on (#579), and also when the target's sex isn't `male` / `female` (#580). The previous fallback to `father` for non-binary parents was wrong; non-binary parents now route to the gender-neutral array regardless of the setting. Reported by [@doctorwodka](https://github.com/doctorwodka).

**Children sort by birth date across Profile View, Canvas Family Tree, and report surfaces ([#586](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/586), [#587](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/587))** - Three rendering surfaces had been emitting children in frontmatter array order rather than birth order: the Profile View Children section (#586), Canvas Family Tree (#587), and seven additional report / visual-tree / family-timeline surfaces (Family Group Sheet, Individual Summary, Register, Descendant Chart, Source Summary, Visual Tree, Family Timeline view). All now sort by birth date with universe-aware canonical-year comparison, so descending fictional eras (BBY / GR / EF / DE etc.) order correctly alongside Gregorian dates. Bio + adopted + step children merge into a single age-sorted list. Reported by [@DigitalDreamn](https://github.com/DigitalDreamn).

## Under the hood

**`main.js` size reduced by 50% (14.7 MB → 8.27 MB)** - Production minify enabled in the esbuild config. No functional changes; identifier mangling is safe for this codebase. The "main.js exceeds Sync Standard 5 MB threshold" caveat that's been in the 1.0 release notes softens significantly (~3.4 MB remaining, vs ~9.5 MB previously). Fully closing the threshold stays post-1.0 scope.

**Dynamic Code Execution scanner Recommendation closed** - A new `patch-pdfmake.js` postinstall strips two `new Function("return this")()` sites from pdfmake's bundled core-js and webpack runtime. Both branches are unreachable in Obsidian's Electron runtime. No functional change.

**Architectural follow-up: sibling-sort consolidation ([#588](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/588))** — The fixes for #532 / #586 / #587 + this release's sweep are now consolidated into a single `sortByBirthDate` option on `RelationshipQueryService.getChildren()`. Tracked at #588 for future-surface coverage + remaining wiring.

**883 tests passing across 68 suites.**
