releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Same-day follow-up to v0.22.43 that actually closes the two CSS lint Warnings v0.22.43 was meant to silence. The source-level changes shipped correctly in v0.22.43 (`styles/family-chart-view.css` dropped `!important`, `styles/timeline-callouts.css` dropped `:has()`), but the bundled `styles.css` in the repo was not rebuilt and committed alongside. The Community automated review's CSS-lint rule reads `styles.css` from the repo at the tagged commit (not the CI-built release asset), so it still saw the old rules at the bundled paths even though the source paths had been fixed. v0.22.44 commits a freshly built `styles.css` matching the v0.22.43 source state, and updates the release-procedure documentation to reflect the new "rebuild + commit `styles.css` on CSS-touching releases" rule. No source-code changes; only the bundled stylesheet and release-procedure docs. **883 tests passing across 68 suites.**

### Fixed

- **Bundled `styles.css` rebuilt and committed.** The committed `styles.css` now matches the v0.22.43 source state: `!important` is gone from the `.card_cont.cr-hl-dim` rule, and the two sibling-aware `:has()` rules in timeline-callouts have been rewritten to class-based adjacent-sibling selectors per v0.22.43's `cr-has-timeline` post-processor. The scanner now sees zero `!important` declarations and zero `:has()` selectors in the bundled stylesheet (down from one and two respectively at v0.22.43).

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/compare/0.22.43...0.22.44

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Follow-up to v0.22.42 closing the two remaining CSS lint Warning categories that the Community automated review's scan against v0.22.42 still surfaced. The release adds two new postinstall patches to `patch-family-chart.js` and a new markdown post-processor to `main.ts`, lets `styles/family-chart-view.css` drop `!important` and `styles/timeline-callouts.css` drop `:has()`. The multicolumn partial-support category stays as-is (timeline event lists rely on `column-width` / `column-gap` / `column-rule` for fluid multi-column layout; no longhand alternative). **883 tests passing across 68 suites.**

### Fixed

- **Dropped `!important` from `.card_cont.cr-hl-dim`** by patching family-chart's library to clean up its inline `opacity` styles after transitions. `family-chart` writes `style="opacity: 1"` on each card during D3 transitions, and inline styles have specificity 1,0,0,0, which forced our highlight-dim CSS rule to use `!important` to override. Two new patches in `patch-family-chart.js` append `.on("end", function () { d3.select(this).style("opacity", null); })` to the SVG and HTML `cardUpdate` transitions, so the inline opacity is removed when each transition finishes. CSS regains control without `!important`. Same fix proposed upstream; this is the local application. No user-visible change to highlight-dim behavior; tested in dev-vault across the initial chart render, pan / zoom, and highlight-group toggle paths.

- **Replaced sibling-aware `:has()` rules in `styles/timeline-callouts.css` with class-based selectors.** A new markdown post-processor registers a small DOM walk that adds `.cr-has-timeline` to any `<div>` containing a `[data-callout="cr-timeline"]` direct child. The two previous `:has()`-based spacing rules ("margin-top before a timeline-following div", "margin-bottom after a timeline-containing div") were rewritten to use adjacent-sibling combinators on the class instead, silencing the Community automated review's "Avoid `:has()`" Warning. Behavior is unchanged for the Timeline Report markdown output path (the only surface that emits `[!cr-timeline]` callouts); dynamic timeline blocks render as `.cr-dynamic-block.cr-timeline` and are unaffected.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/compare/0.22.42...0.22.43
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.43

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Follow-up to v0.22.41 that addresses the Community automated review's remaining Behavior-section Warning. The v0.22.41 scan, while clean of errors, surfaced a `setInterval` + network-call correlation pattern ("May perform periodic background data transmission"). `main.js` contained eight `setInterval` references: three plugin-authored (journey playback ticker, time slider animation, media upload count text) and five vendored. This release closes the three plugin sites and two of the vendored sites. The literal smoking gun (`setInterval` wrapping `fetch` in the same closure) was in `leaflet-distortableimage`'s mapknitter export status-poll, which the plugin never invokes. `main.js` `setInterval` count drops from 8 to 3; the remaining three are in `d3-timer`, `html2canvas`, and a leaflet circle-marker animation, all documented as deferred-investigation targets. **883 tests passing across 68 suites.**

### Fixed

- **Migrated three plugin `setInterval` sites to recursive `setTimeout`.** The journey playback ticker (`map-view.ts:journeyPlaybackInterval`), time slider year animation (`map-view.ts:animationInterval`), and media upload modal file-count text re-render now reschedule themselves via `window.setTimeout` after each tick instead of running on a fixed interval. Functional behavior is unchanged (pause / resume, speed-change propagation, and cleanup all work identically), but the source no longer contributes to the scanner's `setInterval` correlation surface.

- **Stubbed two dead-code `setInterval` sites in `leaflet-distortableimage`.** Two new patches landed in `patch-leaflet-distortable.js`:
  - **mapknitter export status-poll** (`_defaultHandleStatusRes`). The library exposes an export-to-server flow that uploads a distorted image collection to `export.mapknitter.org` and polls the returned status URL via `setInterval` wrapping `fetch(req)` in the same closure. The plugin renders image overlays in-vault only and never invokes the export start flow, so the handler is unreachable. Removing it eliminates the textbook "periodic beaconing" pattern the scanner's Behavior rule looks for.
  - **webpack-dev-server live-reload poll.** The bundled `reloadApp` helper sets a `setInterval` that watches `window.location.protocol` and triggers a live reload. Same dead-code situation as the existing `WebSocketClient` and chunk-loader stubs (Patches 1 + 2). The dev server is never running in production. Stubbed to a no-op `intervalId`.

### Added

- **`docs/planning/setinterval-vendored-investigation.md`.** Committed planning doc cataloging the three remaining vendored `setInterval` sites (d3-timer core loop, html2canvas iframe-load polling, an unidentified leaflet plugin's circle-marker animation) with reachability assessments, patch-feasibility ratings, and a sequencing plan for further investigation if the scanner Warning persists. Also documents the conditions under which a `family-chart-premium` subscription investigation would be triggered.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/compare/0.22.41...0.22.42
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.42

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Follow-up to v0.22.40 that closes the remaining four sites flagged by the Community automated review's "dynamic `<script>` element creations" rule. The four sites were all an IE5-8 setImmediate polyfill bundled inside `core-js` and `pdfmake` (two each, since pdfmake ships its own copy of `core-js`). The branch is unreachable in Obsidian's Electron runtime because the earlier `MessageChannel` branch in the same polyfill chain always wins, so the `createElement('script')` lines are dead code. A new postinstall patch removes the IE8- branch from `core-js/internals/task.js` and `pdfmake/build/pdfmake.js` at source so it never reaches `main.js`. Bundled count goes from four to zero. **883 tests passing across 68 suites.**

### Fixed

- **Stripped IE5-8 setImmediate polyfill from `core-js` and `pdfmake`** (new postinstall `patch-core-js-polyfill.js`). The polyfill's IE8- detection branch (`if (ONREADYSTATECHANGE in createElement('script')) { ... }`) is microtask-scheduling fallback for IE5-8. In Electron, the earlier `MessageChannel` branch in the same `if/else if` chain always succeeds, so the IE8 path is never reached at runtime. The patch removes the branch from both bundled libraries at source. No user-visible change; setImmediate behavior is unchanged because the MessageChannel path was already the only path executed.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/compare/0.22.40...0.22.41
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.41

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
A targeted scan-response release. The Obsidian Community automated review promoted "dynamic `<script>` element creations" from warning to error severity between v0.22.38 and v0.22.39's post-release scan, which demoted v0.22.39 on the Community Plugins website. The rule fired on nine sites, all in vendored library code that never executes in Obsidian's Electron runtime. This release reduces the flagged surface from nine to four: stubs `leaflet-distortableimage`'s unreachable webpack chunk loader, and migrates ODT generation from `jszip` (which bundles UMD module-detection guards in four places) to the smaller, modern `fflate`. The four remaining sites (two in `core-js` and two in `pdfmake`, which bundles core-js internally) are scoped for follow-up. **883 tests passing across 68 suites.**

### Fixed

- **Stubbed unreachable webpack chunk loader in `leaflet-distortableimage`**. The bundled library includes webpack's lazy-chunk loader (`__webpack_require__.l`) which dynamically creates a `<script>` element to fetch chunks. The plugin emits a single bundle (no code splitting), so this code path never executes, but the dynamic `createElement('script')` was flagged at error severity by the Community automated review. A postinstall patch (mirroring the existing `patch-family-chart.js`) now stubs the loader to a no-op that immediately invokes its `done()` callback. No user-visible change.

### Changed

- **Migrated ODT generation from `jszip` to `fflate`**. `jszip`'s bundled output contained four UMD module-detection guards using `document.createElement('script')`, all flagged at error severity. `fflate` is a smaller, modern, no-UMD-detection alternative supporting the same writer + reader subset. The migration introduces a thin adapter at `src/utils/zip.ts` (`ZipBuilder` / `ZipReader`, JSZip-shaped API) so the four call sites (Reports -> ODT, Family Chart -> ODT, Book -> ODT, and Gramps `.gpkg` import) needed only near-mechanical edits. Functional behavior is unchanged; ODT files open identically in LibreOffice and the Gramps reader handles both tar.gz and ZIP-format `.gpkg` inputs as before. Centralizing the library boundary in the adapter means any future `fflate` version bump or alternative-library swap is a one-file change.
- **Internal: ZIP-format `.gpkg` test fixture**. Added `tests/fixtures/gramps/gramps-app-export-test11-small-zip.gpkg` plus a repack script (`tests/fixtures/gramps/repack-to-zip.js`) that converts tar.gz `.gpkg` archives (the format Gramps' export wizard produces) into ZIP-format archives. Lets the ZIP reader code path in `gpkg-extractor.ts` be exercised in dev-vault testing; Gramps does not produce ZIP-format `.gpkg` directly, so the existing fixtures only covered the tar.gz reader path.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/compare/0.22.39...0.22.40

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### BRAT users

Charted Roots is now in the Community Plugins directory. If you installed via BRAT, see [Discussion #573](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/discussions/573) for the 3-step switch. Skipping the migration is fine, but if you install from the directory *without* first removing Charted Roots from BRAT, some views may misbehave.

---

### What's new

**Event Relative ordering UI** ([#569](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/569)) - the Create / Edit Event modal now exposes "After these events:" and "Before these events:" chip-list pickers for setting `before` / `after` frontmatter arrays. The topological sort in the timeline exporters has supported these constraints since v0.20.x; until now they could only be set via manual YAML editing.

### Map view fixes

- **Spurious WebSocket console error in DevTools during edit mode** is gone. `leaflet-distortableimage` accidentally bundles a dev-server hot-reload client; a postinstall patch now stubs it out.
- **Cluster cleanup no longer breaks subsequent map opens.** The pre-existing `L.DistanceGrid is not a constructor` cascade is contained via defensive try-catches in `MapController.destroy()`. Root cause tracked at [#574](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/574).
- **Plugin registrations survive repeated open / close cycles.** Both leaflet plugin loaders defensively reattach `window.L = L` on every invocation.
- **Child-map header layout no longer wraps awkwardly** on long map names. Breadcrumb moved to its own row above the toolbar, selector capped at 240px with ellipsis truncation.

### Fixed

- **Family Chart no longer freezes Obsidian when relationship data is asymmetric** ([#575](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/575)). Person notes where a relationship `_id` (e.g., `father_id`) is set but the wikilink half is missing previously caused family-chart's tree construction to enter an infinite loop. A bidirectional-symmetry pass now drops unreciprocated parent / child / spouse references before they reach the library; the chart renders correctly with whatever symmetric data remains. Reporter [@D4B2A](https://github.com/D4B2A).

### Internal

25 duplicate-selector warnings between `map-view.css` and `leaflet-distortable.css` consolidated into a single theme-aware block. `styles.css` drops 274 lines, Community automated review's CSS lint surface is now in its fully-irreducible state.

**883 tests passing across 68 suites**.

The largest visible change is a migration of a number of button markup sites to Obsidian's native `ButtonComponent` API: modal "Cancel" / "Save" / "Apply" footers across the plugin now match Obsidian's standard button styling and inherit your theme's button look automatically. Compact list-row buttons (in places like the organizations, places, and sources lists) stay at their current smaller size for layout density.

Also in this release:

- Eliminated 60+ cross-file CSS duplicate selectors flagged by the scanner.
- Replaced 27 `:has()` selectors with explicit class-based equivalents; the perf-warning count drops to near-zero.
- Removed `!important` from 5 of 6 sites where it wasn't actually needed.
- Dropped ~750 lines of dead code, mostly internal hygiene with no user-facing impact.

### Fixed

883 tests passing across 68 suites.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
A targeted scan-warning cleanup release responding to the v0.22.36 community automated review (which passed but reported a large warning surface, `:has()` perf, `!important`, browser partial-support, duplicate-declaration, and cross-file duplicate-selector warnings). No user-facing changes; all internal hygiene aimed at the scan.

## What changed

**`build-css.js` strip narrowed to file-level directives only.** v0.22.34 introduced a blanket strip of `stylelint-*` directives from the bundle to dodge the cross-component bleed problem from v0.22.32/33. That strip was too broad; it removed the per-line `stylelint-disable-next-line` directives that suppress `:has()` perf, `!important`, and browser partial-support warnings, alongside the file-level forms it was actually targeting. The strip now uses a negative-lookahead regex `(?!-[a-z])` to distinguish file-level forms (stripped, they bleed) from per-line forms (preserved, they don't). Restores per-line suppression for roughly 100+ scan warnings.

**Per-line stylelint silencers added at the 9 documented-legitimate `!important` sites.** Locations span `base.css`, `control-center.css`, `family-chart-view.css`, `map-view.css`, and `universe-wizard.css`. Each directive carries a `-- reason` description naming the legitimate use case: utility-class display toggle (must beat all caller specificities), leaflet defensive tile override (max-width / max-height to allow custom sizing), family-chart inline-style override during overlay animation.

**Three truly-identical utility-class duplicates consolidated into `base.css`.** `.cr-hidden` (moved from `control-center.css` + `universe-wizard.css` into `base.css` as a shared selector group with `.crc-hidden`); `.crc-hidden` (duplicate removed from `control-center.css`); `.crc-text--center` (duplicate removed from `place-modals.css`). The remaining cross-file duplicate-selector warnings in the scan represent intentionally-different rule bodies that share a class name (e.g., `.crc-modal-header` in `media-modals.css` vs. `control-center.css` are two distinct modal-header styles). Those aren't safe to merge and stay as-is.

**Five stylelint duplicate-declaration warnings resolved** in `styles/control-center.css`. `.crc-field-list code` had four duplicate properties (`padding`, `background`, `border-radius`, `font-size`) from an earlier-draft / later-edit overlap. Cascade-aware merge kept the final values plus the missing `font-family`. `.cr-progress-bar-container` had three same-value duplicates (`height`, `background`, `border-radius`) — removed.

## Scanner-architecture notes (docs)

[`docs/developer/automated-review-notes.md`](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/docs/developer/automated-review-notes.md) now records two new findings:

- **Description-requirement applies to ALL inline directives**, not just disables. The v0.22.35 scan flagged 125 `eslint-enable` directives lacking `-- reason` text. Pattern: `/* eslint-enable <rules> -- reason. */`.
- **Vendored-library findings for the 9 dynamic `<script>` element creations** flagged in v0.22.36. All benign polyfill code: ~5 sites in `core-js` (pulled in transitively via `jspdf` → `canvg` as an `optionalDependency`), 3 sites in `jspdf`'s own deferral helpers, 1 site in `leaflet-distortableimage`'s webpack chunk loader. None load external scripts. Possible mitigations (drop `canvg` via npm override, replace `leaflet-distortableimage`, replace `jspdf`) deferred as moderate risk; accepted as documented vendored-polyfill false positives.

## Stability

Fifteenth patch in the v0.22.22-anchored stability window. All changes internal hygiene with no user-facing surface; none reset the soak clock. **883 tests passing across 68 suites**, lint clean of errors, build clean.

## Install

Charted Roots is installed via the [BRAT](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat) Obsidian plugin. Community Plugins directory submission is in progress; once approved, install directly from Obsidian's Settings -> Community Plugins.

Release assets (`main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css`) carry verified GitHub artifact attestations. Verify with:

```sh
gh attestation verify main.js --repo banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots
```

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
A same-day follow-up to v0.22.35 that resolves the new error category surfaced by the Community automated review after v0.22.35's release and (hopefully!) completes the recursive orphan sweep that v0.22.35's dead-code removal missed. No user-facing changes; all internal hygiene.

## What changed

**125 `Unexpected undescribed directive comment` errors -> resolved.** Every EOF `eslint-enable` directive that v0.22.35 added (paired with the existing line-1 `eslint-disable` for the `@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-*` family) now carries a `-- reason` description. The line-1 disables already had descriptions (`-- Obsidian API returns any-typed surfaces ...`); only the v0.22.35-added EOF enables lacked them. The scanner enforces description-required on disable AND enable directives. Each enable now reads `/* eslint-enable <rules> -- Match scope of file-level disable at top. */`.

**Recursive orphan sweep, five transitive layers cleared.** v0.22.35 deleted thirteen `_*`-prefixed dormant helpers based on the scan's unused-vars list at that point in time. That cleanup didn't iterate: removing those helpers orphaned ~40 imports and another eleven downstream functions whose only callers were among the deleted code. Each iteration of the cleanup surfaced the next layer of orphans (imports -> functions -> more imports -> more functions, five layers deep) until lint reported no remaining unused-vars in the affected files.

**Net code reduction: −872 lines across `people-tab.ts` and `trees-tab.ts`.** Imports removed include `Setting`, `TFile`, `normalizePath`, `CanvasGenerator`, `GedcomImporterV2`, `GedcomImportProgressModal`, `PersonPickerModal`, `ReferenceNumberingService`, `createPersonNote`, `renderPersonTimeline`, `EvidenceService`-adjacent symbols, and many more. Downstream functions removed include `handleGedcomImportV2`, `executeGedcomImport`, `promptAssignReferenceNumbersAfterImport`, `selectRootPersonForNumbering`, `renderFactCoverageDetails`, `renderProofSummariesSection`, `renderProofCard`, `deleteProofSummary`, plus several smaller helpers.

## Lesson captured for future cleanups

After deleting dead code, ALWAYS run `npm run lint | grep "never used\|but never used"` and iterate until the filter is empty before declaring cleanup done. Directed lists from a single scan can hide cascades that only surface when the leading layer is removed.

## Stability

Fourteenth patch in the v0.22.22-anchored stability window. All changes internal hygiene with no user-facing surface; none reset the soak clock. **883 tests passing across 68 suites**, lint clean of errors, build clean.

## Install

Charted Roots is installed via the [BRAT](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat) Obsidian plugin. Community Plugins directory submission is in progress; once approved, install directly from Obsidian's Settings -> Community Plugins.

Release assets (`main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css`) carry verified GitHub artifact attestations. Verify with:

```sh
gh attestation verify main.js --repo banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots
```

A targeted scan-cleanup release. The Obsidian Community automated review against v0.22.34 surfaced seven gating errors plus a handful of source-level warnings; each is addressed here. No user-facing changes; all internal hygiene.

## What changed

**Five `@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-*` scope errors -> paired with EOF enables.** Across 125 source files, every file-level `eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-*` directive now has a matching `eslint-enable` at end of file, mirroring the trimmed rule list rule-for-rule. The scanner enforces scope hygiene on disables; the disables themselves silence the underlying ~600 `any`-from-Obsidian-API patterns from showing up as warnings.

**Two `obsidianmd/ui/sentence-case` errors -> file-level disables removed entirely.** The scanner forbids disabling this rule (`Disabling 'obsidianmd/ui/sentence-case' is not allowed`), even though the eslint-plugin itself doesn't enforce that restriction. (It lives in a "Required" wrapper layer the scanner adds on top.) Historical scan inspection showed the rule's underlying violations have never appeared in scan output, so the file-level disables that landed in v0.22.34's CSS hygiene sweep were a strict regression. They're gone now; local lint keeps the rule at `warn` with project-specific `brands` and `acronyms` arrays.

None had external callers; removal also drops orphaned support (a `RelationshipField` interface, `fatherField` / `motherField` / `spouseField` state, an `updateHelpText` helper, two unused imports).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
A scan-cleanup release. The v0.22.33 stylelint error-level blocker is fixed and the bulk of Obsidian's Community automated review warning surface is cleared. One user-facing fix tags along.

## What you'll notice

- **Map view Fullscreen button now shows an icon.** Long-standing visual gap: the leaflet-fullscreen package's CSS references a sprite PNG that was never bundled into our `styles.css`, so the button has been a blank square since the feature first shipped. Replaced with inline SVG icons drawn via CSS mask (theme-adaptive, adapts to light and dark themes).

## Under the hood

This release leans heavily on internal cleanup aimed at the new Community automated review platform:

- **`!important` reduced 102 -> 11 across stylesheets.** Most uses were defensive against expected style competition rather than necessary inline-style overrides. Refactored via specificity (added `.cr-map-view` prefix to leaflet rules, chained-class modifiers, etc.). The remaining 11 are all legitimate (leaflet's defensive core, `.cr-hidden` utility classes, documented inline-style overrides).
- **21 duplicate-selector pairs consolidated.** Net −189 lines across `control-center.css`, `cleanup-wizard.css`, `import-export-wizard.css`, `profile-view.css`, `tree-output.css`. Some were pure deletes (subset / overridden / identical); others required real cascade-aware merges (property merging into the surviving rule).
- **`:has()` perf warnings silenced** at 46 sites via per-line catch-all disables. My uses are narrowly scoped to modal sizing, where broad selector invalidation isn't a concern.
- **`no-unsafe-*` typing warnings silenced** at ~2,855 sites across 146 source files via file-level disables. Obsidian's API returns `any`-typed surfaces (frontmatter, file caches, plugin state); proper wrapper typing would add code without changing runtime safety.
- **Sentence-case Batch B audit closed** at 436 sites via file-level disables across 102 source files (the rule misfires on quoted button labels, month names, and proper-noun section paths).
- **Hex color format aligned to 6-digit form** across the component stylesheets.
- **Bundled `styles.css` no longer carries stylelint directives.** The build step strips them from the bundle; source files keep them for per-file lint.
- Browser partial-support warnings (text-indent, text-decoration longhands, multicolumn, break-inside) annotated.

## Known limitations carried forward

- **main.js > 5 MB.** Obsidian Sync Standard subscribers can't sync this file. Workarounds: install via BRAT, install manually, or upgrade to Sync Plus. Users on other tiers (or no sync) are unaffected. Bundle reduction is tracked for a 1.x polish milestone.

## Stats

- 883 tests passing across 68 suites
- Twelfth patch in the v0.22.22-anchored stability window; all `medium-priority` or lower, none reset
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.34

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
A single-fix follow-up to v0.22.32. Obsidian's Community automated scan against v0.22.32 turned up one error-level finding — a `no rules have been disabled` stylelint complaint at `styles.css:90`, which was the inverse of the directive-bleed bug v0.22.32 was meant to fix. The defensive auto-inject from v0.22.32 was emitting a closing `/* stylelint-enable */` between every component, even those that had no file-level disable to close, producing an orphan enable on the bundle.

## What changed

The auto-inject is now conditional. `build-css.js` counts file-level `stylelint-disable` and `stylelint-enable` directives per component and emits the closing enable only when there are more disables than enables. Line-scoped variants (`stylelint-disable-next-line`, `stylelint-disable-line`) don't count toward the balance since they don't bleed across components.

In the current source tree no component has an unbalanced count (every file with a file-level disable closes it explicitly) so the auto-inject doesn't fire for any current component. The defensive net stays in place for future component files that forget to close their scope.

## Scan posture going forward

Everything else from the v0.22.32 scan was warning-tier:

- Both build-provenance attestations on `main.js` and `styles.css` now show as verified passes - the CI workflow's main feature working end-to-end.
- `>5 MB main.js` (Sync Standard issue) - bundle-reduction work tracked separately.
- `setInterval` + network - false positive (the three setInterval sites are journey playback / animation tickers / count-up text; the network call lives in `geocoding-service.ts`; different code paths).
- ~600 `Unsafe any` source warnings - long-tail typing work.
- `chalk` dev-dep note - build-script-only, not bundled to users.

## Stats

- 883 tests passing across 68 suites
- Eleventh patch in the v0.22.22-anchored stability window; all `medium-priority` or lower, none reset

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
A short tidy-up patch on the heels of v0.22.31. One user-visible fix (the era-abbreviation follow-up from [#563](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/563)), one new piece of release infrastructure (build-provenance attestations + a CI workflow), and a quiet pile of internal alignment with `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd` 0.3.0, which launched the same day as Obsidian's new Community automated review platform and reversed several of yesterday's rules.

## What you'll notice

- **Era abbreviations now render in the dynamic Timeline flat-list view** ([#563](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/563) follow-up). v0.22.31's fix wired the era-aware year display into the `{year}` template substitution and the sectioned default render, but missed the flat-list path, which is the most common Timeline-block rendering. Inputs like `BBY 1045`, `ABY 25`, `EF 30` now keep their era prefix in the flat-list view too. Caught by [@doctorwodka](https://github.com/doctorwodka) during v0.22.31 verification, with a relationship-block confirmation from [@DigitalDreamn](https://github.com/DigitalDreamn).

## Verifiable releases

Releases now publish through a GitHub Actions workflow that runs the full gate set (lint + lint:css + test + build), attests build provenance on `main.js` / `manifest.json` / `styles.css` via `actions/attest-build-provenance@v2`, and creates a draft release on tag push. The release description is still hand-curated; the workflow just produces the artifacts and the attestation.

End users and Obsidian's scanners can verify any release asset:

```sh
gh attestation verify main.js --repo banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots
```

This addresses the two missing-attestation Recommendations from Obsidian's Community automated review and aligns with the build-provenance posture the platform expects going forward.

## Behind the scenes

- **Community-review scan blocker cleared**: the bundled `styles.css` was emitting a `selector-class-pattern has already been disabled` stylelint error, caused by two component CSS files (`leaflet-distortable.css`, `timeline-callouts.css`) opening with `/* stylelint-disable */` and missing the matching enable, so the disables bled across the concatenated bundle. Per-file fix plus a defensive `build-css.js` change that auto-injects `/* stylelint-enable */` between every component, so future component files can't recreate this pattern.
- **`eslint-plugin-obsidianmd` upgraded 0.2.9 -> 0.3.0** - the new release reversed v0.22.31's `activeWindow.X` timer migration (the rule now recommends `window.X`) and dropped its `prefer-create-el` rule. ~152 timer sites flipped back; eight previously-unflagged `requestAnimationFrame` calls covered as well.
- **Bare `document.X` migrated to `activeDocument.X`** across 163 sites for pop-out window correctness - same shape as v0.22.31's `activeWindow` migration but for the document object. No visible change unless you use pop-outs.
- 46 `document.createElementNS svg` calls converted to Obsidian's `createSvg(tag)` helper; ten lazy `require()` call sites converted to top-level ES imports; the local `no-unused-vars` warning category cleared.

## Stats

- 883 tests passing across 68 suites
- Tenth patch in the v0.22.22-anchored stability window; all `medium-priority` or lower, none reset
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.32

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
This patch closes a fast-moving five-issue fictional-date reporter cluster that surfaced overnight. Every fix is mechanical and self-contained, so the regression risk is low. It also lands the first round of fixes for Obsidian's new Community automated review platform (launched today) and clears the long-standing pop-out-window-timer backlog.

## One thing to know up front

The "Open command menu" command has been renamed to **"Open quick actions"** (id `open-quick-actions`). The new Obsidian Community automated review rejects commands whose id or name contains the word "command". The launcher itself is unchanged; same searchable categorized modal, same six categories, same content. If you had a custom hotkey bound to the previous "Open command menu", **the binding will go inactive after the update**; rebind it under **Settings -> Hotkeys** by searching for "Open quick actions". The Control Center dashboard tile is now labeled "Quick actions" to match.

## Fictional-date fixes ([@doctorwodka](https://github.com/doctorwodka) and [@DigitalDreamn](https://github.com/DigitalDreamn))

- **[#562](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/562)** - Fictional dates with trailing "ish" or "?" (e.g., `EF 10ish`, `circa BBY 1045`) no longer drop off the Timeline Density chart. The parser now strips approximation markers before pattern matching.
- **[#563](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/563)** - Era abbreviations now render alongside years in the dynamic Timeline and Relationship blocks. Inputs like `BBY 1045`, `ABY 25`, or any custom-era abbreviation no longer get stripped to bare digits.
- **[#564](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/564)** - Multi-era events sort by canonical year when you click "Compute sort order" in the events tab. Previously `EF 10` and `DE 5` compared as alphabetical strings.
- **[#565](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/565)** - Timeline-block age annotations no longer silently render era-stripped values for cross-era inputs (the "100 years off" symptom).
- **[#566](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/566)** - Cross-era multi-era characters no longer silently drop from Longevity Analysis and Marriage Patterns. Resolved alongside #562.

## Pop-out windows and plugin startup

If you ever drag a Charted Roots tab into its own window, the refresh-debounce and focus-deferral timers now run on the window the view is actually in, so they fire correctly even after the main window goes to the background. This was a 152-warning category from the new ESLint plugin upgrade; cleared across three commits. No user-visible change unless you use pop-outs.

Plugin load shaves sub-100ms by deferring vault-listener registration and the relationship-history init to layout-ready, matching the existing pattern used for the version-upgrade check.

## Under the hood

A handful of internal correctness and consistency improvements:

- **Real fix**: the Create Person modal and post-GEDCOM-import sync sweep now respect folder filter, inclusive-parents, and DNA-tracking settings (two paths were bypassing them).
- The eleven `UniverseService` consumer sites consolidate onto the `createUniverseService` factory, matching the pattern used elsewhere.
- The categorized command launcher's modal classes stay unchanged internally; only the user-facing command id, name, and dashboard tile label changed.

## Stability window

This is the ninth patch in the v0.22.22-anchored window. (All `medium-priority` or lower, none reset.) **880 tests passing across 68 suites** (was 834 / 63; +46 from the fictional-date cluster).

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
A patch around two reporter-surfaced fixes and the housekeeping side of a recent ESLint plugin upgrade.

## What you'll notice

- **Negative-year decades on Statistics now bucket correctly** ([#560](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/560)) - on Longevity Analysis and Timeline Density, fictional-vault birth years like `-25` no longer land in the `-30s` bucket. The "-20s decade" now spans `-20` through `-29` the way you'd expect. Reported by [@doctorwodka](https://github.com/doctorwodka).

- **Wikilinks no longer redirect to recovered-duplicate files** ([#559](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/559)) - when a vault had a canonical Charted Roots note plus an outside-CR duplicate sharing the same `cr_id` (typically recovered via Obsidian's File Recovery, or copied during troubleshooting), saving a related note could silently rewrite the wikilink to point at the duplicate. The note then "disappeared" from Family blocks and Edit Person children lists because the resolver no longer landed on a CR-typed note. The fix scopes cr_id resolution to files that also carry the expected `cr_type` in their frontmatter, so non-CR duplicates can't shadow your canonical notes anymore. Surfaced by [@DigitalDreamn](https://github.com/DigitalDreamn) via [#537](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/537).

## Behind the scenes

- **Family Chart cards no longer interpret person names as HTML.** The circle-card render path was rebuilt using DOM APIs instead of an `outerHTML` template literal, so an `<` or `&` in a name no longer renders as markup.
- **ESLint baseline cleared** of all real-signal errors after the recent `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd` 0.2.9 upgrade. 76 fixes covering fire-and-forget promises, frontmatter coercion safety, cross-window-safe `instanceOf` checks, and dev-dependency hygiene. Plus a first pass on the sentence-case rebaseline.

## Stats

- 834 tests passing across 63 suites
- Eighth patch in the v0.22.22-anchored stability window; all `medium-priority` or lower, none reset

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## What's in this release

A patch built around a proactive bidirectional-sync audit and one bug surfaced during testing.

### What you'll notice

- **Property aliases UI now lists `alt_name`, `pronouns`, `religion`, and `caste`.** If you use custom YAML key names for descriptive fields, you can now map them through Settings -> Properties -> Person properties. The fields shipped throughout the rest of the plugin months ago but never made it to the aliases UI.
- **Renaming a person now updates wikilinks on every relationship-array field that references them**, including indexed-spouse slots, `adopted_child` / `step_child` arrays on parents' notes, the gender-neutral `adoptive_parent` array, and any custom relationship like `mentor` or `godparent`. Previously, only a hardcoded set of nine fields got the canonical-form rewrap.
- **The bidirectional linker now honors the `partners` alias for `spouse`.** If you've chosen `partners` as your canonical spouse-field name in the property aliases UI, you now get reciprocal writes, marriage-detail mirroring, and deletion detection — same as users who use `spouse:`.
- **Setting `step_child: [[Child]]` on a parent's note now back-fills the child's `stepfather` / `stepmother`.** The reverse direction has always worked; this closes the symmetric gap.

### Quieter improvements

- **Organization member lists and Add Relationship reciprocal writes** now produce canonical-form wikilinks with basename-ambiguity disambiguation, matching how the rest of the writer surface has been working since v0.22.27.
- **Self-referential typos no longer auto-propagate.** If you accidentally point a kinship field at the same person's own note, the linker now logs a warning and skips, matching how the bio paths have always behaved.

### Under the hood

- **Bidirectional-sync audit** ([planning doc](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/docs/planning/bidirectional-sync-audit.md)) catalogued the five disjoint sync locations and the seven concrete gaps across the six relationship field families. Six fixes shipped here; the seventh (delete-cleanup for non-person entities) is filed as #557 for post-1.0.
- **822 tests passing across 61 suites** (was 818). Four new regressions cover the `partners` alias paths in `isSpouseInFrontmatter`.

### Issues closed

#551, #552, #553, #554, #555, #556, #558. (#557 remains open as a post-1.0 tracking issue.)

---

This is the seventh patch in the v0.22.22-anchored stability window. None of the fixes reset the gate; soak continues through ~2026-05-28.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.29

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
A focused patch closing the remaining display-layer gaps in the wikilink-input cluster and adding property-based fuzz coverage to catch the next variant before users see it.

## What changed

**Edit Person modal - Spouse field cleanup ([#543](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/543) follow-up).** When opening Edit Person on someone with a spouse linked via a disambiguated wikilink (e.g., `[[Charted Roots/People/Rebecca Wilkin|Rebecca Wilkin]]`), the spouse list now shows just the display name (`Rebecca Wilkin`) same as Father / Mother already do. The marriage location field gets the same cleanup. The v0.22.25 fix had covered Father / Mother / place fields but missed the indexed-spouse render path; @DigitalDreamn caught the gap during verification with a clear screenshot, and this release closes it.

**Edit Organization and Edit Event modals - same display cleanup ([#549](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/549)).** Parent organization, Seat, Place, and Timeline fields all now display the friendly name when the underlying frontmatter holds a path-form wikilink. For the user-typed fields (where you can edit the value freely), the writer also re-canonicalizes on save so edits don't lose the wikilink structure.

## Under the hood

**Property-based fuzz coverage extended to four more wikilink helpers ([#548](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/548)).** The existing fuzz suite for `createSmartWikilink` (added between v0.22.24 and v0.22.25) now has parallel suites for the helpers that handle wikilink display (`extractDisplayLabel`), basename disambiguation (`getCanonicalLinktext`), and the organization / event-side wikilink writers. +79 tests across four files. When the next variant in this bug cluster surfaces, the fix flow becomes "add the new shape to the corpus, the existing assertions catch it" (proactive prevention rather than reactive triage).

The fuzz expansion also caught a small whitespace-handling gap in `extractDisplayLabel` (bracket-wrapped inputs with internal whitespace preserved the spaces); fixed with a one-line trim.

## Stability

- Sixth patch in the v0.22.22-anchored window. All `low-priority` or defensive coverage; none reset the gate.
- 818 tests passing across 61 suites (was 739 / 58). +79 tests.
- Reporter: @DigitalDreamn for #543's spouse-field gap.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.28

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
A preventative patch, no user-reported bugs, just proactive cleanup work during the v0.22.22-anchored stability window.

## What changed

Charted Roots has four "graph" services that hold in-memory caches of your vault's people, places, organizations, and universes. Each service rebuilds its cache after writes (e.g., creating an organization, editing a universe, running a data-quality cleanup). All four shared a subtle race: the rebuild ran *before* Obsidian's metadata cache had finished indexing the just-written change, which meant new entries could be silently dropped from the cache or edited entries could show pre-edit values until something else triggered another reload.

The family-graph service had been masking the race with a hard-coded 2-second wait inside its reload path; the other three had no mitigation at all. v0.22.27 replaces that 2-second wait with an event-driven one. The cache rebuild now waits exactly as long as Obsidian needs (typically tens of milliseconds), then proceeds.

## What you'll notice

- **Batch operations finish ~2 seconds faster.** Data-quality cleanups on the People tab, the cleanup wizard, and the bidirectional-relationship fix flow all paid an unconditional 2-second tax on every run. Gone.
- **Newly created organizations, universes, and places appear immediately** in their respective dropdowns and pickers. Pre-0.22.27, a new entity could occasionally be invisible until you reopened the relevant view; that window is closed.
- **No user-facing changes** beyond the above (this is internal plumbing).

## Stability

- 5th patch in the v0.22.22-anchored window (after v0.22.23, v0.22.24, v0.22.25, v0.22.26). All preventative or low-priority. None reset the gate.
- 739 tests passing across 58 suites (was 730 / 56). 9 new regression tests cover the cache-race shape across the four services.
- Tracking issue [#547](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/547) stays open for the audit's other half (external-edit invalidation), which is deferred to 1.x
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.27

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
**Title:** Charted Roots v0.22.26

A structural patch around adopted/step children in family-relationship rendering. Five user-facing fixes ship behind one architectural change: a new `RelationshipQueryService` ([#546](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/546)) that consolidates how every consumer walks the family-relationship graph. Pre-#546, each renderer / report / exporter reimplemented its own walk, leaving coverage gaps every time the schema added a new relationship type.

- **Canvas Family Tree now renders adopted children when generated from any ancestor of the adoptive parent** ([#545](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/545)). The adoptive-parent edge was being emitted but the child was never added as a positioned node, so canvas rendering dropped the edge for missing endpoint position. Fixed across the descendant-tree builder, full-tree builder, and the family-chart layout's fallback positioning.

- **Step-parent edges now emit regardless of traversal order** ([#545](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/545) thread). Edges were silently dropped when the step-parent was reached via a different path first. The cycle-detection check was doubling as an edge-emission gate. Edge emission now decoupled from cycle-checking; the visited set only governs further BFS traversal.

- **Family timelines now include adopted and step children**. The family-timeline view (People Tab badge, Control Center modal) was iterating bio children only. A focal person whose only children were adopted would see a timeline showing just self and spouse. Same fix corrects the badge's `memberCount` undercount for blended families.

- **GEDCOM-X export now includes gender-neutral parent relationships**. The exporter walked gender-specific bio and adoptive parent fields but never the gender-neutral `parents:` array (persons using that notation had their parent relationships silently omitted from export). Consolidating into one service call closed the gap.

**Under the hood:** Fourteen consumer files migrated to route through the new service; each call site now declares its `include` variant explicitly (`'all' | 'bio' | 'adopted' | 'step'`). The standalone `sibling-walker` helper module retired entirely. Remaining sites (canvas-split, layout engines, parts of profile-view) are tracked under [#546](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/546) for follow-up (architectural consistency, not user-visible bugs).

**Stability window unchanged.** All five user-facing fixes are `bug` (medium-priority); none reset the gate. Anchor stays at [v0.22.22](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/releases/tag/0.22.22), soak window through ~2026-05-28.

**730 tests passing across 56 suites** (+24 net from v0.22.25: 35 new for the service, 11 new for tree-builder regressions, 22 retired with `sibling-walker`).

**Reporter:** thanks to @DigitalDreamn for [#545](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/545) and the step-parent edge case, which surfaced the architectural pattern that drove the rest of this release.

**Full changelog:** [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#02226---2026-05-09)
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.26

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
**Title:** Charted Roots v0.22.25

A focused three-fix patch closing the remaining loose ends from the [#537](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/537) verification cluster (a UI display gap, a writer-side bypass, and a cache-timing race). Each is a different symptom of the same theme: code paths that should have gone through the canonical helpers but didn't.

- **Edit Person modal now parses wikilinks for display** ([#543](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/543)). The "Linked to:" labels and read-only input fields were rendering raw wikilink content (`Charted Roots/People/Errol Naberrie|Errol Naberrie`) instead of clean labels (`Errol Naberrie`). Surfaced after [v0.22.24](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/releases/tag/0.22.24)'s path-disambiguation landed — the canonical `[[path|basename]]` form is correct on disk but the modal didn't parse it for display.

- **Org wikilink writes from the membership flow now go through `createSmartWikilink`** ([#542](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/542)). `addMembership` was pushing the org wikilink into the `membership_orgs` array verbatim, bypassing the input-shape normalization and basename-ambiguity disambiguation that every other relationship-field write got. New entries now go through the canonical helper, and a full-array rewrite pass on every save normalizes existing entries — historical residue heals on the next add or remove.

- **Org-side member sync now waits for the metadata cache to refresh before reading person notes** ([#541](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/541) follow-up). The v0.22.24 fix triggered the sync from person-side membership flows, but the sync re-read the metadata cache to assemble the member list, and Obsidian's `processFrontMatter` updates the file synchronously while the cache update fires asynchronously. Result: the sync ran on stale cache and wrote a member list "trailing one update behind" (adding person N propagated person N-1 to the org's frontmatter). The fix adds a cache-refresh wait before triggering the sync.

**Plus a defensive test infrastructure addition:** a property-based fuzz suite for `createSmartWikilink` exercising the writer against every input shape seen in the wild ([#537](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/537), [#538](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/538), [#540](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/540)). Catches the next variant in this cluster proactively (fix shape becomes "add the new shape to the corpus + the existing test catches it.")

**Stability window unchanged.** All three fixes are `low-priority` or refinements. Anchor stays at [v0.22.22](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/releases/tag/0.22.22), soak window through ~2026-05-28.

**706 tests passing across 55 suites** (+35 from v0.22.24: 15 new for the fuzz suite, 20 new for `extractDisplayLabel`).

**Reporter:** thanks to @DigitalDreamn for the careful verification work that surfaced all three.

**Full changelog:** [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#02225---2026-05-08)
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.25

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
A four-fix batch closing relationship-correctness gaps surfaced during the [v0.22.22](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/releases/tag/0.22.22) verification sweep.

**Two extensions to the v0.22.22 wikilink-writer self-heal pattern:**

- **File paths in wikilink alias slots now self-heal on save** ([#538](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/538)). Some on-disk wikilinks had a file path in the alias slot, typically residue from periods when a vault contained duplicate-basename files outside the plugin's folder structure. The v0.22.22 self-heal collapsed pipe-stem accumulation but didn't recognize path-form input. The fix adds a slash-strip step so the path collapses back to the basename on the next Edit Person save.
- **The wikilink writer now disambiguates when a basename is shared with another vault file** ([#540](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/540)). When two files share a basename (e.g., a plugin-managed person note and an unrelated note elsewhere in the vault) Obsidian's link resolver could land on the wrong file. The writer now detects ambiguity at write time and emits the path-form target with the basename as alias, so links point unambiguously to the intended file while the display text stays clean.

**Plus two more relationship surfaces:**

- **Dynamic Relationship Block's `type: all` mode now displays custom-typed relationships** ([#539](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/539)). The wiki claimed "all" mode would show extended family plus custom-typed relationships from the `relationships` array (mentor, godparent, ally, etc.); in practice only family-graph-derived sections rendered. The fix fetches custom relationships via the relationship service, filters to non-family-mapped types, and renders one section per type after Siblings.
- **Adding a membership from Edit Person now syncs to the organization's `members` frontmatter** ([#541](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/541)). The reciprocal sync only fired from the org-side "Manage Members" modal; person-side adds left the org's own frontmatter stale. Mostly invisible in the standard views (which scan person notes), but Obsidian Bases queries against the org's frontmatter saw out-of-date data. The fix calls the existing sync from both `addMembership` and `removeMembership`.

**Stability window unchanged:** all four fixes are `medium-priority` or `low-priority`, none reset the [1.0 stability gate](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/VERSIONING.md#when-100-ships). Anchor stays at v0.22.22, soak window through ~2026-05-28.

**671 tests passing across 54 suites** (+10 from v0.22.23 for #538 + #540 coverage).

**Reporter:** thanks to @DigitalDreamn for the careful verification sweep that surfaced all four.

**Full changelog:** [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#02224---2026-05-08)
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.24

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
A focused single-fix patch for the original [#534](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/534) marriage-detail symmetric-write bug.

When linking a new spouse via Edit Person and adding marriage details (date / location / status / divorce date) in the same save, the indexed companion fields used to write to the editing person's frontmatter but never mirror onto the spouse's. The spouse ended up with just the flat `spouse:` link. No marriage details. Reported by @DigitalDreamn last night while debugging the wider cascade.

**The fix is unified.** Both new-link and re-edit-with-details paths now route the target into indexed format whenever the source provides marriage details, regardless of the target's current shape. Empty targets get `spouse1` + `spouse1_id` + `spouse1_marriage_*`. Targets with existing flat data for other spouses get a new indexed slot alongside the flat data. (`detectSpouseTargetFormat` recognizes the mixed state as 'indexed' on the next sync and consolidation happens naturally over time.)

**Stability window unchanged** - still anchored to [v0.22.22](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/releases/tag/0.22.22), ~21 days of soak ahead. #534 is labeled `medium-priority`, not critical/data-loss, so the [1.0 stability gate](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/VERSIONING.md#when-100-ships) doesn't reset.

**661 tests passing across 54 suites** (+10 from v0.22.22 for the new `findNextOpenSpouseSlot` helper).

**Full changelog:** [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#02223---2026-05-07)
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.23

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
A critical data-loss fix anchors this patch and gives it the headline. **#537** addresses a wikilink alias-accumulation cascade where repeated saves added another `|alias` segment to existing wikilinks until they became unparseable, at which point the bidirectional linker treated the slot as broken and silently dropped it, propagating the loss outward through related notes (children scrubbed from parents, parents from kids).

**The fix is self-healing.** Vaults with already-corrupted entries (triple-pipe wikilinks like `[[basename|basename|alias]]` or further-accumulated forms) collapse back to canonical form automatically on the next save through Edit Person — no manual repair needed.

The bug was a regression introduced in v0.22.17 / v0.22.19 work. Per the [1.0 stability gate](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/VERSIONING.md#when-100-ships), a critical/data-loss issue filed during the soak window resets the anchor. **New anchor: v0.22.22; new soak window through ~2026-05-28.**

Around #537, four enhancements:

- **Person Profile Memberships section** (#536) - new section between Relationships and Events on a person's profile, mirroring the inverse direction of the Organization Profile View's Members section.
- **Org Profile Members section grouped + sorted by role** (#535) - uppercase role headings, names sorted within each group, ordering matches the dynamic Members block via a shared helper.
- **Per-image captions in the dynamic media gallery block** (#523) - right-click any thumbnail to set, edit, or remove a caption. Captions persist as flat-YAML, ride through frozen-gallery export, and reshuffle in lockstep with media reorder.
- **Children section sorted by birth date** (#532 follow-up) - bio + adopted + step children intermix chronologically instead of appending by relationship type.

**651 tests passing across 54 suites.**

**Reporters:** thanks to @DigitalDreamn (#532 follow-up + #537), @doctorwodka (#535 + #536), and @xBlack-Dogx (#523).

**Full changelog:** [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#02222---2026-05-07)
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.22

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
Three fixes follow up on display gaps left over from v0.22.20's adopted-child routing change.

## Fixed

- **Adopted children and siblings now appear consistently across the Dynamic Relationship Block and Timeline** (#531). Two cascade effects from v0.22.20's #525/#526 routing change went unnoticed at release: bio-side siblings stopped seeing their adopted siblings, and adoptive parents stopped seeing their adopted children under "Children". Both views now surface those rows correctly, with adopted children labeled "Adopted child" — mirroring how adoptive parents are already labeled.
- **Siblings in the Dynamic Relationship Block now sort by birth date** (#532). Display order had always followed the order children were listed in the parent's frontmatter `children:` array (fine when that order matched birth order, but exposed the moment a sibling was added later or out of order). Sort respects custom-calendar conventions, so descending fictional eras (Star Wars BBY) order oldest-first the same as Gregorian dates.
- **Custom relationship types filed under the Family category now render in the Profile view** (#533). A user-defined type like `twin` configured under "Family" was being silently dropped between the Other subsection's category filter and the Family subsection's bio-only rendering, leaving the row invisible regardless of any notes. Family-category custom types now appear inline in the Family subsection with their notes from v0.22.20.

All three were reported by @DigitalDreamn while verifying v0.22.20.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.21

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
Three bug fixes, two of them follow-ups to v0.22.19 attempts that didn't actually resolve their bugs, plus a new one surfaced by the same reporter while testing.

## Fixed

- **Map view filter row no longer clips off the right edge on Android phones** ([#528](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/528)): The v0.22.19 attempt didn't actually fix this. The collection picker and year-range inputs on the Map view's toolbar now reliably wrap to their own line on phone-sized screens, instead of stacking against the right edge.

- **Relationship Calculator now produces correct labels in both directions for step and adoptive relationships** ([#525](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/525), [#526](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/526)): The v0.22.19 fix only resolved the child → parent direction. Going the other way (parent → adopted/step child) was still mislabeled as a blood relation. Now resolves correctly in both directions, and bio sibling paths through a stepparent (e.g., a stepchild ↔ the stepparent's bio child) correctly read as Stepsibling rather than blood sibling.

  *Behavior change*: existing vaults with step or adopted children declared on the parent's note (via the Add Custom Relationship modal's bidirectional propagation) will now read those relationships correctly. Previously they were silently misclassified as bio children.

- **Notes from the Add Custom Relationship modal are now persisted** ([#530](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/530), via discussion [#529](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/discussions/529)): The "Notes (optional)" textarea was capturing what users typed but silently discarding it on save. Notes now persist as parallel flat properties (e.g. `godparent_notes` alongside `godparent` / `godparent_id`) and display in the Entity Profile relationships section beneath each row that has one. Currently scoped to non-bio relationships (custom types + step / adoptive / foster / etc.).

Stability window remains anchored to v0.22.17. 622 tests passing across 52 suites.

Reporters: @DigitalDreamn (#525, #526, #530), @banisterious (#528).
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.20

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
Four bug fixes covering relationship calculations, wikilinks, and mobile layout.

## Fixed

- **Relationship Calculator now handles step and adoptive relationships** ([#525](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/525), [#526](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/526)): The calculator no longer returns wrong labels (or "Not related") for paths through step or adoptive parents. It now produces **Stepparent** / **Stepchild** / **Stepsibling** / **Adoptive parent** / **Adopted child** / **Adoptive sibling** labels in both directions, and flags `Blood relation: No` whenever a path crosses a step or adoptive edge.

  *Behavior change*: existing vaults with step or adoptive relationships will see corrected labels in the Calculator, the path-step display, and the kinship report generator. Paths previously labeled "Not related", "Sibling-in-law", or "Child / Blood: Yes" through these edges will now read with the correct prefix.

- **Wikilinks from the relationship picker now resolve correctly when a person's name differs from their filename** ([#524](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/524)): Wikilinks no longer fall back to a bare `[[name]]` form that doesn't resolve when the name and filename don't match (e.g., filing under maiden name with the display name set to married name).

- **Map view filter row no longer clips off the right edge on Android phones** ([#528](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/528)): The collection picker and year-range inputs on the Map view's toolbar now wrap to their own line on phone-sized screens, instead of overflowing past the viewport.

Stability window remains anchored to 0.22.17. 610 tests passing across 51 suites.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.19

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
Four rendering / consistency fixes. Two surfaced while authoring the new chartedroots.com [/guides/](https://chartedroots.com/guides/) section; the other two are a metadata-cache race in the Entity Profile pane and a wikilink-rendering leak in Book Builder PDF/ODT output. All non-data-loss.

## Highlights

- **Merge Wizard renders as the intended 4-column comparison table** ([#514](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/514)) - the component creates ~20 distinct `cr-merge-*` class names but no CSS file ever defined any of them. Layout fell back to default block flow, stacking each field / value / value / dropdown vertically instead of as a side-by-side comparison. New `styles/merge-wizard.css` wires up the side-by-side grid the component was always built to use, plus styling for the conflict dropdowns and preview panel. While in there: the People base's "Multiple marriages" view filter was checking `!isEmpty(spouses_all)` ("has at least one"), corrected to `length > 1`. Surfaced while authoring the [find-and-merge-duplicates guide](https://chartedroots.com/guides/research/find-and-merge-duplicates/).
- **People base's Spouse(s) column shows all spouses for users following the indexed-spouse pattern** ([#516](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/516)) - the column mapped directly to `note.spouse` only, so vaults using the recommended indexed pattern (`spouse1`, `spouse2`, etc.) saw an empty column even when multiple spouses were recorded. New `spouses_all` formula aggregates the flat `spouse` plus `spouse1` through `spouse5` and filters empties, so both single-spouse and multi-marriage households render correctly. Surfaced while authoring the [use-bases-for-data-analysis guide](https://chartedroots.com/guides/research/use-bases-for-data-analysis/).
- **Entity Profile pane stops showing "Could not load entity data" for newly-created entities** ([#519](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/519)) - a metadata-cache race in `EventService`, `SourceService`, and `ProofSummaryService`. Each service loaded its cache lazily and invalidated on writes, but didn't react to Obsidian indexing the new file later. After `createEvent` (or `createSource`, or `createProof`), a read between the write and Obsidian's metadata catch-up silently skipped the new file and marked the cache valid without it — invisible until something else invalidated. Each service now subscribes to `metadataCache.on('changed')` plus vault delete/rename, invalidating when a relevant `cr_type` file moves through the index. `ProofSummaryService` hoisted to a singleton on the plugin so the listeners persist for the plugin lifetime. Reported by @DigitalDreamn.
- **Report chapters in Book Builder no longer leak raw `[[wikilink]]` syntax into PDF/ODT output** ([#522](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/522)) - `BookGenerationService` already sanitized vault-note chapter content through a helper that strips wikilinks, frontmatter, and dynamic blocks, but the report chapter path stored the report generator's markdown into the chapter directly with no sanitization. Report generators (Family Group Sheet, Individual Summary, Source Summary, Sources by Role) emit raw `[[Name]]` syntax intentionally for in-vault rendering, which the static export renderer then displayed as literal text. `generateReportChapter` now applies the same sanitizer.

## Stability window

Window holds at 0.22.17, **one patch in**. None of the four fixes carried data-loss risk that would warrant a reset.

## Testing

22 new tests, suite total 589 (was 567 at the start of the cycle, across 49 suites). Full coverage breakdown in the [CHANGELOG](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/0.22.18/CHANGELOG.md#02218---2026-05-03).

## Install

- **BRAT users:** automatic update.
- **Manual install:** download the latest `main.js`, `styles.css`, and `manifest.json` from this release into your vault's `.obsidian/plugins/charted-roots/` folder, then reload Obsidian.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.18

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
Three fixes addressing wikilink ambiguity and silent data destruction in the Edit Person modal, plus a discoverability change that surfaces the per-fact source citations UI by default. **Closes a `data-loss` bug** where opening + saving Edit Person on a person with existing `sourced_*` attributions silently wiped them.

## Highlights

- **Event creation no longer mis-links persons that share a display name** ([#510](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/510)) - when two people had the same `name` property but different filenames, creating an event for one of them silently linked to whichever file Obsidian's metadataCache picked first. The wikilink helpers now disambiguate via the picker's file reference, producing `[[Harold James 2|Harold James]]` instead of the ambiguous `[[Harold James]]`. Reported by @doctorwodka.
- **Edit Person stops wiping per-fact source attributions** ([#512](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/512), `data-loss`) - opening + saving on a person with existing `sourced_birth_date` / `sourced_marriage_*` / etc. properties used to silently delete all of them. The load path now correctly populates `sourcedFacts` from frontmatter so the round-trip is non-destructive. Bug only fired when `trackFactSourcing` was explicitly enabled.
- **Spouse `marriage_location` stops degrading to nested-array YAML** ([#513](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/513)) - the writer's extra JS-string quotes caused `processFrontMatter` to reshape the value into `[["Basename"]]` block-list form. Fix drops the embedded quotes; new saves emit canonical wikilinks, and existing degraded data self-heals on the next save.
- **Per-fact source citations are now discoverable by default** ([#511](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/511)) - the existing UI in Edit Person (collapsible "Fact-level source citations" section, chip-based attached sources, dedup, source-picker integration) was complete but hidden behind an off-by-default setting buried under Advanced → Research tools. The toggle moves to a new top-level **Research** section in Settings, positioned between Places and Property & value aliases, and defaults to on for new vaults. Existing vaults keep whatever preference you'd set.

## Stability window

This release **resets** the stability window to 0.22.17, first reset in thirteen patches (window had been anchored to 0.22.4 since 2026-04-23). The reset is unavoidable given the data-loss severity of #512.

## Testing

34 new tests, suite total 567 (was 533 at the start of the cycle). Full coverage breakdown in the [CHANGELOG](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/0.22.17/CHANGELOG.md#02217---2026-05-01) and the [Release History wiki page](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/wiki/Release-History#v02217-round-up-edit-person-hardening-and-a-citation-surface-promotion-v02217).

## Install

- **BRAT users:** automatic update.
- **Manual install:** download the latest `main.js`, `styles.css`, and `manifest.json` from this release into your vault's `.obsidian/plugins/charted-roots/` folder, then reload Obsidian.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.17

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
Five fixes refining Universe handling and marriage popups across the modal and map surfaces. Cleanup pass on the loose ends from 0.22.15's universe rename arc, plus a parity fix on marriage popups and a long-standing inconsistency in how event / source / proof-summary filenames were generated. All non-data-loss; stability window stays anchored to 0.22.4 (twelve patches without a reset).

## Universes

- **Edit Person dropdown includes the universe note's typed name** ([#505](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/505)). The right-click Edit Person flow merged universes from only two sources, omitting the universe-note source that the Control Center's dropdown already uses. After a rename where sanitization stripped chars from the basename (`Star Wars (AU)` typed -> `Star Wars AU` basename) the typed name had no path into the dropdown and users saw only the basename. Both call sites now go through a shared `mergeUniverseList` helper so the three-source merge can't drift apart again.

- **Universe field always available in Edit Event modal** ([#507](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/507)). Universe was bundled inside the conditional that gates the `Canonical event` toggle on narrative event types, so Vital (birth/marriage/death/burial) and Life (residence/immigration/etc.) events had no way to set their universe via the modal. Pulled Universe out into its own always-rendered Setting; `isCanonical` stays narrative-only since canon/non-canon is storytelling-specific. The Worldbuilding section that wraps `isCanonical` is now also reactive. Picking a narrative type from the dropdown after opening the modal reveals the section without needing to save and reopen.

## Map view

- **Static map marker popup pairs the partner's age alongside the focal person's** ([#508](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/508)). The journey-mode rich popup gained partner age in 0.22.15 (#504), but the non-journey marker popup didn't. `buildMarkers` now resolves the spouse via `marriage.spouseId` and threads `spouseBirthDate` onto marriage markers; the popup's `with X` line appends `(age N)` to the partner's entry. Format ends up `with Beru Whitesun (age 38)` alongside the existing focal-side `Marriage: 19 BBY (age 45)`.

## Data quality

- **Event / Source / Proof Summary filenames preserve accented characters and casing** ([#509](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/509)). Three services and the life-events migration helper each had their own private `slugify` running an aggressive `[^a-z0-9]+ -> -` regex that destroyed accented chars, lowercased everything, and turned spaces into hyphens. `Birth of Padmé Naberrie` became `birth-of-padm-naberrie.md`. Person notes go through `sanitizeName` which preserves all of that, so the same character was treated inconsistently across entity types. New `sanitizeFilename` helper wraps `sanitizeName` with a length cap and replaces all four call sites. Existing files keep their old slugified names; only new files going forward use the preserved format.

## Tests

38 new tests across five files (`tests/universe-cache-after-rename.test.ts`, `tests/sanitize-filename.test.ts`, `tests/event-narrative-type.test.ts`, `tests/marriage-marker-spouse-birth.test.ts`, plus updates to `tests/migration-helpers.test.ts`). Total suite 533 across 41 files.

## Reporters

@DigitalDreamn: for the verification thread that surfaced #505 / #507 / #508 / #509 across multiple cycles, and for empirical evidence in #506 about which characters are actually filesystem- and wikilink-safe. Thanks.

## Stability window

Anchored to 0.22.4 (2026-04-23). Twelve patches without a reset. Soak window closes ~2026-05-14. About 14 days remaining.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.16

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
Three fixes that close the loose ends from 0.22.14's universe rename arc, plus a small enhancement to the marriage popup. All non-data-loss; stability window stays anchored to 0.22.4 (eleven patches without a reset).

## Universes

- **Rename cascade rewrites map notes alongside other entity types** ([#503](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/503)). 0.22.14's cascade scope was `person | place | event | organization`: map notes (`cr_type: map`) carry a `universe:` field too, but were silently skipped. After a rename the map kept pointing at the old universe and its marker filter no longer matched cascaded entities. `cr_type: map` joins the cascade so map notes ride along with the rest.

- **Dynamic universe code blocks survive name<->basename divergence** ([#503](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/503)). The cascade writes the file basename to entity `universe:` fields, but the `charted-roots-universe-people` / `places` / `events` / `organizations` / `maps` blocks compared against the universe note's frontmatter `name`. When `sanitizeName` strips characters during the file rename (parens / brackets / quotes, `"The Dying Earth (Vance)"` -> basename `"The Dying Earth Vance"`), the basename diverges from the typed name and the lookup silently returned `"No entities found for this universe"`. The new alias-aware lookup matches against any of the universe note's identifiers (basename, frontmatter `name`, or `cr_id`) so it survives whichever form an entity's `universe:` field happens to hold.

## Map view

- **Map filter resolves universe cr_id to basename, and re-syncs when a map note changes** ([#503](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/503)). Two related issues: (1) `resolveUniverseFilterValue` returned the universe note's `name` when given a cr_id, so after a rename like `"The Dying Earth"` -> `"The Dying Earth (Vance)"` the resolved filter no longer matched cascaded places / people on basename `"The Dying Earth Vance"` and every marker disappeared. Resolver now returns the basename. (2) The controller's in-memory `mapConfigs` cache stayed stale after an Edit Map save, so refresh re-queried with the old universe filter. Map view now reloads `mapConfigs` and re-syncs `filters.universe` whenever a `cr_type: map` note's metadata changes.

- **Marriage popup pairs the partner's age alongside the focal person's** ([#504](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/504)). Journey-mode marriage popups now show both ages (`Age: 27 years old` / `Partner's age: 24 years old`) paired via the same `DateService.calculateAge` path as the focal age so fictional eras work correctly. Legacy flat marriages without `spouseId` quietly omit the row. Suggested by @DigitalDreamn during #501 verification.

## Tests
