20 new tests across four files (`tests/universe-rename-cascade.test.ts`, `tests/universe-entities-by-file.test.ts`, `tests/resolve-universe-filter.test.ts`, `tests/journey-marriage-spouse-birth.test.ts`). Total suite 500 across 37 files.

## Reporters

@DigitalDreamn - for the 0.22.14 verification thread that surfaced #503 and the #501 verification observation that prompted #504. Thanks!

## Stability window

Anchored to 0.22.4 (2026-04-23). Eleven patches without a reset. Soak window closes ~2026-05-14.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.15

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
Three fixes plus a Cleanup Wizard step. Closes the Universe rename direction end-to-end and pairs up marriage markers between spouses.

## Universes

- **Edit Universe modal renames the file when the name property changes** ([#488](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/488) Part 3) - Part 2's cascade only fired on file renames, so editing the name via the modal silently left entities pointing at the old name. The modal now triggers a real file rename, and the cascade fires automatically. Closes the rename direction end-to-end (Part 1: dropdown sourcing > Part 2: cascade > Part 3: modal trigger).

## Map view

- **Marriages between two spouses render as one combined marker with both partner names** ([#501](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/501)) - Owen+Beru's marriage at Tatooine used to produce two stacked markers; now it's one marker with both names in the popup. Sibling to [#493](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/493), but for marriages (which live on each spouse's frontmatter rather than as `cr_type: event` notes). Journey-mode popup also gains a `Partner` row for marriage waypoints.

## Data quality

- **Cleanup Wizard step: add cr_id to place notes** ([#502](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/502)) - Place notes lacking `cr_id` are silently excluded from the place graph (by-name lookups, dropdown options, map markers don't see them). New step 15 surfaces them and offers a "Generate cr_id" fix. Layer 2 of the [#471](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/471) plan; complements the diagnostic warning shipped in 0.22.9.

## Tests

- **Fence `FamilyGraphService.extractPersonNode` non-person rejection** ([#489](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/489) follow-up) - 6 tests covering the inclusion check from 0.22.11. Custom `cr_type` values like "hex" / "faction" no longer fall through and get coerced into people.

---

**Reporters:** @DigitalDreamn surfaced #488 Part 3 (during 0.22.13's Part 2 verification) and the marriage-marker observations that became #501 (during #493 / #498 verification).

**Tests:** 14 new across the cycle, suite total 480.

**Stability window:** still anchored to 0.22.4 (2026-04-23 -> ~2026-05-14). Tenth patch in the 0.22.x run without a reset; all four changes are non-data-loss. ~14 days of soak window left.

**Install via BRAT:** `banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots`
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.14

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
Six fixes: a map-coverage cluster plus two follow-ups to 0.22.12.

## Map view

- **Multi-spouse marriages on the map** ([#498](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/498)) - journey mode and marker layer read every indexed `spouseN_marriage_*` slot, not just the legacy flat `marriage_*` fields. People with multiple spouses no longer have their marriages silently dropped from the map.
- **Multi-participant events render as one marker** ([#493](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/493)) - a `cr_type: event` note referenced by multiple people now surfaces a single combined marker with all participants listed in the popup, instead of N stacked markers at the same place.
- **Events at child places render via parent coordinates** ([#494](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/494)) - a child place with `parent_place: [[Parent]]` and no own coords now inherits the nearest ancestor's positioning, so events at "Lars Homestead" (or "Stockholm" without coords) appear at Tatooine / Sweden rather than disappearing.
- **Custom event labels survive in journey mode** ([#499](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/499)) - the rich popup and play-control label now show the original event type (e.g., `Backstory`) for custom events instead of the generic `Custom`. Sibling fix to [#466](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/466)'s static-marker fix.

## Modals

- **Coordinate inputs in the Place modal** ([#496](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/496) follow-up) - X / Y and Lat / Long now live inside a single Setting row as compound controls, sidestepping an Obsidian default `:first-child` / `:last-child` padding rule that was misaligning the previous two-setting-item layout. Look up button stacks below the lat / long inputs.

## Relationships

- **Marriage detail mirroring across mixed spouse-format states** ([#481](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/481) follow-up). When one partner uses the indexed `spouseN:` format and the other is still on the legacy flat `spouse:` / `spouse_id:` shape, the bidirectional linker now atomically promotes the flat partner to indexed before mirroring marriage details, instead of bailing out silently.

---

**Reporters:** @DigitalDreamn diagnosed and reported #498, #494, #499, the #481 follow-up, and the #496 follow-up testing; @doctorwodka closed out #491 verification (the tab labels + merge step from 0.22.12).

**Tests:** 30 new, suite total 466.

**Stability window:** still anchored to 0.22.4 (2026-04-23 > ~2026-05-14). Ninth patch in the 0.22.x run without a reset; all six changes are non-data-loss.

**Install via BRAT:** `banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots`
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.13

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
Eleven fixes: three new bugs, eight follow-ups / cross-cutting cleanups. Largest 0.22.x patch, all non-data-loss; stability window stays anchored to 0.22.4 (eight patches without a reset). Big themes:

- **Marriage info now mirrors between spouses** ([#481](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/481)). Filling marriage_date / marriage_location on one partner used to leave the other partner with just a spouse link and no marriage details. The bidirectional linker now mirrors the full marriage-detail field set the deletion handler already enumerated, on both initial fill and updates.
- **Universe rename cascades end-to-end** ([#488](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/488) Part 2). Part 1 in 0.22.11 fixed the Edit Person dropdown; Part 2 closes the loop on the actual data (`universe:` plain-string references on people / places / events / organizations rewrite to the new name on rename).
- **Journey mode no longer drops popups silently**. The dedup that prevented pixel-coord places from collapsing onto (0,0) was over-aggressive across event types; a custom event at the same place as a death would swallow the death popup ([#487](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/487)). Now distinct event types at the same place both survive.
- **Map UX polish.** Path label outline setting for legibility on colorful image-map backgrounds ([#483](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/483)), journey playback dwell time reframed as an explicit dwell-time selector with second labels so popups are actually readable ([#486](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/486)), zoom-aware label suppression at low zoom ([#482](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/482)), journey playback preserves the user's zoom between steps, burial waypoints honor per-map visibility filters consistently with the other waypoint types.

Plus a `Manage memberships` affordance on the person side so editing org membership doesn't require navigating to the org first ([#490](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/490)), Person Picker fixes that surface user-named collections as tab labels and merge components sharing a `group_name` ([#491](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/491) partial), Pixel coordinates section X / Y row alignment in the Place modal ([#496](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/496)), and the Fan chart PDF report option hidden until the renderer actually produces a fan chart ([#492](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/492)).

Reporter mix: @DigitalDreamn for #481, #487, #488, #496, plus the diagnostic that turned #487 from a doc-gap into a real code fix; @doctorwodka for #483, #486, the Collections-tab pushback that drove #491.

### Added

- Person picker uses collection names for tab labels and merges components sharing a `group_name` (#491 partial; broader Collections rework post-1.0)
- "Manage memberships..." affordance on the person context menu (#490)
- Map path label outline setting (None / White / Black) for legibility on colorful or dark backgrounds (#483)

### Changed

- Journey playback popup dwell time is now configurable with explicit second labels (`2s` / `4s` / `6s` / `10s`, default `4s`); semantics: dwell time per step, not total step duration (#486)
- Journey playback preserves the user's zoom level between steps

### Fixed

- Universe note rename cascades to `universe:` references on people / places / events / organizations (#488 Part 2)
- Marriage detail fields (`spouseN_marriage_date` / `_marriage_location` / `_marriage_location_id` / `_marriage_status` / `_divorce_date`) propagate bidirectionally between spouse notes (#481)
- Journey waypoint dedup key includes event type so death popups survive when a custom event sits at the same place (#487)
- Burial waypoints honor the per-map visibility filter consistently with the other waypoint types (#487 sibling)
- Pixel coordinates X and Y rows align vertically in the Create / Edit Place modal (#496)
- Map path labels hide instead of overflowing when zoomed out (#482)
- Fan chart PDF report option hidden from the catalog until a real fan-layout renderer is implemented (#492)

### Stability and tests

- 8th patch in the 0.22.x stability run; window still anchored to 0.22.4
- 436 tests passing, 14 new for this release covering the eventType dedup key (#487) and the FamilyComponent merge logic (#491)
- Build clean; type-check carries the existing TS-cleanup backlog (no new errors introduced this cycle)

### Install via BRAT

`banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots`
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.12

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
Six fixes: three follow-ups to recent patches plus three new bugs. All non-data-loss; stability window unchanged. Notable: the #442 follow-up effectively closes [#478](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/478) by removing its trigger condition, and #485 closes the lat/lng-only-on-pixel-CRS cluster that ran across [#448](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/448) / [#474](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/474).

## Fixed

### Map view

- **Map path labels render upright on multi-waypoint paths via single-segment label-host** ([#472](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/472) follow-up): The 0.22.10 longest-segment-direction heuristic still left labels upside-down on some multi-waypoint paths (most visibly when two characters' journey paths shared the same visual segment in opposite traversal directions). Root cause: leaflet-textpath repeats the label along the entire polyline path and applies a single global 180° rotation, so a path that bends in different directions can't be made upright everywhere by a single rotation. Fixed by hosting labels on a separate invisible polyline covering only the longest screen-space segment of the source path. The visible polyline keeps its full multi-waypoint shape; the label renders once per polyline with the correct flip; overlapping segments from different polylines each get their own correctly-oriented label.
- **Journey mode no longer briefly frames the bottom-left corner of `CRS.Simple` maps before flying to the first waypoint** ([#485](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/485)): `MapView.applyJourneyFilter`'s initial `fitBounds` call read `marker.lat` / `marker.lng` only. Pixel-coord places default those to 0, so on a custom image map the bounds collapsed around `(0, 0)` — the bottom-left corner (and the camera framed there briefly before flying to the actual first waypoint). Third site in the #448 / #474 cluster; all three journey-mode paths now use pixel coordinates correctly on `CRS.Simple` maps.

### Relationships

- **Person delete cleanup sweeps wikilink fields and handles single-relationship scalars** ([#442](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/442) follow-up): Two gaps in the original 0.22.7 cleanup. (1) Wikilink-bearing relationship fields (`father`, `children`, `step_child`, etc.) were not swept. The original implementation delegated to Obsidian's wikilink rewriting, but Obsidian only rewrites on rename, not on delete. Deleted persons left broken `[[placeholder]]` links visible in the properties pane. (2) The "array" relationship fields are de-facto polymorphic: YAML serializers emit a scalar string for a single element and an array for multiple, but the original cleanup's array-only branches silently skipped the scalar form, so deleting an only-child / only-step-child / only-spouse left the parent's `*_id` arrays pointing at the dead cr_id. Fix extends the planner with a parallel wikilink-sweep branch (gated on the deleted file's basename) and converts every "array" handler to accept both shapes. Wikilink matching handles path-prefixed, display-aliased, heading-anchored, and block-anchored forms; case-insensitive. **Effectively closes [#478](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/478)** by removing its trigger (broken wikilinks no longer linger past delete to be tripped on by the next save).

### Timeline

- **Negative birth years preserve sign when followed by a letter suffix** ([#476](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/476) follow-up): The 0.22.10 fix worked for `-90` but not for `DE -5740ish`. The standalone-negative regex required a trailing `\b` (word boundary) which fires correctly between digit and whitespace / punctuation but NOT between digit and letter — so suffixed forms fell through to the bare-digits fallback that strips the sign. Replaced the trailing `\b` with a `(?=$|[^0-9])` lookahead so the negative-detection branch matches against any non-digit terminator. Also removed a duplicate `extractYear` implementation in the relationships renderer (a pre-existing bare `\b(\d{4})\b` regex that pre-dated #476 entirely, same class of bug on a different surface) and routed both call sites through the shared service helper.

### Universes

- **Edit Person modal's Universe dropdown reflects renamed and newly-created Universe notes** ([#488](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/488) Part 1): The dropdown was sourced from the distinct `universe:` field values across person and place notes (not from the actual Universe notes in the universes folder). Renaming a Universe note left the dropdown showing the old name because no person/place file had been updated to reference the new name yet. Fix unions `UniverseService.getAllUniverses()` (the universe-notes folder) into the cached list alongside the existing graph extractions, so renamed and newly-created Universe notes appear immediately. Doesn't address the deeper rename-cascade question (referencing notes still hold the old name in their `universe:` field until a future bidirectional rename-handler updates them). Tracked as #488 Part 2.

### Person notes

- **Notes with custom non-person `cr_type` values no longer appear in the Person notes view** ([#489](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/489)): A note with `cr_type: hex` (or any user-defined type the plugin doesn't know about) was being treated as a person and listed in the control center's Person notes browser. `FamilyGraphService.extractPersonNode` ran an exclusion list. Checking for the known non-person types (source, event, place, organization, proof_summary, universe, citation) and treating any `cr_id`-bearing note that didn't match as a person. User-defined custom types like `hex` or `faction` fell through every exclusion and got coerced into people. Fix adds an explicit `isPersonNote` inclusion check, which already handles the unknown-`cr_type` case correctly while preserving the legacy "cr_id with no `cr_type` → treat as person" behavior for older vaults.

## Stability

Stability window: 2026-04-23 -> ~2026-05-14, **still anchored to 0.22.4** (zero resets across 0.22.5 -> 0.22.11). Seven patches in this run, twenty-nine issues addressed.

## Tests

**422 tests** passing across 26 suites. 24 new tests in 0.22.11 cover scalar/array shapes for the cleanup planner, wikilink format normalization, the suffixed negative-year regex, and combined sweep cases.

## Reporters

@DigitalDreamn for #472 follow-up, #442 follow-up, #485 (via #474), and #488 Part 1; @doctorwodka for #476 follow-up; @Lemmeron for #489.

---

**Install via BRAT:** Add `banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots` and pin the version to `0.22.11`.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.11

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
Five fixes: three follow-ups to recent work plus two new bugs that surfaced during this week's website-capture sessions. All non-data-loss; stability window unchanged. Notable: one of the fixes (#477) was a regression introduced by 0.22.9's #472 fix and caught + fixed within 24 hours.

## Fixed

### Map view

- **Map path label flip decision now segment-aware** ([#472](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/472) follow-up). The 0.22.9 fix introduced a `shouldFlipPathLabel` helper that picked between leaflet-textpath's `'flip'` and `'auto'` modes based on the path's screen-space chord (start point -> end point). That helped most cases but missed paths where the chord direction disagrees with the segment where the label actually renders; leaflet-textpath places labels along the path itself, not along the chord, so multi-waypoint paths could have a chord pointing one way and a visible label segment pointing the other. Helper now finds the longest segment in screen-space and uses *its* direction; falls back to chord behavior naturally for 2-point paths since the only segment is the chord. Also defensively flattens `getLatLngs()` to handle the `LatLng[][]` multi-polyline case. Reported by @DigitalDreamn after re-verifying 0.22.9 on her Star Wars galaxy map.

- **Console error spam from map path labels** ([#477](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/477)). The 0.22.9 path-label fix passed `orientation: 'auto'` to leaflet-textpath as the no-flip branch, on the assumption that `'auto'` meant "follow path direction naturally." But leaflet-textpath@1.3.0 only recognizes `'flip'` (180°), `'perpendicular'` (90°), and numeric rotations. Any other value falls through and is injected literally into the SVG `transform` attribute. The result was `transform="rotate(auto cx cy)"`; invalid SVG syntax, which the browser rejects with one console error per path label rendered. Fixed by omitting the `orientation` key entirely when no flip is needed; leaflet-textpath then skips its rotation block and the text follows the path geometry naturally (the behavior we'd assumed `'auto'` was producing). 0.22.9 regression caught + fixed within 24 hours. Reported by @DigitalDreamn.

### Timeline

- **Negative birth years no longer flip positive on the timeline block** ([#476](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/476)). `DynamicContentService.extractYear` handled four sign-bearing cases (BCE/BC suffix, ISO negative like `-0011-01-15`, AD/CE suffix, standard 4-digit years) but had no path for custom-era formats with an explicit standalone minus sign (for example, `DE -5740`). The 4-digit-year regex used `\b` (word boundary) which ate the leading minus, dropping the sign and rendering the year as positive on the timeline. Added a new branch that captures a digit run preceded by a standalone minus sign while excluding ISO date separators (where the hyphen is between digits). 5 new regression tests in `tests/date-helpers.test.ts`. Reported by @doctorwodka.

### Calendar View

- **Calendar View accepts era-suffixed years and round-trips them** ([#480](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/480)). The Calendar View's year input was a bare `<input type="number">` constrained to `year > 0 && year < 10000`, with the value rendered as plain `String(this.currentYear)` (no era awareness, no descending-era support). Sibling to [#453](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/453)'s map time slider work (the Calendar View was one more parallel year-rendering area that hadn't been routed through `DateService`). Year input is now `type="text"` and routes through `DateService.parseDate`, accepting era-suffixed strings ("82 BBY", "1499 ABY", "30 AC"), ISO dates, and bare signed integers. When the parser resolves a fictional date, the system's universe is recorded so subsequent renders (including those after month-boundary rollovers) format via `formatCanonicalYear` for round-trip era display. Persisted state gains a `yearUniverse` field so era context survives view reopens. The `year > 0` constraint is dropped. Surfaced during the Calendar View capture session.

### Modals

- **Create / Edit Place modal CSS overflow and coord-input width** ([#459](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/459) follow-up). Two CSS issues left over from the 0.22.8 fix. (1) The `flex-shrink: 0` rule on the modal's info column made it refuse to yield space when descriptions were long. (On narrower viewports the description text held its full natural width and pushed the 220px control column past the modal's right edge, hiding inputs under the scroll bar.) Dropped `flex-shrink: 0` so the info column shrinks (descriptions wrap to multiple lines) while keeping `min-width: 120px` for readability. (2) The 220px input-width rule was cascading into the Latitude/Longitude inputs, which have their own `width: 100%` rule but were losing on specificity. Bumped the coord rule's specificity so the rules tie and source order tilts the coord rule to win (Lat/Long inputs fill their respective halves of the row again). Reported by @DigitalDreamn after re-verifying 0.22.8.

---

**Stability window:** anchored to 0.22.4 since 2026-04-23. v0.22.10 is non-data-loss across all five fixes, so the window is unchanged — soak target ~2026-05-14.

**Tests:** 395 passing (5 new for the #476 standalone-negative-year cases; the other four fixes are UI-only and ship without new test coverage).

**Reporters:** big thanks to @DigitalDreamn for the #459 follow-up, #472 follow-up, and #477 reports. Three of five fixes from her continued vault testing on the Lars / Star Wars fixture. @doctorwodka for #476 from her DE-format vault. #480 self-reported during the Calendar View website-capture session.

**Install via BRAT:** add `banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots`, then update.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.10

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
Five fixes across map view, timeline, and diagnostics. All non-data-loss, all targeted polish on top of 0.22.8. Continues the post-0.22.4 stability window without resetting it.

## Fixed

### Map view

- **Journey-mode camera flies to pixel-coord waypoints on CRS.Simple image maps** ([#474](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/474)). On image-based maps, the camera fly-to and rich-popup placement were landing at the bottom-left corner — pixel-coord places default lat/lng to `0`, and the camera path was lat/lng-only. Fixed by routing through `[pixelY, pixelX]` when on pixel CRS. Same coord-system-aware pattern as [#448](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/448), which fixed the journey-path *build* path; this fixes the camera-*follow* path.
- **Map path labels read consistently upright across CRS.Simple maps and diagonal lines** ([#472](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/472)). leaflet-textpath's `'flip'` mode picks rotation directly from latlng coords and produced inconsistent results on diagonal lines and image maps (some labels rendered upright, others upside-down on the same map). Path direction is now computed in screen-space (after CRS projection) before deciding flip-vs-auto.
- **Map time slider derives its year range from data and renders era-formatted labels** ([#453](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/453)). The slider was hardcoded to a 1800–2000 real-world span; on fictional-era universes the slider's range never intersected the data. Now derives min/max from `MapData.yearRange` and renders labels via a new `DateService.formatCanonicalYear` helper ("82 BBY", "5 ABY"). **Eighth and final site of the DateService-bypass cluster**, which has run across the 0.22.5 → 0.22.9 cycle (data-quality validator, map popup ages, data-quality date inconsistencies, timeline ages, map marker popups, map year extraction, statistics dashboard, and now the time slider).

### Timeline

- **Older siblings' births no longer appear before the focal person's birth** ([#469](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/469)). The sibling-births block in `gatherFamilyEvents` had no reality-window guard for events predating the focal person's birth. Padmé Naberrie's timeline showed her older sister Sola's birth (50 BBY) above Padmé's own birth (46 BBY). Added a symmetric `isEventBeforeFocalBirth` helper mirroring [#457](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/457)'s `isEventAfterFocalDeath`. Same-year siblings (twins) still surface; the guard fires only on unambiguous before-focal-birth via canonical-year comparison, so fictional descending eras compare correctly. Third area in the **reality-window cluster** alongside [#456](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/456) and [#457](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/457).

### Diagnostics

- **Place notes silently excluded from the place graph now log a warning** ([#471](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/471)). `PlaceGraphService.extractPlaceNode` early-returns when a place-shaped note lacks a `cr_id`, so such notes never enter the place cache and aren't visible to by-name lookups, modal dropdowns, or map markers. The exclusion was completely silent. No log, no UI area, no data-quality flag. Added a `warn`-level log including the file path so the exclusion is now discoverable from the dev console. A follow-up data-quality check that surfaces and offers to fix missing-`cr_id` places is queued for a later cycle.

---

**Stability window:** anchored to 0.22.4 since 2026-04-23. v0.22.9 is non-data-loss across all five fixes, so the window is unchanged, soak target ~2026-05-14 if nothing critical comes up.

**Tests:** 390 passing (4 new tests for the #469 reality-window cases; #474 verified manually since it requires Leaflet map mocking out of scope for the current harness).

**Reporters:** big thanks to @DigitalDreamn for surfacing #453, #469, #472, and #474 across continued testing on her Lars / Star Wars fixture, and for the #464 investigation that produced #471.

**Install via BRAT:** add `banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots`, then update.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.9

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description: Round-up release: nine fixes addressing eleven issues across the map view, timeline, statistics dashboard, and Create Place modal. Biggest patch yet for 0.22.x. All non-data-loss; the stability window running since v0.22.4 (2026-04-23 -> ~2026-05-14) continues unchanged. Driven primarily by @DigitalDreamn's continued vault testing on the Lars / Star Wars fixture, with @doctorwodka contributing the marriage-statistics report. ## Fixed - **Statistics Dashboard date-inconsistency counter respects fictional eras** ([#437](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/437) follow-up) - the 0.22.6 fix routed `data-quality.ts`'s `parseYear` through `DateService`, but the Statistics Dashboard has a parallel "Date inconsistencies" counter inside `StatisticsService` with its own `extractYear` regex that had no era awareness. For BBY descending eras, `1045 BBY` read as 1045 and `1042 BBY` as 1042, and the naive `birthYear > deathYear` check fired on coherent lifespans. `extractYear` now defers to `DateService.parseDate` first; same fix shape as #437 / #454. Real-world dates unchanged. - **Map year extraction respects fictional eras** ([#454](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/454)) - `MapDataService.extractYear` required a 4-digit numeric year, so fictional-era timestamps like `82 BBY` / `41 BBY` parsed as `undefined`. Broke chronological sort on fictional-era timelines, dropped events from year-range filters, and contributed to the "no journey path built" symptom on fictional-calendar maps before [#448](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/448) was identified. Now defers to `DateService.parseDate` first. - **Marriage statistics respect the fictional-dates age cap** ([#458](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/458)) - two marriage-stat sites (age at first marriage in Marriage Patterns, marriage duration in Longest Marriages) had a hardcoded `<= 80` upper bound that bypassed the `maxAge` getter the rest of the engine uses. With fictional dates enabled, the cap was supposed to lift to `Infinity` for long-lived characters but stayed at 80, silently dropping any marriage age over that threshold. Both sites now defer to `this.maxAge`. - **Map journey paths build correctly for pixel-coord places** ([#448](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/448)) - on custom image maps, every place uses pixel coordinates and has no lat/lng, but `buildJourneyPaths` deduped consecutive waypoints by lat/lng only. Every pixel-coord waypoint shared `(0, 0)`, the dedup collapsed all of them into one entry, and the "≥ 2 unique waypoints" check failed silently. New `journeyWaypointDedupKey` prefers `placeId` when available; falls back to a composite of both coordinate systems. - **Custom-relationships overlay arcs paint on top of family-link layer in the typical case** ([#450](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/450)) - overlay arcs were intentionally painted under family links to protect against heavy-stack occlusion ([#386](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/386)), but that hid the typical case (1-3 arcs per focal person) behind family links. Now flips: paint on top by default, fall back to under-links only when an endpoint pair has 3+ stacked arcs (preserves the [#386](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/386) guarantee). - **Timeline filters relative events outside the focal person's reality window** ([#456](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/456), [#457](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/457)) - two related leaks where the timeline surfaced events that didn't fit the focal person's lived experience. Step-siblings' births appeared on each other's timelines because the sibling-iteration walked each parent's `childrenCrIds` without distinguishing biological from step-children ([#456](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/456)). Spouse deaths surfaced on the survivor's timeline even when the survivor pre-deceased the spouse ([#457](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/457)). Paired fix: step-sibling filter mirroring [#441](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/441), plus a new `isEventAfterFocalDeath` guard via `DateService.getCanonicalYear`. Audit covered parent deaths in the same pass. - **Person-delete cleanup now sweeps the `step_child_id` field on stepparents' notes** ([#442](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/442) follow-up) - the cleanup planner shipped in 0.22.7 listed `stepchild_id` (no underscore), but the bidirectional-linker writes `step_child_id` (underscore between `step` and `child`). Two parallel hardcoded lists never matched; deleting a person who was a stepchild left their cr_id stranded in the stepparent's `step_child_id` array. Renamed the entry to match the field the rest of the plugin actually writes. - **Create Place modal recognizes parents created earlier in the same session and writes their cr_id** ([#463](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/463), [#464](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/464)) - two bundled bugs sharing a stale `placeGraph` cache. `ensureCacheLoaded` only loads when the cache is empty, so newly-created place notes weren't visible to subsequent Create Place modals in the same session. Symptoms: typing an existing parent's name produced a spurious "doesn't exist" auto-create prompt ([#464](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/464)); saving anyway wrote only the wikilink without the `parent_place_id` companion ([#463](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/463)). Two-part fix: refresh the cache on modal open; resolve typed parent names to cr_ids in `checkForMissingParent` before the auto-create branch fires. - **Map popups for custom (`cr_type: event`) markers surface the original event type** ([#466](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/466)) - when an event's `event_type` didn't match a built-in `MarkerType` (e.g., `backstory`), it was resolved to `custom` and the popup rendered the literal `Custom: (age N)`, dropping the category context. `LifeEvent` and `MapMarker` now carry `customLabel` populated from the raw event type or event-note's title; popup uses it when type resolves to `custom`. - **Custom event marker color is now visually distinct from death event markers** ([#465](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/465)) - default `customMarkerColor` was pink (`#ec4899`), close enough to death red (`#ef4444`) that scanning the map for one type vs. the other was harder than it should be. Changed to yellow (`#eab308`). - **Create Place modal text inputs render at consistent widths** ([#459](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/459)) - Universe and Parent place text inputs were narrower than Name, Aliases, and Collection in the same modal. Long descriptions on those rows pushed the info column wider and the existing `flex-shrink: 0` rule prevented yielding space back. Set a fixed 220px width on text inputs. - **Wikipedia clipper template renders infobox photos correctly in Obsidian** ([#440](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/440)) - the `wikipedia-biography-basic.json` template preserved infobox HTML with protocol-relative image URLs (``) which Obsidian's `app://` renderer can't resolve. Added a `replace` filter that rewrites `="//` to `="https://`. Re-import the template to pick up the fix; not part of the versioned plugin code, ships via `docs/clipper-templates/`. ## Tests 376 tests pass (was 343 at start of cycle, +33 across new test files): `tests/map-data-service-extract-year.test.ts` (10), `tests/statistics-marriage-age-cap.test.ts` (6), `tests/timeline-reality-window.test.ts` (8), `tests/map-popup-custom-label.test.ts` (3), `tests/statistics-extract-year-fictional.test.ts` (6). ## Reporters - @DigitalDreamn - eight of the eleven fixes from continued Lars / Star Wars vault testing. - @doctorwodka - [#458](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/458) (marriage stats age cap). ## Stability window No reset. All eleven fixes are non-data-loss. Window continues from 0.22.4's start: 2026-04-23 -> ~2026-05-14. --- Full changelog: [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md) Wiki narrative: [Release-History](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/wiki/Release-History)
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.8

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
Round-up release: seven changes across the map view, timeline, profile pane, and a new universe -> calendar link. Six follow-ups from @DigitalDreamn's testing on their Star Wars / Lars-family vault, plus the #432 Phase 1 universe-calendar wiring and a default flip. All non-data-loss; the stability window running since v0.22.4 (2026-04-23 -> ~2026-05-14) continues unchanged.

## Added

- **Universe wizard and Edit Universe modal can link a default calendar** ([#432](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/432) Phase 1) - three-way picker (None / Built-in / Custom) replacing the binary toggle, with slug-match preselect when the universe name matches a built-in (Star Wars -> Galactic Standard, Middle-earth -> Middle-earth Calendar). Edit Universe modal gets a matching Calendar field; the Universes tab shows "Default: <name>" under the entity counts. Layered contract per [the planning doc](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/docs/planning/universe-calendar-linking.md): the parser is unchanged at this phase, just the link is recorded. Existing universes without `default_calendar` continue to behave as before — no migration. Phase 2 (parser-side reading + bare-year inference) deferred.

## Changed

- **Spouse deaths now appear on the surviving spouse's timeline by default** ([#447](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/447)) - `timelineShowSpouseDeaths` flipped from `false` to `true`. The toggle stays in Settings -> Charted Roots -> Timeline for opt-out. Existing users who haven't customized the setting will start seeing spouse deaths on surviving spouses' timelines after upgrading.

## Fixed

- **Deleted persons' cr_ids are removed from referencing notes' relationship arrays** ([#442](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/442)) - Obsidian rewrites wikilinks on delete, but the parallel `*_id` arrays carrying cr_id strings were left orphaned. Charted Roots now registers a `metadataCache.on('deleted')` handler that scans every other person note and removes the deleted cr_id from canonical fields (parents, step-parents, adoptive parents, adopted children, stepchildren, partners, children, indexed-spouse slots) plus user-aliased equivalents. Existing vault-wide orphans can still be cleaned via the data-quality "Remove orphaned cr_id references" tool.

- **Stepchildren's birth events no longer appear on stepparents' timelines** ([#441](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/441)) - Cliegg Lars's timeline displaying "Birth of Anakin Skywalker" was a real bug (Cliegg married Anakin's mother after Anakin was born). Family-graph now derives `stepchildrenCrIds` on each parent by inverting the children's `stepfather_id` / `stepmother_id`, and the timeline's children-births block skips any id present in the new array.

- **Stepchildren are visibly labeled in the Entity Profile View** ([#443](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/443)) - adopted children already rendered as "Adopted child"; stepchildren did not. The Children block now de-duplicates by labeling adopted and step children with their specific category and falling back to "Child" only when neither marker applies.

- **Map marker popups show ages and full date ranges** ([#444](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/444)) - clicking a marker now appends `(age N)` for non-birth events and renders `from - to` for duration events. Previously Shmi Skywalker Lars's death popup read `Death: 22 BBY` (no age) and Cliegg's residence at Ator from 64 BBY to 22 BBY read `Residence: 64 BBY` (end date dropped). Handles real-world ISO dates, BBY/ABY fictional eras, and era crossings via the same `DateService` path the journey-mode rich popup already used. Sibling fix to #434.

- **Map journey mode explains why playback isn't available** ([#445](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/445)) - entering journey mode for a person without ≥ 2 places with valid coordinates left the toolbar chip showing but no playback panel, with no explanation. The bottom of the map now renders an inline placeholder naming the person and stating what's needed.

- **Map fullscreen control tooltip** ([#446](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/446)) - the small black button below the zoom controls showed the literal text "undefined" as its tooltip (option-shape mismatch with `leaflet-fullscreen`). Now reads "Enter fullscreen" / "Exit fullscreen" correctly. Click behavior was already working.

## Tests

330 tests pass (was 279 at the start of this cycle, +51 across five new files): map marker popup data, universe-calendar suggestions, stepchildren timeline filtering, person-delete cleanup, and the existing #439 timeline-renderer-age suite.

## Reporter

@DigitalDreamn for the entire vault-testing chain that surfaced six of the seven fixes off her #434 verification follow-up. Thanks!

## Stability window

No reset, all seven changes are non-data-loss. Window continues from 0.22.4's start: 2026-04-23 -> ~2026-05-14.

---

Full changelog: [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)
Wiki narrative: [Release-History](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/wiki/Release-History#v0227-round-up-map-ux-stepchild-handling-and-universe-calendar-linking-v0227)
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.7

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
Three non-data-loss fixes that all share the same DateService-bypass root cause first surfaced in v0.22.5's #433 / #434 cluster. Two of these (#437, #438) were filed during v0.22.5 testing; the third (#439) was filed off [@DigitalDreamn](https://github.com/DigitalDreamn)'s verification follow-up on #434, where a screenshot revealed a fourth surface where the same naive year-subtraction pattern lived.

Pattern of the cluster: when a feature was scaffolded for real-world dates and a fictional-calendar surface was added later, the surface inherited the wrong arithmetic. v0.22.6 closes the loop on every place that consumed dates without going through `DateService`.

## What's fixed

- **Date-inconsistency checks respect fictional eras** ([#437](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/437)) - Data-quality checks like `DEATH_BEFORE_BIRTH`, `UNREASONABLE_AGE`, `BORN_BEFORE_PARENT`, and `PARENT_TOO_YOUNG` no longer false-positive on coherent BBY-style descending-era lifespans (e.g., born 1045 BBY, died 1042 BBY = three years). `FUTURE_BIRTH` and `FUTURE_DEATH` are skipped entirely for fictional dates, since they only have meaning against the real-world current year.
- **Map journey now picks up `cr_type: event` notes** ([#438](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/438)) - The map view's journey playback previously only read inline `events:` arrays from the person's frontmatter, missing life events recorded as separate event notes referencing the person via `persons: [[...]]`. For vaults built around the dedicated event-note schema, journeys collapsed down to whatever the dedicated frontmatter milestones supported. Both schemas now feed the same downstream code, deduped by `event_type | place | date`.
- **Timeline dynamic block respects fictional eras when annotating ages** ([#439](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/439)) - The `charted-roots-timeline` block on a person note in a fictional-calendar universe was showing the BBY year for each event but no age annotation alongside it, even though the Born line correctly displayed "age 0", exposing the inconsistency. All eight age-annotation paths in the timeline renderer (own events, family events, context-note events, marriages, divorces, adoptions) now defer to `DateService.calculateAge` with the person's universe.

## Test coverage

38 new unit tests across three files fence the cluster: 10 cases for #437 (BBY span coherence, parent-child ordering, FUTURE-check skipping), 13 for #438 (merge / dedup / wikilink-vs-raw normalization / date coercion), 15 for #439 (BBY span ages, era crossings, real-world dates, fallback). Suite total: **279 tests passing**.

## Stability window

This release does **not** reset the 3-week BRAT stability window. All three fixes are non-data-loss. The window continues from v0.22.4: **2026-04-23 > ~2026-05-14**. Per VERSIONING.md, only critical data-loss bugs reset the clock.

## Install via BRAT

Existing BRAT users will pick up v0.22.6 automatically on the next refresh. New install:

1. Install the [BRAT plugin](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat).
2. Add `banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots` as a beta plugin.
3. BRAT will fetch the latest release and enable Charted Roots in your vault.

## Credits

[@DigitalDreamn](https://github.com/DigitalDreamn) for the verification follow-up on #434 that surfaced the timeline-renderer screenshot, leading to #439.

---

**Full changelog:** [CHANGELOG.md#0226---2026-04-25](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#0226---2026-04-25)
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.6

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
Three non-data-loss fixes surfaced during triage of @DigitalDreamn's Star Wars universe setup (#428) and #429 investigation. The underlying discovery: `DateService` (the central entry point for standard + fictional date parsing) existed as an exported class but was never instantiated on the plugin, so consumers either degraded to numeric-year logic or flagged fictional dates as invalid. This release wires the service up and brings three surfaces into the fold.

**No stability-window reset**. All three are non-data-loss. Window continues from 0.22.4's start (2026-04-23 > ~2026-05-14).

### Fixed

- **Map popup age and duration respect fictional calendars** (#434): The person-journey popup computed age as a plain numeric subtraction of extracted years, which silently produced the wrong answer whenever a fictional calendar was in play. Eras that count down (BBY), era transitions (BBY > ABY), and any universe whose dating system the plugin understood were all ignored. The popup now defers to `DateService.calculateAge` with the person's universe passed in; real-world journeys keep the existing numeric-subtraction behavior as a fallback. Duration-at-location gets the same treatment.

- **Data-quality validator accepts fictional dates** (#433): `isStandardDateFormat` recognized only real-world numeric shapes (`YYYY-MM-DD`, `YYYY-MM`, `YYYY`), so dates like `22 BBY` or `ABY 1042` on persons in a fictional-calendar universe were flagged as `NON_STANDARD_DATE` and the "Fix all" bulk-normalize path tried to rewrite them. The validator now consults the date service for fictional-format recognition and leaves them alone. Applies across the format-issue surfacer and the normalize / preview paths.

- **Dynamic-content processors skip cleanly when the source file is missing** (#431): `DynamicContentService.buildContext` threw `Could not find file: …` whenever a registered metadata handler fired for a container note that had been renamed or deleted before Obsidian cleaned up the `MarkdownRenderChild`. The exception was uncaught and flooded DevTools on every metadata tick. `buildContext` now returns `null` with a warn log, matching the skip-and-warn pattern `BidirectionalLinker` already uses; all 18 callsites across the media, timeline, relationships, sources, transfers, and extractions processors bail out cleanly when the context can't be built.

### Changed

- **Internal:** `DateService` is now instantiated on the plugin and kept in sync with settings (previously defined and exported but never constructed). Groundwork for #432 / #433 / #434.

### Testing

- Suite remains at 241 tests, all passing. Changes are behavior-threading and null-guard additions that existing suites cover.
- Verified manually against the #428 repro (Galactic Standard Calendar on a `universe-star-wars-<suffix>` universe, with a custom-calendar workaround keyed to the full universe ID) and the #431 repro (rename a frontmatter-linked note with a dynamic block open, watch DevTools).

### For the #428 reporter

The workaround in the triage reply still applies; create a custom fictional date system keyed to your full universe `cr_id` for auto-detection of BBY/ABY dates. This release makes that path cleaner (data quality validator won't flag your fictional dates) and makes map popups show correct ages/durations.

Full contract-wiring for universe<->calendar linking (#432) still pending (that's the bigger design conversation tracked separately).
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.5

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
**Critical data-loss hotfix.** Linking a step-father or step-mother in the Edit Person modal showed a success notice on save, but the step-parent fields were silently dropped. They never reached the file. Three-way asymmetry between the create and edit paths: the modal emitted the fields correctly, `createPersonNote` handled them correctly, but `updatePersonNote` had no step-parent branch at all. The load path was also incomplete, so existing step-parent values never round-tripped into the modal on re-open. Full writeup in [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#0224---2026-04-23).

## Fixed

- [#429](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/429): **Critical data loss.** Edit Person modal now correctly persists step-father and step-mother relationships. The fix closes three gaps at once: the load path (`loadRelationships` now extracts step-parent singletons with the same wikilink-fallback pattern as adoptive parents), the plumbing (`bulk-operations.ts` passes the new fields to the modal on edit), and the write path (`updatePersonNote` gains step-father and step-mother write branches mirroring the adjacent adoptive-parent pattern, with clear-on-unlink support). Step-parent relationships now round-trip cleanly through the edit modal, and the bidirectional linker's existing reverse-sync propagates the relationship to the other person's note automatically.

## Under the hood

Test suite grew from 235 to 241 tests. 6 new regression tests in `relationship-loader.test.ts` cover the step-parent load path: no data, explicit IDs, wikilink fallback, basename fallback, stepmother parity, and coexistence with adoptive parents.

## Related observation

The reporter also observed existing Universe and Collection fields being cleared during the same broken save. I couldn't reproduce that wipe in a post-fix clean environment, and code-tracing found no path from a normal step-parent save to unrelated-field clearing. The most likely explanation is a user-state-dependent interaction with one of the dropdowns during the original session. If the wipe recurs after updating to 0.22.4, please reopen [#429](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/429) with the new reproducer.

## Stability window

Per VERSIONING.md, this critical regression resets the 3-week window. New window: **2026-04-23 -> ~2026-05-14.**
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.4

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
**Medium-priority UX fix.** Creating a Collection through the Create Place modal wrote the value to the place note's frontmatter correctly, but the Collection was invisible to the Edit Person modal's Collection dropdown, the Control Center's Collections tab, and the dockable Collections sidebar. The reverse direction already worked; this fix closes the asymmetry. Full writeup in [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#0223---2026-04-23).

## Fixed

- [#426](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/426): Place-only Collections now surface in the Edit Person dropdown and Control Center views. The underlying `FamilyGraphService.getUserCollections()` scanned person notes only, and three UI surfaces relied on it as their sole source. A new pure helper merges person-side and place-side collection counts into a unified cross-entity list, and the three UI surfaces now call through it. Control Center badges render contextually ("X people", "X places", or "X people, Y places" for mixed collections). `FamilyGraphService.getUserCollections()` itself is unchanged. The Canvas Collection Overview and Collection Analytics stay intentionally person-focused.

## Under the hood

Test suite grew from 222 to 235 tests. New `aggregateCollections` pure helper at `src/core/collections-aggregator.ts` is the single merge point for the three UI surfaces. 13 new regression tests cover person-only, place-only, mixed membership, tie-breaking, duplicate-name collapse, and zero-count row dropping.

## Stability window

Not reset — #426 is a medium-priority UX bug, not critical data-loss. Window continues from 0.22.2's start: **2026-04-23 -> ~2026-05-14.**
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.3

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
**Critical data-loss hotfix.** Opening Edit Person on a note whose frontmatter had `children_id` (or `spouse_id` / `parents_id`) without the paired wikilink array showed an empty relationships section, and saving wiped the `*_id` block entirely. This release fixes that and resets the 3-week BRAT stability window for 1.0. Full writeup in [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#0222---2026-04-24).

## Fixed

- [#415](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/415): **Critical data loss.** Edit Person modal no longer wipes IDs-only relationship arrays. The inverse shape of [#410](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/410). (That fix covered wikilinks-without-IDs, but when the wikilink key was entirely absent the loader exited before ever looking at the IDs.) The load path now falls back to walking the `*_id` array when the wikilink key is genuinely absent, resolving each ID back to a display name; on save, the frontmatter is healed to the full dual-storage shape. Applies symmetrically to `children` / `spouse` (legacy array) / `parents`.

## Under the hood

Test suite grew from 209 to 222 tests. New `resolveCrIdToName` pure helper inverts the existing `resolveNameToCrId` resolver; `loadAlignedArray` now has symmetric wikilink-first and IDs-only walk paths. Explicitly empty `children: []` is distinguished from an absent key, so intentionally-empty lists don't resurrect stale IDs. Orphan IDs (pointing to deleted person notes) are preserved round-trip as visible `[[id-str]]` placeholder wikilinks rather than silent drops or `[[]]` corruption.

## Stability window

Per VERSIONING.md, this critical bug resets the 3-week window. New window: **2026-04-23 -> ~2026-05-14.**
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.2

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
**Critical data-loss hotfix.** 0.22.0 introduced a regression where editing a spouse to add marriage metadata could wipe spouse data on both sides of the relationship. This release fixes that and resets the 3-week BRAT stability window for 1.0. Full writeup in [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#0221---2026-04-23).

## Fixed

- [#423](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/423): **Critical data loss.** Adding marriage date / location / status to an existing spouse no longer wipes spouse data on both notes. The bidirectional linker now correctly distinguishes format migrations (flat <-> indexed) from actual deletions, and its unlink-cleanup path removes all five `spouse{N}_*` metadata fields consistently.

## Under the hood

Test suite grew from 189 to 209 tests. New `isSpouseInFrontmatter` pure helper guards the deletion-detection path in `BidirectionalLinker.syncDeletions` against phantom-deletion cascades on format migration.

## Stability window

Per VERSIONING.md, this critical regression resets the 3-week window. New window: **2026-04-23 -> ~2026-05-14.**
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.1

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
**Stability release on the path to 1.0.** Critical data-loss bug fix plus five other bug fixes and one feature addition. This release resets the 3-week BRAT stability window described in the [Road to 1.0 discussion](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/discussions/411). Full writeup in [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#0220---2026-04-22).

## Fixed

- [#420](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/420): **Critical - data loss.** Cross-note spouse writes no longer corrupt a target's indexed multi-spouse frontmatter. Carved out from the #410 extension.
- [#416](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/416): Timeline no longer crashes when a date field is a bare numeric year (`born: 1893` without quotes).
- [#414](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/414): Life Events Migration no longer creates duplicate event notes on re-run, semantic dedup by `(persons, event_type, date)`.
- [#417](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/417): Dynamic relationships block now surfaces adoptive siblings in `extended` / `all` modes with an `Adoptive sibling:` label.
- [#419](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/419): Symmetric custom relationships (e.g., `twin`) now auto-propagate to the target person's note.
- Person picker no longer crashes when a person has a bare-year date field (same class as #416, different helper, `formatDisplayDate`).

## Added

- [#408](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/408): Burial now renders on the person timeline block alongside birth, death, adoption, and marriage.

## Under the hood

Test suite grew from 109 to 189 regression tests. Five new pure-helper modules with their own test files (sibling walker, relationship property writer, event identity, spouse format detector, date-display coercion). Migration-service pure helpers extracted per the 1.0 testing-gate criterion. `tsc` dropped from the build chain and exposed as `npm run type-check` for on-demand checking.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.22.0

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
**Stability release on the path to 1.0.** Four Edit Person modal round-trip bugs fixed; no feature work. This release starts the 3-week BRAT stability window described in the [Road to 1.0 discussion](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/discussions). Full writeup in [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#0210---2026-04-21).

## Fixed

- [#410](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/410): Relationships no longer dropped when IDs are partial or wikilink basenames differ from `name`
- [#406](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/406): Clearing Universe (and 10 other optional fields) now actually clears frontmatter
- [#412](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/412): Nickname field now round-trips on edit
- [#413](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/413): Toggling Endogamy flag off now persists

## Under the hood

Vitest test harness added with 31 regression tests for the relationship load path. New [VERSIONING.md](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/VERSIONING.md) documents plugin-specific SemVer rules and 1.0 criteria.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.21.0

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
A community-testing-driven patch release following v0.20.61. Thanks to @DigitalDreamn and @doctorwodka for the detailed reports that shaped most of this release.

### Changed

- **Custom relationship overlay rendering overhaul** ([#404](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/404)): Overlays now arc under the tree rather than cutting straight across cards, so they read distinctly from structural family lines on larger trees. For adopted / step / foster overlays that map onto a structural parent-child link, the link itself is restyled with the overlay's color, dash, and tooltip — one expressive line per relationship instead of two coincident ones. Purely custom overlays (sire / childer, mentor, ally, etc.) always arc.

### Fixed

- **Unlinking a relationship via Edit Person now updates both sides** ([#405](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/405)). Clears were silently no-oping, and reverse-inference was bringing removed links back from the other side's note. Both halves fixed.
- **Editing a person no longer clears relationships from notes missing `_id` frontmatter keys** ([#403](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/403)). The modal now falls back to resolving wikilink names when the `_id` field is missing, and saves heal the data shape going forward.
- **Export logs button no longer fails silently on subsequent uses** ([#402](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/402)). Errors now surface via a user-visible notice.
- **Overlay lines draw at correct positions on initial render of large trees** ([#386](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/386) follow-up). The re-render schedule now waits for card positions to stabilize instead of relying on a fixed delay that wasn't long enough for staggered entrance animations.
- **"Show children's births" toggle now correctly excludes adopted children** ([#396](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/396) follow-up). The two toggles are now fully independent.

**Full changelog:** [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.62

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
A follow-up release focused on adoption-event coverage on timelines, marriage/divorce rendering fixes, and community-reported polish on the custom relationships overlay from v0.20.59.

### Added

- **Adoption events on adoptive parents' timelines** ([#396](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/396) follow-up): When an adopted child has `adoption_date` set, the adoptive parent's timeline now renders an "Adopted [[Child]]" entry on that date. Adoption is a shared life event. (It appears on both the adoptee's and the adoptive parents' timelines, matching the marriage-on-both-sides pattern.) Always on, no toggle.
- **"Show adopted children's births" toggle** (new, under **Settings -> Advanced -> Family events on timelines**): Independent of the existing "Show children's births" toggle, which covers biological children only. Default **off**. When enabled, adopted children's birth dates render on the adoptive parent's timeline as family events. Explicit opt-in keeps biological and adoptive child visibility as separate controls.

### Fixed

- **Sibling births now render when siblings share parents via the `parents` array** ([#401](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/401)): The sibling-discovery loop for the "Show sibling births" setting was only reading `father` / `mother` when collecting a person's parents, so siblings who share parents declared exclusively in the gender-neutral `parents` array were invisible on the timeline. The collection now also includes `parents`, matching how the parent-deaths pass already worked.
- **Adopted child's birth no longer duplicated when also listed as a biological child** ([#396](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/396) follow-up): If a person is referenced in both `adopted_child_id` and `children_id` on the same parent, the timeline used to emit two birth entries when both "Show children's births" and "Show adopted children's births" were enabled. The adopted-children pass now skips births already emitted by the biological pass; adoption events themselves still render (they're distinct from births).
- **Marriage and divorce timeline entries no longer render as raw text** ([#399](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/399)): The initial v0.20.60 implementation embedded `[[Spouse]]` inside the title string, producing nested wikilinks that Obsidian's markdown parser couldn't resolve. Titles now use plain text for the spouse name with the link provided separately, matching how existing family-event entries are constructed.
- **Custom relationships overlay: four follow-up fixes from community testing** ([#386](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/386)):
  - **Wider hover target for tooltips.** The 2px stroke required pixel-precise cursor placement to trigger the tooltip. A transparent 14px hit line now sits behind each visible line, making hover reliable without thickening the visible stroke.
  - **Built-in relationship types honor the overlay flag.** Enabling "Render on family chart as overlay line" on a built-in type (e.g., Master/Apprentice) was saving correctly but silently dropped on read (the customization-merge function wasn't propagating the new flag). Built-in types now render as overlay lines when the flag is on.
  - **Inverse-type pairs no longer double up visually.** When two relationship types are inverses of each other (sire/childer, mentor/disciple, captor/prisoner) and both are overlay-enabled, each side of a declared pair used to produce its own line at identical coordinates. The overlapping strokes reinforced, making dotted look like dashed and dashed look like solid. The dedupe pass now canonicalizes to the alphabetically-earlier type id so the pair collapses to a single line.
  - **Card-position lookup scoped to the current chart.** `d3.selectAll('.card_cont')` previously searched the entire document, so stale or hidden cards from other family chart tabs contributed to the position map (potentially drawing overlay lines from wrong coordinates). The selector is now scoped to the current chart container.

**Full changelog:** [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.61

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Highlights

A focused follow-up to 0.20.59, shipping four user-reported bug fixes and feature gaps that all surfaced in the week since. Inline life-event rendering gets meaningfully expanded.

### Inline life events expand

The `charted-roots-timeline` block on a person's note already derived `born` and `died` inline from frontmatter. Three new event types join that list, all driven by data the user already has on the note.

- **`adoption_date` frontmatter property** ([#396](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/396)) -- Set `adoption_date: YYYY-MM-DD` on an adoptee's note and an "Adopted" entry renders on their timeline. Parsed into the family graph; written back by the Create/Edit flows; always on, no toggle.
- **Marriages and divorces** ([#399](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/399)) -- Spouse-relationship metadata (`spouse1_marriage_date`, `spouse1_divorce_date`, `spouse1_marriage_location`, etc.) now surfaces as "Marriage to [[Spouse]]" and "Divorce from [[Spouse]]" timeline entries. Marriages are always on; divorces default on but have a dedicated **Show divorces** toggle for users who prefer to hide them.
- **Manually-declared siblings** ([#398](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/398)) -- The existing "Show sibling births" setting previously only derived siblings from shared parents. It now also picks up siblings declared via the built-in `sibling` relationship type, covering worldbuilder cases where the parents aren't modeled as notes.

### Adoption duplication fix

- **Adoptive parents no longer rendered twice** ([#392](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/392)) -- Follow-on to the initial #392 fix in 0.20.59. When an adoptive parent had `adopted_child` set on their note, the reverse-inference pass was duplicating them into the child's gender-neutral array even though they were already in the gender-specific slot. Child's relationships block now shows each parent once, labeled correctly as "Adoptive father" / "Adoptive mother."

## Thanks

Thanks to @doctorwodka and @DigitalDreamn for the detailed reports and quick iteration on each fix. The marriage/divorce toggle design, the sibling-relationship gap, and the adoption duplicate were all shaped by hands-on testing of 0.20.59 and direct feedback.

## Full changelog

See [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#0.20.60) for the complete list with links to each issue.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.60

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Highlights

This release expands the family chart into a much more expressive surface. Two new visualization features plus a batch of adoption-handling fixes.

### Family chart gets smarter

**Custom relationships overlay** ([#386](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/386)) - Custom non-family relationships (liege/vassal, ally/rival, master/apprentice, godparent, and any custom types you create) can now render as styled overlay lines on the family chart. Each relationship type has a new **Render on family chart as overlay line** toggle, decoupled from the existing tree-structure integration, so a type can be tree-only, overlay-only, or both. The Display menu gains a "Show custom relationships" master toggle plus per-type toggles when multiple overlay-enabled types exist. Lines are styled by the type's color and line style, stack when the same pair has multiple overlay relationships, respect the "as of" date filter, and show a hover tooltip with source, type, target, and date range.

**Highlight groups** ([#379](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/379)) - A new "Highlight groups..." entry in the Display menu opens a modal for defining up to three concurrent highlight groups. Each group pairs a person property (sex, occupation, title, religion, caste, nationality, universe, collection) with a value and a palette color; matching cards render with a colored glow while non-matching cards dim to 30% opacity. Useful for spotting patterns in large trees (e.g. "who are all the magicians?", "which families share this religion?", etc.) Twelve palette colors available.

### Family timeline badge (completes a long-standing gap)

A **users badge** now appears on person rows in the Control Center People tab when the family unit (person + spouses + children) has events. Clicking opens a modal showing the aggregated family timeline. Events color-coded by family member and sorted chronologically. The underlying rendering helpers shipped back in v0.10.0 and the wiki has documented this feature since, but the call site that adds the badge to the UI was never wired up. That's now fixed.

### Adoption handling, straightened out

A batch of related fixes so adoption works correctly end-to-end:

- **Edit Person modal** now reads and writes adoptive parent fields ([#390](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/390)). Previously silently dropped updates and failed to display existing values.
- **Add Relationship modal** now writes adoptive parents to the correct gender-specific field based on target's sex ([#391](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/391)). Previously lumped both parents into the gender-neutral array.
- **Dynamic relationships block** now shows adoptive parents (and step parents, [#395](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/395)) on the adopted/step child's note ([#392](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/392)). Previously asymmetric (shown on parent's side only).

### Calendarium + real-world dates together

**Event modal now preserves `date` when `fc-date` is present** ([#393](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/393)). If you keep both a real-world `date` and a fictional `fc-date` on the same event (for cross-referencing your worldbuilding timeline with our calendar), the modal was fighting with the real-world date on save. It now writes edits to `fc-date` when the Calendarium field is present, leaving an independent `date` untouched.

## Thanks

Special thanks to @doctorwodka, @DigitalDreamn, and @rianneklok for the detailed reports and thoughtful feedback that drove this release. Several of these features such as the overlay, the highlight groups, and the adoption fixes, were shaped in direct response to community discussions and issues over the past week.

## Full changelog

See [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#0.20.59) for the complete list with links to each issue.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.59

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Family Chart polish & worldbuilder feedback

This release is driven largely by a thoughtful [worldbuilder discussion (#371)](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/discussions/371) about what the Family Chart needs to handle complex trees and non-traditional use cases. Every addition here benefits genealogists too; better card layouts, point-in-time views, and sort options have been high-value for both audiences.

### Added

- **Report Wizard entry points** ([#372](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/372)): the Report Wizard is now reachable from the command palette (*Open report wizard*), a new card in the Trees & Reports tab, and the person-note context menu (*Generate report*, with the person pre-selected).

- **Additional in-tree card field toggles** ([#374](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/374)): six new toggles in the Family Chart display menu: **Show title**, **Show occupation**, **Show nickname**, **Show religion**, **Show caste**, **Show pronouns**. Cards grow vertically to fit whatever you enable, and all card styles (rectangle, compact, mini) adjust. Useful for worldbuilders putting rank/title on display, and for genealogists who want occupation or religion visible at a glance.

- **Sort spouses by marriage date** ([#375](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/375)): mirroring the existing "sort children by birth date" toggle. Spouses render first-married (left) to most-recently-married (right); spouses without a marriage date fall to the end. Useful for serial remarriages and polygamous family trees where frontmatter order doesn't reflect chronology.

- **"As of" date filter for Family Chart** ([#376](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/376)): a new Time group on the toolbar with a date picker and clear button. When a date is set:
  - People born after that date are hidden
  - People who died before that date render with a dashed border and reduced opacity, preserving family structure
  - Marriage lines are shown only when the marriage was active on the selected date

  Selected date persists across restarts. Useful for "what did my family look like in 1870?" genealogy questions and for tracking character states across a fictional timeline.

### Fixed

- **Family chart card spacing decoupled from card width** ([#373](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/373)): the `compact` and `mini` card styles used to look visually identical to the default `rectangle` because horizontal spacing between siblings was fixed regardless of card width. Each style now carries a matching default spacing (rectangle 250px, compact 200px, mini 140px), with a new **Tight (140px)** spacing preset. Spacing is clamped to `card_width + 20px` so no preset can collapse cards into each other. The Compact and Mini styles also now respect the Show avatars toggle, instead of force-hiding avatars.

### Documentation

- **Expanded custom-relationship type catalog** ([#383](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/383)): the [Custom Relationships wiki page](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/wiki/Custom-Relationships) previously documented 12 types across 4 categories; the plugin actually ships 25+ types across 6 categories. Added the missing Feudal/World-building (liege/vassal, ally/rival), DNA/Genetic (DNA match, opt-in), and fuller Social and Professional categories. Added a new **When to Use Non-Family Relationships** section surfacing FAN-network research, worldbuilding, and household-tracking use cases.

### Known limitations

- Custom non-family relationships (liege/vassal, ally/rival, etc.) aren't yet rendered on the Family Chart itself. They exist as data and appear in text views but aren't visualized on the tree. Tracked in [#386](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/386); will also extend the as-of date filter to those relationships when it ships.

### Coming next

The next release will focus on the **custom-property-definitions epic** ([#377](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/377)): user-defined fields on person notes with typing (text / number / tag-list / date / image), renderable across the in-tree card, info panel, and Entity Profile View. Unblocks several filed follow-ups ([#378](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/378), [#379](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/379), [#380](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/380), [#381](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/381), [#382](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/382)).
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.58

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
This release resolves six TODO items identified during the codebase cleanup scan (#365–#370), filling in placeholder functionality across reports, events, schemas, organizations, and the report wizard.

### Added

- **Marriage data in Family Group Sheet** (#370): Reports now include marriage date and place extracted from spouse relationship data, in both markdown and PDF output. Multiple marriages are supported with per-spouse sections.
