- **Multiple person picker in event modal** (#366): Events like marriages or group events can now have additional people beyond the primary person. Uses the existing `persons[]` frontmatter array, with support for edit mode and form state persistence.
- **Targeted schema validation** (#367): The "Validate matching notes" context menu on schemas now validates only notes matching that schema, with a progress modal and result summary - instead of being a no-op.
- **Organization membership statistics** (#368): The Organizations statistics card now shows real membership counts (people with memberships, total memberships, empty organizations) instead of placeholder zeros.
- **Universe and collection pickers in Report Wizard** (#369): Report types targeting a universe or collection now have fuzzy-search picker modals instead of "not yet implemented" notices.
- **Web Clipper discoverability** (#364): Info boxes in the Places, Sources, and People tabs link to Web Clipper wiki templates.

### Fixed

- **Step-parent sex assignment** (#365): Step-parent relationships now check the target person's sex and assign to `stepmotherCrIds` or `stepfatherCrIds` accordingly, instead of always defaulting to stepfather.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.57

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## What's New

### Linked map drill-down navigation (#361)
Full drill-down navigation between custom maps, enabling hierarchical map exploration:
- **Place-linked maps:** `linked_map` on place notes - clicking a place marker shows an "Open [map name]" button in the popup
- **Parent/child hierarchy:** `parent_map` on map notes - breadcrumb navigation in the toolbar (e.g., "The Dying Earth -> River Scaum"), plus a parent map dropdown in the Map Creation Wizard
- **Overlay regions:** `parent_region_x/y/w/h` on child map notes - clickable dashed overlay rectangles on the parent map showing where child maps sit

### Child map markers and interactive region editing (#362)
Visual tools for managing child map regions on parent maps:
- Gold map-icon markers on parent maps for every child map, with popup showing "Open map" and "Edit/Draw region" buttons
- **On-map region editing:** drag the rectangle to reposition, drag corner handles to resize, with a floating save/cancel toolbar - all at full map zoom for precision
- Region drawing modal in the Map Creation Wizard for setting the region during map creation
- "Draw/Edit parent region" context menu item on child map notes
- "Child maps" layer toggle in the Layers menu

### Universe dynamic blocks (#359, #360)
- **Entity listing blocks** for universe notes: `charted-roots-universe-people`, `-places`, `-events`, `-organizations` - auto-refreshing tables with clickable wikilinks, sortable by name/date/type
- **Map thumbnails block:** `charted-roots-universe-maps` renders clickable map image thumbnails with place count badges

### Other
- Book Builder link added to the Report Wizard for discoverability

## Bug Fixes
- Place markers not showing on custom maps when universe field stored a cr_id instead of display name
- "Create place here" and "Link existing place" on custom maps wrote universe cr_id instead of name
- "Link existing place" crashed on custom maps due to undefined `folderFilter`
- Universe dropdown in map wizard showed cr_ids alongside names
- Universe map thumbnails showed the same place count for all maps
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.56

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## What's New

### Image Region Crop for Thumbnails ([#354](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/354))

Select a region of an image (e.g., a face in a group photo) to use as the thumbnail. Right-click any image in the media block to define a crop region with a visual selection modal.

- **Visual crop modal:** Draw a draggable, resizable rectangle on the image with darkened overlay and live preview
- **Crop applied everywhere:** Media block, Family Chart avatars, Entity Profile View header and media section
- **`media_crop` frontmatter:** Crop coordinates stored per-image, backward compatible with existing `media` property
- **Gramps import:** Crop regions from Gramps `.gpkg` files are automatically imported (percentage-based coordinates converted at render time)
- **Edit/remove:** Right-click a cropped image to edit or remove the crop

### Family Chart Info Panel Expansion ([#351](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/351))

The Person details panel now supports editing all major person fields:

- Alt name, pronouns, occupation (text inputs)
- Birth place, death place (PlacePickerModal with Pick/Clear buttons)
- Relationships (+ Add button opens full Add Relationship modal with bidirectional sync)
- Research level (dropdown, 0-6), collection (text input), sources (+ Link source button)

## Fixes

- **PDF thumbnail sizing** ([#350](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/350)): PDF thumbnails now match the same grid cell dimensions as image thumbnails using `object-fit: contain`.
- **Burial event type in events array** ([#355](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/355)): `burial` now works as an event type in the person-level `events` array for map markers.
- **Entity Profile View media thumbnails blank** ([#356](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/356)): Image thumbnails in the profile view media section were invisible due to a missing dot in the file extension comparison.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.55

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## What's New

### PDF Previews in Media Blocks ([#350](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/350))

PDFs attached to person and source notes now show a first-page thumbnail preview instead of a generic file icon.

- **charted-roots-media block:** PDF thumbnails in reading view
- **Sources tab:** PDF previews in the Control Center media gallery
- **Entity Profile View:** PDF thumbnails in the media section

Uses Obsidian's built-in PDF.js (via `loadPdfJs()`) with in-memory caching for fast subsequent renders. Click behavior unchanged — opens the PDF in Obsidian's viewer (compatible with PDF++). Falls back to icon placeholder if thumbnail generation fails.

### Technical Debt Cleanup

- Completed service access standardization (Phase 3): migrated 59 direct service constructor calls to plugin factory methods across FamilyGraphService, PlaceGraphService, and SourceService
- Completed large file splitting (Phase 4): extracted ~2,170 lines from create-person-modal, family-chart-view, and cleanup-wizard-modal into focused companion files
- Minor type safety and unused export cleanup (Phase 5)
- Updated developer documentation with service access patterns and project structure
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.54

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## What's New

### Alt Name Across All Views ([#347](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/347), [#348](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/348), [#349](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/349))

The `alt_name` property introduced in v0.20.52 now displays across all major views:

- **Entity Profile View** ([#349](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/349)): Shown below the main name in the profile header with muted styling
- **Map view popups** ([#347](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/347)): Shown below the person name in birth, death, marriage, burial, and event marker popups
- **Visual tree charts** ([#348](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/348)): Additional text line on SVG tree chart nodes (all layouts) and Excalidraw export nodes

Combined with v0.20.52's Family Chart card support, `alt_name` is now visible everywhere a person's name appears.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.53

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## What's New

### Alt Name Display on Family Chart Cards ([#346](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/346))

New `alt_name` frontmatter property for displaying alternate names on Family Chart View cards. Designed for multilingual genealogy where both native script and romanized names need to be visible at a glance.

```yaml
---
name: Cugel the Clever
alt_name: 张三
---
```

- Alt name renders as a second line below the main name on Family Chart cards
- Shown in the Person details panel (between Last name and Birth date)
- Card height auto-adjusts when any person in the chart has an alt name
- Cards without `alt_name` are unaffected

Additional views (map popups, canvas trees, Entity Profile View) tracked in #347, #348, #349.

**[Documentation](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/wiki/Frontmatter-Reference#basic-information)**
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.52

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Fixes

### Context Menu Actions Broken ([#344](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/344))

**Regression in v0.20.50.** The "Add essential properties," "Add cr_id," and several other context menu actions silently failed after the context menu refactoring. Helper functions were extracted to a separate file but not imported back into the builder functions that use them.

If you are on v0.20.50, please update to v0.20.51 immediately.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.51

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## What's New

### Heat Map Intensity Control ([#336](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/336))

The heat map is now fully configurable:

- **Layers menu toggle:** Dedicated heat map section with low/medium/high intensity selector
- **Settings > Places:** Default intensity dropdown plus customizable radius, blur, and opacity values for each preset
- **Native density:** Per-point intensity scaling removed in favor of leaflet.heat's relative density algorithm. Clusters naturally appear hotter than isolated points regardless of dataset size.

### Layers Menu Reorganized

Data layers (markers, paths, places) grouped together, with heat map as a visual overlay section at the bottom separated by dividers.

## Fixes

### Birth Markers Hidden Under Death Markers ([#343](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/343))

Birth and death markers at the same location overlapped in separate cluster groups, hiding one marker and showing an incorrect count. All event marker types now share a single unified cluster group. Overlapping markers combine into one cluster with the correct total count, and clicking the cluster spiders out to show all individual markers. Cluster icon color reflects the dominant event type.

### Technical Debt (Phase 1-2)

- Consolidated duplicate wikilink extraction and date formatting utilities
- Extracted 30+ context menu helper functions into separate file
- Extracted 11 entity-specific menu builders from the monolithic `registerContextMenus()` function, reducing it from ~4,080 lines to ~150 lines of clean dispatch logic
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.50

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## What's New

### People Folder Mismatch Warning ([#342](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/342))

When the Map View finds 0 people in the configured People folder but detects person notes in other folders, a Notice now warns you to check Settings > Folders. This prevents silent missing markers when person notes live outside the expected folder path.

## Fixes

### Heat Map Intensity Tuning ([#336](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/336))

Follow-up tuning based on community feedback, The heat map was too intense on custom pixel maps with moderate data. Reduced radius, blur, and minimum opacity for a more balanced appearance while remaining clearly visible.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.49

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Fixes

### Heat Map on Custom Maps ([#336](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/336))

The heat map layer was nearly invisible or completely hidden on custom pixel-coordinate maps. Three issues fixed:

- **Rendering behind image overlay:** Heat layer canvas was underneath the custom map image. Fixed z-index ordering to render above.
- **Wrong zoom scaling:** Pixel maps use much lower zoom levels (0-3) than geographic maps. `maxZoom` is now set relative to the current zoom so points render at full intensity.
- **Increased visibility:** Minimum opacity set to 0.6 for pixel maps and 0.3 for geographic maps. Intensity scaling bumped (≤10 markers: 5×, ≤30: 3×, ≤100: 2×). Radius and blur tripled for pixel maps.

Also fixed the stats page "places without coordinates" check to recognize both `coordinates_lat`/`coordinates_long` and `custom_coordinates_x`/`custom_coordinates_y`.

### Universe Wizard Custom Maps ([#340](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/340))

Custom maps created via the Universe Wizard appeared squished because the wizard defaulted to `coordinate_system: geographic` with fixed ±100 degree bounds, ignoring the image's actual aspect ratio.

- Default coordinate system changed from `geographic` to `pixel`
- Image dimensions now loaded automatically and written as `image_width`/`image_height`
- `default_zoom: 1` set for pixel mode maps

**Note:** Maps already created with the old defaults will need manual fixes -- change `coordinate_system: pixel`, add `image_width`/`image_height`, and remove the `bounds_*` properties.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.48

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## What's New

### Calendar View ([#299](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/299))

New workspace view showing a monthly calendar grid of significant dates across your vault.

- **Color-coded event dots:** blue (birth), red (death), yellow (marriage) on day cells
- **Text labels toggle:** switch between dots-only and labeled mode showing person names
- **Flexible navigation:** month dropdown, year input (jump to 1842 instantly), prev/next arrows, Today button
- **Day detail panel:** click a day to see all events with person name, type, year, years ago, and place
- **Imprecise dates:** entries with month but no day shown in a "This month, day unknown" section
- **Filtering:** toggle event types (birth, death, marriage, residence, occupation, etc.) and living/deceased status
- **Right-click to create events:** context menu on day cells opens Create Event with date pre-filled
- **Keyboard navigation:** arrow keys for month nav, T for today
- **State persistence** month, year, filters, and label toggle persist across reloads

**Entry points:**
- Command palette: "Open calendar view"
- Control Center dashboard tile
- Events tab: Calendar button
- Person note context menu: "Show on calendar" (navigates to birth month/year)
- Event note context menu: "Show on calendar" (navigates to event date)

**[Documentation](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/wiki/Calendar-View)**
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.47

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## What's New

### Source Note Hierarchies ([#337](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/337))

New `source_parent` and `source_parent_id` properties for modeling multi-document record groups - probate packets, census pages, record series, and multi-volume collections.

- Set a parent source in the **Create/Edit source** modal under **Additional details > Parent source** (with autocomplete)
- Or add `source_parent: "[[Parent Note]]"` and `source_parent_id` directly in frontmatter

**[Documentation](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/wiki/Evidence-And-Sources#source-hierarchies)**

### Source Hierarchy Display and Navigation ([#338](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/338))

The Entity Profile View and Sources tab now understand parent-child source relationships:

- **Parent source** link on child source profiles
- **Child documents** section on parent source profiles (type badge, title, date)
- **Related documents** section showing sibling sources with the same parent
- **Source tree** - collapsible tree visualization on parent profiles
- **Hierarchy filters** in Sources tab: "has parent," "no parent (top-level)," and "children of" a specific parent

### Other Additions

- **"Create source" context menu** on the Sources folder for quick source note creation

## Changed

- **Collapsible sections in modals** restyled from action-link appearance (blue plus icon) to section-header style (bordered container, chevron icon) for clearer affordance

## Fixes

- **Heat map barely visible with sparse data** ([#336](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/336)): Intensity now scales inversely with point count - sparse datasets render at 3× intensity. maxZoom raised from 10 to 15 so the heat layer stays visible when zoomed in.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.46

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## What's New

### Person-Focused Map Journey ([#295](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/295))

New **journey mode** in the Map View for exploring a single person's geographic path through life.

- **Person isolation:** Click the route button in the toolbar or right-click a person note → "Show journey on map" to enter journey mode. All other markers and paths are hidden, focusing on the selected person's events and journey path.
- **Animated playback:** Step through waypoints with prev/play/next controls. Smooth fly-to animation, progress bar, step counter, and adjustable speed (0.25×–2.5×).
- **Rich waypoint popups:** Each stop shows event type, date, place, age, duration at location, and event description.
- **Family journey overlay:** Toggle dimmed journey paths for immediate family members, color-coded by relationship: blue (parents), pink (spouses), emerald (children). Click a family path to switch focus to that person's journey.

**[Documentation](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/wiki/Geographic-Features#journey-mode)**

## Fixes

- **Map tiles blocked by OpenStreetMap** ([#333](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/333)): Tile images now set `referrerPolicy='no-referrer'` to prevent Obsidian's Electron `app://` referrer from being rejected by OSM. Applied to main map, mini-map, profile view map preview, and world map preview.
- **Map preview blank after collapse/expand** ([#334](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/334)): Leaflet map in place profiles no longer destroys on collapse; calls `invalidateSize()` on re-expand.
- **World map preview invalid tile requests** ([#335](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/335)): Added bounds constraint to prevent tile requests at negative x coordinates.
- **GeoNames 401 errors silent:** Now shows a Notice when GeoNames returns 401, with guidance to check Settings. Setting description links directly to the GeoNames registration and activation pages.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.45

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
Hotfix for timeline context margin calculation.

### Fixed

- **Context lifespan margin includes family events** (#332): The context lifespan margin was using all timeline entries (including sibling births, spouse deaths, etc.) to calculate the year range, causing historical context events far outside the person's own lifespan to appear. Now only the person's own events determine the ±margin range.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.44

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
Hotfix for timeline alignment.

### Fixed

- **Historical context events extra spacing** (#330, #331): Context events (from historical overlay notes) had extra left padding causing misalignment with personal and family events. Same root cause as the family event padding fixed in v0.20.42.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.43

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
This release fixes several timeline rendering issues, a relationship assignment bug, and orphaned data cleanup.

### Fixed

- **Create child assigns wrong parent role** (#329): Adding a child via context menu incorrectly assigned a female parent as "father" because the sex comparison only checked for the normalized value `F`, not the raw frontmatter value `female`. Now handles all formats.

- **Orphaned children_id cleanup**: When adding a child, existing `children_id` entries that point to deleted notes are now automatically removed.

- **Timeline spacing and alignment** (#330): Entries without event type icons had inconsistent left alignment compared to entries with icons. A hidden placeholder now maintains consistent positioning. Family event items also had extra padding causing misalignment.

- **Timeline format string rendering** (#331): Multiple fixes for the `format` parameter:
  - Surrounding quotes from YAML values no longer appear as literal characters
  - Spaces between placeholders (e.g., `" in "` between `{title}` and `{place}`) no longer collapse
  - Icons now render on format string entries (previously skipped entirely)
  - Family events sort correctly when no historical context note is configured
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.42

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
Hotfix for the statistics dashboard.

### Fixed

- **Gender distribution and citation statistics not rendering** (#326): The expandable person lists under Sex & Gender Distribution and the Citation Statistics section were referencing an undefined service property, causing both to silently fail. The sections now render correctly with clickable person lists and citation metrics.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.41

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
Bugfix release addressing three issues reported by the community.

### Fixed

- **Gender distribution lists not loading** (#326): Expandable person lists under each sex/gender category weren't populating when toggled. Also fixed `sex: unknown` being counted as "Other" instead of "Unknown" in the distribution chart.

- **Adopted children duplicated in Profile View** (#328): Adopted children appeared as both "Child" and "Adopted child" in the relationships section. Now only the more specific "Adopted child" label is shown.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.40

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### New feature

**Expandable person lists per sex/gender category** (#326): The Sex & Gender Distribution section in the statistics dashboard now includes expandable person lists under each category. Click "Show" to reveal clickable links to person notes:
- **Male / Female**: all people in each category
- **Other**: shows the actual value entered (e.g., "non-binary") next to each name
- **Unknown (explicitly stated)**: people with `sex: unknown` set intentionally
- **Not stated (missing)**: people with no sex/gender property, for easy back-filling

Categories with zero entries are hidden. All lists lazy-loaded on first expand.

### Bug fix

**Record superlatives 120-year age cap** (#327): The hardcoded 120-year maximum in longevity analysis, record superlatives, and data quality checks is now removed when the "Enable fictional dates" setting is on. Fictional characters with extended lifespans appear correctly in statistics without needing a separate toggle.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.39

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
This release adds customizable timeline display, citation integration features, and several fixes.

### Customizable timeline display templates (#325)

Four new capabilities for controlling how timeline entries are displayed:

- **Layout modes**: new `layout` parameter (or global setting) with three options: `chronological` (default), `grouped` (separate sections for personal/family/historical events with dividers), and `personal-first`
- **Label customization**: six settings under Advanced > Timeline labels to override event labels like "Born", "Died", and "Birth of {name}" with custom text and `{name}` placeholders
- **Format strings**: per-block `format` parameter with placeholders (`{year}`, `{title}`, `{place}`, `{age}`, etc.) to control exactly how each entry renders
- **Template notes**: reference a markdown note via `template: [[Note]]` to define custom sections with independent sort, include, and format options. Set a global default in settings or override per block.

### Citation integration (#324)

Extends the citation metadata model with derived views and coverage tracking:

- **Sync commands**: "Sync sourced fields from citation notes" populates `sourced_*` frontmatter from citation notes (per-person or vault-wide). "Generate citation notes from sourced fields" creates citation notes from existing `sourced_*` data with deduplication.
- **Citation-aware reports**: source summary report includes a Page column when citation notes have page references
- **Statistics dashboard**: new "Citation statistics" section showing total citations, coverage percentage, quality distribution (0-3), and most cited sources

### Fixes

- **Relationship calculator ancestor grouping** (#321): common ancestors who are spouses are now grouped as couples. Additional results show full relationship paths. Fixed missing space before "via".
- **Timeline icon consistency** (#323): when family events are present, personal events always show their type icons for consistent alignment.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.38

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
Two new features for enriching timelines and exploring family connections.

### Family events on timelines (#323)

Person timelines can now display significant life events of family members alongside the person's own events. Four global toggles in Settings > Advanced control which types appear:

- **Children's births**: "Birth of [[Child Name]]"
- **Spouse deaths**: "Death of [[Spouse Name]]"
- **Parent deaths**: "Death of [[Parent Name]]"
- **Sibling births**: "Birth of [[Sibling Name]]"

All off by default. Each entry links to the family member's note, shows age annotations, and renders with a subtle blue background to distinguish from personal events. Add `familyEvents: none` to any timeline code block to suppress on individual timelines.

### Calculate multiple relationships (#321)

The relationship calculator now finds more than just the shortest path between two people. After viewing the primary result, click "Find more relationships" to discover additional connections through different common ancestors (essential for endogamous families and consanguineous marriages). Each result shows the relationship type, common ancestor name, and whether it's a blood or marriage connection. A configurable max search depth (Settings > Advanced, default 10 generations, 0 for unlimited) controls how deep the search goes.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.37

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
This release adds citation-level metadata support and fixes several GEDCOM and Gramps export issues.

### Citation metadata (#316)

A new citation entity type that links a source to a specific fact on a person with page reference and quality assessment, enabling lossless GEDCOM PAGE/QUAY roundtrips.

- **GEDCOM import** generates citation notes from `SOUR` blocks with `PAGE` and/or `QUAY` metadata
- **GEDCOM and Gramps export** writes citation metadata back as `PAGE`/`QUAY` sub-tags on source references
- **"Add citation" command**: manually create citation notes from a person note, with source picker, fact selector, page input, and quality dropdown. Also available in the command menu.
- **Profile View**: person profiles now show a Citations section (after Sources) listing citations grouped by source with fact labels, page references, and color-coded quality badges
- New `citationsFolder` setting (default: `Charted Roots/Citations`)

### Export fixes

- **Gramps export regression**: the Gramps format was listed in the export wizard but had no handler, showing "not yet supported"
- **Place wikilinks**: person-level places in both GEDCOM and Gramps exports were written with `[[]]` brackets instead of plain text
- **Place hierarchy order**: GEDCOM hierarchical places were exported top-down instead of GEDCOM's most-specific-first order
- **Duplicate events in Gramps**: birth, death, burial, and occupation events appeared twice (person-level + event notes)
- **Duplicate places in Gramps**: variant place name formatting created duplicate entries
- **Missing source references**: person-level birth, death, and burial events in both GEDCOM and Gramps had no source references
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.36

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
This release fixes three bugs affecting relationships and the Edit Person modal.

### Fixed

- **Custom relationship types writing to wrong property** (#319): Adding adopted child, step-child, foster child, or ward via the Add Relationship modal incorrectly wrote to the `children` property instead of the type-specific property (`adopted_child`, `step_child`, etc.).

- **Context menu items silently failing** (#320): 16 missing imports in the context menu module caused various menu items (including "Calculate relationship") to do nothing when clicked. An audit identified and added all missing imports, preventing further occurrences.

- **Clearing fields in Edit Person not saving** (#322): Clearing a date or occupation field in the Edit Person modal appeared to save (success notice shown) but the change wasn't persisted. The cleared value was treated as "unchanged" rather than "set to empty."
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.35

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### New feature

**Cross-project research queries** (#303): Two new ways to see all research activity for a person aggregated across projects:
- **Profile View section**: Person profiles now include a "Research activity" section that groups IRNs, log entries, journals, reports, and projects referencing the person by project, with date ranges, type badges, and result indicators. Hidden when empty, lazy-rendered on expand.
- **Command + modal**: "Find related research for person" via the command palette or command menu. If the active note is a person, it opens directly; otherwise a person picker appears first.

### Bug fix

**Built-in relationship additions from context menu** (#318): Adding father, mother, spouse, or child via the right-click context menu silently failed. The `RelationshipManager` import was lost when context menus were extracted from main.ts during a prior refactor. Custom relationships were unaffected. The Add Relationship modal was also fixed to properly delegate built-in family types to `RelationshipManager`.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.34

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
This release delivers comprehensive GEDCOM 5.5.1 field coverage, ensuring near-lossless roundtrip data fidelity between Charted Roots and other genealogy software.

### Comprehensive GEDCOM field coverage (#317)

**New fields (full import + export):**
- **Name components:** NPFX (prefix like "Dr."), NSFX (suffix like "Jr."), SPFX (surname prefix like "von")
- **Person attributes:** TITL, RELI, NATI, DSCR, IDNO, PROP, CAST, NCHI, NMR, SSN — all 10 now export (import already worked)
- **Burial:** BURI.DATE and BURI.PLAC both import to person frontmatter and export
- **Cause of death:** DEAT.CAUS imports to `death_cause` and exports
- **Age at event:** AGE sub-tag stored on event notes and re-exported
- **Nickname:** NICK now exports (import already worked)

**Export roundtrip fixes:**
- Family events (MARR, DIV, MARB, MARC, MARL, MARS, DIVF) now export on FAM records instead of as generic EVEN on individual records
- FAM records include divorce date from spouse relationship metadata
- NAME line constructed from explicit components (e.g., `Dr. Johann /von Müller/ Jr.`)
- Duplicate BIRT/DEAT/BURI/OCCU records eliminated when person-level data already covers them
- OCCU exports as inline value (`1 OCCU Farmer`) instead of NOTE sub-tag
- FROM/TO date ranges now parsed and exported (previously only BET/AND was supported)
- Six missing event type export mappings added: MARB, MARC, MARL, MARS, DIVF, CHRA
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.33

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
This release improves tree chart readability, fixes GEDCOM import issues, and refines the Book Builder person picker.

### Changed

- **Default root person shows picker** (#308): The person picker now always opens when selecting a Book Builder template, with the default root person pre-selected in the search field. Previously, the picker was skipped entirely when a default was found, with no way to choose a different person.

### Fixed

- **Tree charts scaling down nodes** (#310): All four chart layouts (pedigree, descendant, hourglass, fan) now expand the canvas to fit content at full size, so names remain fully readable regardless of tree size. This applies to both Book Builder tree chapters and standalone visual tree PDF exports.
- **Record superlatives with fictional negative years** (#312): Fictional dates with era prefixes and negative years (e.g., `DE -90`) were parsed as positive values.
- **GEDCOM import spouse ID replacement and divorce dates** (#314): Three additional issues fixed in the GEDCOM marriage import: indexed `spouse[N]_id` values were not being converted from GEDCOM refs to CR IDs, divorce dates were silently dropped due to double-conversion, and an empty legacy `spouse:` field was written alongside indexed properties.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.32

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
This release focuses on Book Builder stability, bug fixes, and a GEDCOM import improvement.

### Added

- **Default root person for Book Builder** (#308): Templates now auto-select the root person from the active note or the person marked `root_person: true`, so you don't have to search each time.

### Fixed

- **Book Builder button in Control Center** (#307): The "Open book builder" button was passing incorrect arguments, causing it to silently fail.
- **Book Builder missing ancestor/family group chapters** (#309): Template-generated ancestor summaries and family group sheets were blank when fact-level source tracking was disabled, due to the person cache not being loaded.
- **Book Builder saving extra markdown files** (#311): Generating a PDF book also saved each report chapter as a standalone markdown file in the vault.
- **Unreadable tree charts in PDF output** (#310): Visual tree charts used white text on light pastel backgrounds. Text now uses dark tones matching the report palette, and long names are clipped to card boundaries.
- **Record superlatives ignoring years before 1000** (#312): Years like `800` were ignored and negative years like `-1000` had their sign stripped. Now supports 1+ digit years and negative (BCE) years.
- **Gaps report crash in report wizard** (#306): The wizard was missing gaps-specific options, causing a crash when generating gaps reports.
- **Negative findings not scanning research reports** (#305): The negative findings and research timeline parsers now scan `research_report` and `research_project` notes in addition to `research_journal` notes.
- **GEDCOM import dropping marriage date/place** (#314): The importer now maps `FAM.MARR.DATE` and `FAM.MARR.PLAC` to indexed `spouse[N]_marriage_date` and `spouse[N]_marriage_location` frontmatter properties.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.31

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
Bug fix release addressing two issues reported by users.

### Fixed

- **Gaps report crash** (#306): The report wizard was missing gaps-specific options (`fieldsToCheck`, research level filters, max items per category) that existed in the old report modal, causing a `cannot read properties of undefined` crash when generating gaps reports. The wizard now includes the full set of gaps report configuration options.

- **Negative findings not scanning research reports** (#305): The negative findings block and research timeline processors only scanned `research_journal` notes for markdown `→ negative` entries. Users who log findings in `research_report` or `research_project` notes were not seeing their negative findings. Both processors now scan all three note types.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.30

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
This is a hotfix release addressing issues with the historical context overlay introduced in v0.20.28.

**Context lifespan filtering fix** (#304): Context events were being filtered to a narrow ±5 year window around the person's lifespan, which excluded historical events outside that range (e.g., a 19th century war wouldn't appear on a timeline for someone born in 1975). The default is now no filtering; all events from the context note are shown. A new `contextLifespanMargin` setting and per-block `contextMargin` parameter allow users to optionally limit context events to within N years of the person's dates.

**Context note format relaxed** (#304): Lines in context notes no longer require a bullet prefix. Both `- 1861-1865: American Civil War` and `1861-1865: American Civil War` are now accepted.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.29

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
This release adds historical context overlays and age annotations to person timelines.

**Historical context overlay** (#296): Timelines can now display historical events alongside a person's life events, helping you understand the world around your ancestors. Create a context note with date-prefixed list items (e.g., `- 1861-1865: American Civil War`) and reference it in a timeline block with `context: [[Note]]`. Context events are automatically filtered to the person's lifespan and rendered with muted styling and a landmark icon. Set a default context note for all timelines in Settings > Advanced > Default timeline context, or suppress it on individual blocks with `context: none`.

**Age annotations**: All timeline events now display the person's age at the time of each event when a birth date is known.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.28

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
This release adds book builder entry points and fixes book generation issues introduced in 0.20.26.

**Book builder entry points** (#294): The book builder is now accessible from the Control Center's Trees & Reports tab as a dedicated button, and from a new "Books & compilation" category in the command menu. Previously it was only available via the command palette.

**Bug fixes** (#302): Fixed `downloadBook is not a function` error when generating a book. Also fixed blank content in template-derived chapters (ancestors, family groups, reference pages) where the generation service wasn't correctly mapping subject IDs to the field names expected by each report type.

**Documentation**: Added comprehensive documentation for all recent features: book builder wiki page, research timeline dynamic block, record superlatives, brick wall report, unconnected people finder, kinship report, and a new book system developer doc.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.27

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
This release introduces the book builder, a major new feature for compiling family history documents from your vault data.

**Book and narrative compilation** (#294): A new book builder accessible via the "Open book builder" command that lets you assemble reports, visual trees, vault notes, and section dividers into a single PDF or ODT document. A 4-step wizard guides you through book metadata, chapter selection with drag-and-drop ordering, output configuration, and generation with progress tracking. Three starter templates (family history book, research compilation, blank) auto-populate chapters from your family graph -- generating individual summaries for direct-line ancestors and family group sheets for each nuclear family. Books include a cover page, table of contents, consolidated bibliography, and name index. Save your book definition as a `.book.json` file and regenerate it as your vault data changes, with change detection that reports which chapters were updated.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.26

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
This release adds a new research timeline visualization, record superlatives to the statistics dashboard, and removes artificial limits from report configuration.

**Research timeline dynamic code block** (#293): A new `charted-roots-research-timeline` code block that surfaces all research activity across your vault with three view modes. The default **table** view shows a chronological activity log with a gap detection column that flags periods of inactivity. The **heatmap** view renders a GitHub-style contribution grid showing 52 weeks of research activity density. The **timeline** view displays horizontal bars per person or project with color-coded result markers and highlighted gap regions. Supports filtering by person and project, configurable gap threshold, grouping, and freeze-to-markdown.

**Record superlatives** (#301): A new "Record superlatives" section in the statistics dashboard highlighting notable individuals: oldest people, youngest deaths, most children, most marriages, longest marriages, earliest births, most recent deaths, and most documented people. Rendered as a card grid with ranked entries and clickable links.

**Report configuration improvements** (#297, #300): The generation limit in the report wizard and the max degree selector in the kinship report are now free numeric inputs instead of fixed dropdowns, with no upper limit.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.25

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Research & Network Analysis Reports

Three new report types for understanding your family network:

**Brick wall report** (#297): Identifies end-of-line ancestors where research has hit a dead end. Traverses the ancestor tree using Sosa-Stradonitz (Ahnentafel) numbering and lists every terminal ancestor with generation, lineage path, source count, and research level. Includes tree completeness statistics per generation.

**Unconnected people finder** (#298): Finds person notes not linked to a selected person's family network. Uses connected component analysis to distinguish completely isolated records from small disconnected clusters. Shows network coverage percentage and groups results by cluster.

**Kinship report** (#300): Lists all relatives of a person with proper genealogical relationship terms (cousins with removals, in-laws, great-grandparents, etc.) and degree of relationship. Includes blood vs. marriage breakdown and category summaries.

All three reports are available as markdown, PDF, and ODT via the report wizard.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.24

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### Added

- **Negative findings view** (#287): New `charted-roots-negative-findings` dynamic code block that surfaces all negative research results across the vault in one place. Parses two formats: frontmatter on `research_log_entry` notes with `result: negative`, and markdown entries in research journals matching `> negative`. Renders a table with date, source, searched-for text, project, and person columns. Supports optional grouping by person, project, or source (`group: person`), date sorting, project/person filtering, and freeze-to-markdown.

### Fixed

- **Custom relationship duplication in Profile View** (#289): Custom relationship types with a `familyGraphMapping` (e.g., "Sire" mapped to parent) no longer appear twice, once under their custom name in Other and again as a generic label in Family. The Family section now suppresses entries already covered by a custom type.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.23

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### Command Menu ([#290](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/290))

A searchable launcher that puts all 56 plugin commands at your fingertips. Open it with the **"Open command menu"** command (assign any hotkey you like) or from the new tile on the control center dashboard. Commands are organized into 6 categories (Create, View, Edit, Trees & numbering, Bases, and Tools) with fuzzy search that filters across both names and categories.

### Sourced Facts in Edit Person Modal ([#292](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/292))

You can now add per-fact source citations directly from the Edit Person modal. No more manual frontmatter editing. A new collapsible "Source tracking" section shows the 10 trackable facts (birth date/place, death date/place, parents, spouse, marriage date/place, occupation, residence) with a source picker for each. Changes immediately update the coverage score in data quality reports. This section only appears when fact-level source tracking is enabled in Settings > Advanced > Research tools.

### Bug Fixes

- **Custom relationship types in Profile View** ([#289](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/289)): Custom relationship types with a family graph mapping (e.g., "Sire" > parent) now correctly appear in the Other section with their custom names instead of being silently dropped.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.22

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### Added

- **Link to existing event from context menu** ([#288](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/288)): Right-click a person or source note > Events > "Link to existing event" to search for and attach the entity to an existing event's frontmatter.

- **Array pronouns with chip-style input** ([#291](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/291)): The edit person modal now stores pronouns as a YAML array. A chip-style input with preset suggestions (she/her, he/him, they/them) and free-text entry replaces the old single text field. Existing string-format pronouns are read seamlessly and normalized to array on next save.

### Fixed

- **Cannot select roles in Manage Members modal** ([#286](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/286)): Replaced the broken autocomplete dropdown with clickable role chips rendered directly below the input.

- **Profile View relationship duplication** ([#289](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/289)): Relationships with a `familyGraphMapping` (e.g., godparent) no longer leak into both the Family and Other subsections. Direct + inverse relationship duplicates are now removed automatically.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.21

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

### Fixed

- **Inferred relationship duplicates with gendered family properties** ([#285](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/285)): Using `father`/`mother` instead of `parents` in frontmatter no longer produces spurious inferred "Parent" entries in the Relationships tab. The deduplication now treats gendered family properties (`father`, `mother`, `stepfather`, `stepmother`, `adoptive_father`, `adoptive_mother`) as equivalent to their corresponding relationship types (`parents`, `step_parent`, `adoptive_parent`).

- **Role suggest dropdown unclickable in Manage Members modal** ([#286](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/286)): The role autocomplete dropdown in the organization Manage Members modal couldn't be clicked because a CSS overflow container was clipping the popover. Removed the nested scroll container so the suggest dropdown renders properly.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.20

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Entity Profile View: Phase 2 & 3 (#251)

### Phase 2: Inline Editing

Click-to-edit for identity header fields across all five entity types. Click any value to edit in-place -- Enter/blur saves to frontmatter, Escape cancels.

- Text, number, and select (dropdown) inputs
- Editable fields: name/title, dates, birth place, occupation, sex, place category, coordinates, event/source type, repository/seat
- Empty fields show clickable placeholders
- Property alias support and wikilink requoting
- Self-modify guard prevents redundant re-renders

### Phase 3: Polish

- **Lazy section rendering**: content deferred until first expand via `contentRenderer` callback
- **Keyboard navigation**: WAI-ARIA accordion pattern: ArrowUp/Down between sections, Enter/Space to toggle, Home/End to jump
- **Mobile-responsive layout**: 44px touch targets, narrow-pane media query (stacked metadata, hidden summaries below 400px)
- **Embedded Leaflet map**: place profiles show an interactive map with marker; lazy init on expand, cleanup on collapse; "Open in Geo Map" passes focus coordinates

### Bug Fixes

- Relationship names now resolve from personIndex instead of showing raw cr_ids
- Duplicate family entries filtered from Other Relationships subsection
- Private service access patterns corrected
- CSS class names reconciled to match TypeScript DOM output
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.19

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## What's New

**Entity Profile View -- Phase 1 read-only** ([#251](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/251))

A new dockable sidebar view that auto-syncs to the active note, displaying a comprehensive profile for any entity type.

### Highlights

- **Five entity types supported** -- Person, Place, Event, Source, and Organization profiles with entity-specific sections
- **Collapsible sections** -- Relationships (family + custom), events, sources, media, data quality, participants, members, map preview, and referenced facts
- **Sticky identity header** -- Entity type badge, avatar (for persons), key metadata, and quick actions
- **Pin & unpin** -- Freeze the profile on a specific entity while navigating elsewhere; open multiple profile instances for side-by-side comparison
- **Breadcrumb navigation** -- Click entity names within sections to navigate in-place with full history tracking
- **State persistence** -- Section expand/collapse states, pinned entity, and breadcrumb history survive across Obsidian sessions
- **Context menu integration** -- Right-click any entity note → "Open profile" for quick access

### How to use

1. Open the command palette (`Ctrl/Cmd + P`)
2. Type "Charted Roots: Open entity profile"
3. Navigate to any person, place, event, source, or organization note -- the profile follows automatically

### Documentation

- [Entity Profile View (Wiki)](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/wiki/Entity-Profile-View)
- [Implementation Details (Developer)](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/docs/developer/implementation/profile-view.md)
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.18

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## What's New

### Structured Role Lists for Organizations (#274)

New `roles` property on organization notes defines valid roles and display order. Membership modals now show autocomplete suggestions from the organization's roles, with freeform entry still supported. Organization types can define default role templates that auto-populate when creating new organizations. The `charted-roots-members` block uses a 3-level fallback for role ordering: block `role-order` → org `roles` → alphabetical.

### Mills-Aligned Source Classification (#276)

Three optional classification axes from Mills' *Evidence Explained* for GPS-oriented genealogists:

| Property | Values | Question |
|----------|--------|----------|
| `source_classification` | `original`, `derivative`, `authored_narrative` | What is the document? |
| `information_classification` | `primary`, `secondary`, `undetermined` | Who provided the info? |
| `evidence_classification` | `direct`, `indirect`, `negative` | How does it relate to the question? |

Collapsible classification section in Create Source modal. Integrates with evidence analysis and conditionally displays in Source Summary, Sources by Role, and PDF reports. Template snippets updated for census, vital record, and full templates.

### Bug Fixes

- **Create Family modal sets only one parent on children** (#277): Both parents now written in a single frontmatter call per child
- **Duplicate relationships shown in Defined vs Inferred** (#285): Inferred filter now correctly excludes bidirectionally-defined relationships
- **Unknown-sex gray too light** (#280): Darkened for better contrast

### Other

- **Extractions dynamic block** (#284): `charted-roots-extractions` code block for source notes showing all citing entities
- **Research Bases template** (#231): 12 pre-configured views for research workflow entities
- **Family chart empty state** (#282): Improved wording
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.17

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## What's New

### Extractions dynamic block for source notes ([#284](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/284))

New `charted-roots-extractions` code block answers "what have I extracted from this source?" Place it in any source note to see a reverse lookup of all citing entities, rendered in three sections:

- **Persons**: with fact-level citation details (e.g., Name, Birth date)
- **Events**: with type, date, person(s), and place columns
- **Places**: unique places derived from citing events, with event counts

Supports freeze-to-markdown and live updates on metadata changes.

### Research Bases template ([#231](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/231))

New "Create research base template" command generates a `research.base` file with 12 pre-configured views for managing GPS research workflow entities -- projects, reports, individual research notes, journals, and log entries. Includes per-type views, status grouping, in-progress/completed filters, negative findings, and private items. Also available from the Data Quality tab dropdown and the "Create all base templates" command.

## Bug Fixes

- **Create Family modal sets only one parent on children** ([#277](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/277)): Consolidated two separate `processFrontMatter` calls into one per child, fixing a race condition where the second call could overwrite the first, leaving children with only one parent or none.

- **Duplicate relationships in Defined vs Inferred** ([#285](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/285)): When both sides of a relationship were explicitly defined, the inverse inference engine generated a duplicate. The inferred-only filter now correctly deduplicates against existing defined relationships.

**Full changelog:** [0.20.15...0.20.16](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/compare/0.20.15...0.20.16)
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.16

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### Bug fixes

- **Family chart now resolves wikilink-based relationships** ([#281](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/281)): The family chart wasn't wiring up the service that resolves wikilinks like `father: [[Name]]` to their cr_ids, so people using wikilink-based relationships appeared as isolated cards with no connections. All relationship types now resolve correctly.

- **Family chart renders non-binary and unknown sex with distinct colors** ([#280](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/280)): People with non-binary or unknown sex values were incorrectly shown as male. They now render with distinct colors (muted gold for non-binary, gray for unknown) across all card styles and exports.

- **Sources block shows title instead of filename, and displays fact labels** ([#278](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/278)): The `charted-roots-sources` block now uses the source note's `title` property when available, and correctly reads fact-level sources from both the flat `sourced_*` properties and the legacy `sourced_facts` format.

- **Bidirectional linker no longer duplicates indexed spouse entries** ([#283](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/283)): Manually adding `spouse1: "[[Name]]"` without the corresponding `spouse1_id` no longer causes the linker to create a duplicate entry at the next slot.

### UI improvements

- Renamed "Family" dashboard tile to "Create Family" and "Canvas Trees" to "Generate Canvas Tree" for clarity.
- The family chart's empty state now says "Choose from list" instead of "Select a person" to avoid confusion with Obsidian's file selection model ([#282](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/282)).
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.15

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## What's new

- **`charted-roots-sources` dynamic block** (#278): Person notes can now include a `charted-roots-sources` code block that renders a table of all linked sources — gathered from both the general `sources` array and fact-level `sourced_*` properties. Each row shows the source type icon, a clickable title, the date, and which facts the source supports. Configurable via `title`, `sort`, `filter`, and `exclude` options, with freeze-to-markdown support.

## Fixes

- **Freezing dynamic blocks fails on person notes** (#279): The freeze-to-markdown feature could not find the code block when it used the current `charted-roots-*` naming rather than the legacy `canvas-roots-*` form. The freeze function now recognizes either prefix. Template snippets have also been updated to use the current naming convention.

- **Non-binary and unknown sex colors missing from charts** (#280): People with non-binary or intersex sex values appeared with the default unknown color because the chart renderers checked for pre-normalization strings instead of the canonical `X` value. All three renderers (canvas, tree preview, SVG export) now display the correct color, and SVG export gains a distinct muted gold for non-binary.

- **Family wizard corrupts existing notes with duplicate names** (#277): Creating children whose names matched existing people caused the wizard to overwrite the wrong person's parent fields. The wizard now suspends bidirectional syncing during batch creation and uses file-aware wikilinks to avoid misresolution.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.14

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### Added

- **Media block for event, place, and source notes** (#269): The `charted-roots-media` gallery block is now inserted into event, place, and source notes that have media attachments -- both during Gramps/GEDCOM import and via the "Insert dynamic blocks" command. Previously only person notes received a media block.

### Fixed

- **Members block `role-order` config ignored** (#268): The `role-order` config option on `charted-roots-members` blocks was silently discarded. Role groups now display in the user-specified order.

- **Relationship calculator shows "Blood Relation: Yes" for in-laws** (#270): Paths through a spouse link incorrectly reported blood relation as "Yes." Now correctly determined by the absence of spouse links.

- **Non-person notes appearing in People tab** (#271): Notes with unrecognized `cr_type` values (e.g., `hex` for hex map tiles) that also had a `cr_id` were incorrectly detected as person notes. The detection now rejects any note with an explicit type property, even if unrecognized.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.13

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### What's new

- **Members block: `show-notes` and `role-order` options** (#268) -- The `charted-roots-members` code block now supports `show-notes: true` to display membership notes alongside each member, and `role-order: Priest, Knight, ...` to control the display order of role groups instead of defaulting to alphabetical.

### Bug fixes

- **Gramps import: place filename mismatch** (#259) -- When importing places with parent suffixes for disambiguation (e.g., "Pennsylvania USA"), wikilinks pointed to files that didn't exist because the place note writer derived its own shorter filename. Wikilinks now match the actual files created.

- **Members block: missing role headings** (#264) -- Organizations with members in only one role silently hid the role heading, and single-value membership properties stored as plain strings instead of arrays caused role data to be dropped. Both issues are now fixed.

- **Edit place modal: wikilink array corruption** (#263) -- Editing a place note could corrupt user-defined frontmatter arrays containing wikilinks (e.g., Factions, Districts) into unreadable nested objects. This happened because Obsidian's YAML serializer strips quotes from `[[wikilink]]` values, which YAML then reinterprets as nested arrays. A post-save step now re-quotes any bare wikilinks to prevent corruption.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.12

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### What's new

- **Gramps tag import** -- Gramps tags (e.g., "notable", "needs review") are now imported as standard Obsidian tags on person, event, place, and source notes (#267)
- **Organization members dynamic block** -- A new `charted-roots-members` code block renders members grouped by role with clickable links and date ranges, with live updates and freeze-to-markdown support (#268)

### Bug fixes

- **Family chart broken positions** -- Fixed `translate(undefined, undefined)` errors in the family-chart library that caused cards to render in broken positions (#257)
- **GEDCOM duplicate-name linking** -- Spouses, children, and step-parents now link to the correct person when multiple people share the same name. Also fixed the bidirectional sync firing during import and adding children to the wrong parent (#258)
- **Gramps place import duplicates** -- Fixed duplicate state files (e.g., "Iowa USA.md" and "Iowa USA 1.md") and broken place wikilinks caused by multiple map entries resolving to the same file name (#259)
- **Duplicate person in event displays** -- The event edit modal no longer writes a separate `person` property that duplicates the `persons` array (#261)
- **Place edit corrupting frontmatter** -- Editing a place note no longer destroys user-defined array properties like Factions or Districts (#263)
- **Inferred relationships filter** -- The "Inferred only" filter in the Relationships tab now returns results (#264)
- **Organization base template members** — Members added via Manage Members are now visible in the organization base template (#264)
- **Research needed card** -- Fixed the command not appearing for typed person notes, the card being empty on first visit, text-typed `needs_research` not being recognized, and a crash when place notes were queried (#266)
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.11

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### Added

- **Gramps tag import** -- Gramps tags (e.g., "notable", "needs review") are now imported as standard Obsidian `tags` arrays on person, event, place, and source notes (#267)

### Fixed

- **Family chart card positions** -- Fixed broken card rendering caused by invalid SVG transforms during animation (#257)
- **Gramps place hierarchy** -- Places using `<placeref>` chains (e.g., County → State → Country) now import with full hierarchy instead of flat single-level names (#259)
- **Duplicate people in events** -- Event edit modal no longer creates duplicate person entries in event listings (#261)
- **Place edit corrupting frontmatter** -- Editing a place note no longer corrupts user-defined array properties like Factions or Districts (#263)
- **Inferred relationships filter** -- The "Inferred only" filter on the Relationships tab now returns results as expected (#264)
- **Organization member sync** -- Members added via Manage Members are now visible in the organization base template (#264)
- **GEDCOM duplicate name wikilinks** -- Importing people with identical names (e.g., multiple "William Hurst" individuals) now correctly links spouses, children, and step-parents to the right person files (#258)
- **Research needed for person notes** -- The "Add research question" command and Data Quality "Research needed" card now work correctly for person notes (#266)
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.10

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### Added

- **Copy birth/death date from event to person note** ([#262](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/262)): After creating or editing a birth or death event, a clickable notice offers to copy the event date to the linked person's `born` or `died` property. Skips the prompt if the date already matches.

### Fixed

- **CC click-row edit losing spouse/children data** ([#257](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/257)): Opening the Edit Person modal from Control Center's People tab showed raw `cr_id` values instead of names for spouses and children, and corrupted data on save.

- **Research level showing "Not assessed" despite value in frontmatter** ([#257](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/257)): Both edit paths failed to pass `research_level` to the Edit Person modal. Now reads from frontmatter and pre-fills correctly.

- **Self-referential father when adding same-name child** ([#257](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/257)): When a parent and child shared the same name, the bidirectional linker could resolve the child's wikilink back to the parent's own file. Added self-reference guards and excluded the current note from person picker results.

- **Person count off-by-one between views** ([#257](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/257)): VaultStatsService was not excluding proof summary and universe notes from person counts, while FamilyGraphService was.

- **Place type customizations not working** ([#263](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/263)): The Create Place modal's type dropdown was hardcoded and ignored settings. Now reads from settings and respects all customizations.

- **Duplicate person entries in event displays** ([#261](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/261)): When an event had the same person in both `person` and `persons` fields, they appeared twice in timeline displays.

- **Gramps import creating broken place wikilinks for US states** ([#259](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/259)): State abbreviations (e.g., "IA") were not expanded to full names, causing mismatched wikilinks.

### Refactored

- **Consolidate US state abbreviation maps** ([#265](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/265)): Replaced 4 duplicate `US_STATE_ABBREVIATIONS` maps with a single import from `place-name-normalizer.ts`.

### Diagnostics

- **GEDCOM spouse linking investigation** ([#258](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/258)): Added console logging to trace spouse relationship building during GEDCOM import.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.9

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Import & Family Wizard Fixes

This release fixes several bugs in GEDCOM/Gramps import and the Family Wizard.

### Fixed

#### Family Wizard (#257)
- **Children array missing names**: The `children` array could have fewer entries than `children_id` when creating families. Fixed incorrect property name in helper function.
- **Spouse field preserving corrupt data**: When arrays had mismatched lengths, corrupt wikilink data persisted. Now clears both fields when arrays don't match.
- **Edit Person modal**: "Family relationships" panel showed "Click 'Link' to select" even when parent relationships existed.

#### GEDCOM Import (#258, #260)
- **Parent claim conflicts**: When a child appeared in multiple GEDCOM families (remarriage, adoption), multiple parents would claim the same child. Now matches Gramps behavior -- only adds children whose `fatherRef`/`motherRef` matches the current person.
- **Source notes not importing**: Notes stored as references (`@N001@`) weren't resolved to text content. Also added `gedcom_id` property and source type inference.

#### Gramps Import (#259)
- **Missing state-level places**: Place collection skipped single-part names like "Illinois" or "USA". Now collects all places including those from person/event records.

---

**Full Changelog:** [v0.20.7...v0.20.8](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/compare/v0.20.7...v0.20.8)
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.8

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
This release fixes two bugs introduced in v0.20.6:

### Data Quality Tools Missing (#256)

The new "Research needed" section could throw errors that prevented the rest of the Data Quality tab from rendering. Added error handling to ensure vault-wide analysis, batch operations, and data tools always appear.

### Marriage Location Not Saved as Wikilink (#255)

When selecting a place from the picker for marriage location, the value was saved as a plain string instead of a wikilink. Now saves both the wikilink format and the `spouse{n}_marriage_location_id` property for reliable resolution.

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#0207---2026-02-08
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.7

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Needs-Research Tagging

Flag person, event, or place notes for additional research using the `needs_research` frontmatter property. This is part of **Phase 2 of the GPS Research Workflow Integration** ([#230](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/230)).

### New Features

- **`needs_research` property** -- Add research questions to any person, event, or place note:
  ```yaml
  needs_research:
    - "Find birth certificate"
    - "Verify marriage date"
  ```

- **"Research needed" card** in Data Quality tab -- Shows all entities flagged for research with:
  - Summary stats (total entities, questions, counts by type)
  - Clickable entity names that open the note
  - Question badges with truncation for long text

- **Command palette command** -- "Add research question to current note" opens a modal to quickly add questions to the active person/event/place note

### Usage

1. Open a person, event, or place note
2. Use command palette: `Add research question to current note`
3. Enter your research question
4. View all flagged entities in Control Center → Data Quality → "Research needed" card

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#0206---2026-02-07
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.6

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
This release improves internationalization for place lookups and fixes a bug that could cause unexpected frontmatter modifications.

### Changed

- **GEDCOM import no longer adds placeholder descriptions** ([#250](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/250)): Events imported from GEDCOM without a description now have an empty description field instead of "Imported from GEDCOM", reducing timeline clutter.

- **Wikidata place lookup now uses Obsidian's locale**: Place searches via Wikidata now respect your Obsidian language setting instead of hardcoded English. Dutch users will see Dutch place names, German users will see German names, etc. Falls back to English when translations are unavailable.

### Fixed

- **Bidirectional linker adding relationship fields to non-person files** ([#253](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/253)): When linking family relationships, the sync methods now verify that target files have a `cr_id` before adding relationship properties. Previously, any file matching a link target could receive unexpected frontmatter fields.

- **Partial matching in relationship removal causing unintended deletions** ([#253](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/253)): The `removeFromArrayField` method used substring matching which could match "John" inside "Johnny". Now uses exact wikilink target/display name matching to prevent accidental removal of similarly-named entries.

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/compare/0.20.4...0.20.5
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.5

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## CSS cleanup and GEDCOM import fix

### Bug fix

- **GEDCOM event notes** - Fixed an issue where event notes imported from GEDCOM files caused malformed frontmatter, preventing events from appearing on person timelines. Notes are now placed in a separate section in the body of the event file.

### Maintenance (thanks @Julschik!)

- **Border-radius design tokens** - Added `--cr-radius-xs/sm/md/lg` CSS variables and standardized 295 hardcoded values across the codebase. No visual changes; improves maintainability for future theming.
- **Spinner consolidation** - Merged 5 duplicate `@keyframes` spinner definitions into a single shared `cr-spin` animation. No visual changes.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.4

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### Map View improvements and GEDCOM import fix

- **Better marker distinction** - Place markers on the Map View now appear as hollow teal circles, while event markers remain solid. This makes it much easier to tell them apart when both the "All places" layer and event layers are enabled. Event markers also always render on top of place markers.
- **GEDCOM event notes fix** - Notes attached to events (like occupation notes) now import correctly. Previously, notes that referenced shared GEDCOM note records showed the reference code instead of the actual note text.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.3

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### Improved discoverability for dockable sidebar views

The dockable views introduced in v0.20.0 are now easier to find:

- **New Dashboard section** - A "Dockable views" tile grid on the Dashboard tab provides one-click access to all 9 sidebar views (People, Places, Events, Sources, Organizations, Relationships, Universes, Collections, Data Quality)
- **Always-visible dock buttons** - The dock icons on Control Center card headers are now visible at 60% opacity instead of requiring hover

### Maintenance

- Removed unused `d3-dag` dependency (thanks @Julschik)
- Improved type safety in place lookup flow (thanks @Julschik)
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.2

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### Fixed

- **Sources dockable view stuck on "Loading sources..."** — The new Sources sidebar view from v0.20.0 failed to load due to incorrect service initialization. Thanks to @prentissw for identifying this in #241.

- **Media not rendering for filenames with commas** — Images with commas in their filename (e.g., `[[Marvick, Jeanne.JPG]]`) were not displaying in media blocks or on the family chart. The YAML parser was splitting the filename on the comma, causing the file to not be found. Thanks to @jeff962 for reporting in #238.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.1

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Dockable Sidebar Views

### Highlights

**Dockable Sidebar Views**: Nine Control Center tabs can now be opened as persistent, dockable views in the Obsidian sidebar. Hover over a card header to reveal the dock button, or use the command palette. Views include filter/sort/search controls, context menus, and auto-refresh on vault changes. State persists across sessions.

Available dockable views:
- **People** — filterable/sortable person list with expandable details
- **Places** — place notes with category badges and coordinates
- **Events** — timeline table with type/person/date filters
- **Sources** — sources list with type and confidence badges
- **Organizations** — organizations with type badges and member counts
- **Relationships** — relationship table with type/category filters
- **Universes** — universe list with status badges and entity counts
- **Collections** — browse mode switcher (all people / detected families / user collections)
- **Data quality** — read-only dashboard with research gaps, source conflicts, and vault-wide analysis

**Relationships tab enhancements**: Filter dropdowns (by type, category, person), sort options, pagination with load-more, and context menus on rows.

### Bug fixes

- **Media parsing fix**: Avatars on the family chart now display correctly when media references in frontmatter use Obsidian Link objects or nested arrays (#238)

### Other changes

- Removed 3 legacy Control Center tabs (Status, Guide, Statistics) that were superseded by the Dashboard tab, reducing the total from 17 to 14

### Technical notes

- The monolithic `control-center.ts` (13,760 lines) has been decomposed into independent component files, reducing the modal shell to ~1,451 lines (89% reduction). Each tab now lives in its own file with a consistent `render*Tab()` API and typed options interface. (#239, #240)
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.20.0

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### Fixed
- **Drag-to-reorder broken in editable media blocks** (#236): In `canvas-roots-media` blocks with `editable: true`, dragging images to reorder them stopped working after the lightbox fix in v0.19.19. Drag-and-drop now works correctly again.
- **Schema validation including non-person notes** (#237): When using the "All people" scope, schema validation was incorrectly including events, sources, places, and other entity types. Now correctly validates only person notes.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.19.22

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### Added
- **Media gallery context menu** (#234): Right-click images in media blocks to access "Open in Obsidian" (enables compatibility with plugins like Image Metadata) and "Open in new tab" options.

### Fixed
- **Gramps import using wrong birth/death dates** (#233): When importing from Gramps, if a person appeared in multiple birth events (their own birth plus others' births where they were a parent or witness), the wrong date could be imported. Now correctly preserves the person's own birth/death data.
- **Extract events from source creates broken source link** (#235): Source links now use the display title instead of the slugified filename.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.19.21

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

This release fixes several issues reported by users.

### Changed

- **Event icon display mode options renamed** (#184): The setting options are now clearer: "Text label", "Icon (with tooltip)", and "Icon with label".

### Fixes
