- **Custom organization types now appear in statistics** (#225): Organizations with custom types were being counted but not displayed in the Organizations tab statistics breakdown.

- **Timeline sentences preserved in icon-only mode** (#184): The event type verb (Born, Married, etc.) is no longer removed from timeline sentences when using icon-only display mode.

- **Member list updates reliably after editing** (#226): The Manage Members modal now properly waits for Obsidian's cache to update before refreshing, fixing issues where members would temporarily disappear after editing.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.19.20

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### New Features

#### Research Report Export (#229)
Export your markdown research report notes as professionally formatted PDF or ODT documents. Unlike other reports that generate content from structured genealogical data, this new export takes an existing markdown note and renders it with professional formatting.

**To use:**
1. Create a research report note with `cr_type: research_report` in the frontmatter
2. Open the Report Wizard (Control Center > Reports)
3. Select "Research report export" under the Research category
4. Choose your note and export format (PDF or ODT)

**Features:**
- Converts markdown headings, lists, tables, blockquotes, and inline formatting
- Preserves footnotes (as endnotes in PDF, native document footnotes in ODT)
- Optional cover page with custom title/subtitle
- Available alongside other research reports like Source Summary and Media Inventory

#### PDF Table Formatting Options (#228)
Two new options in PDF exports improve table readability across page breaks:
- **Keep table rows together** - prevents rows from breaking mid-content
- **Repeat table headers** - shows column headers on continuation pages

#### Footnote Preservation (#227)
Obsidian reference-style footnotes (`[^1]` with `[^1]: content` definitions) are now preserved in PDF and ODT exports. ODT uses native document footnotes; PDF collects them as endnotes in a "Notes" section.

### Bug Fixes

- **Organization management** (#225, #226): Fixed custom organization types being reset to "Other", file explorer context menu opening in wrong mode, and membership data corruption when editing
- **Media lightbox mode switch** (#232): Clicking images in `canvas-roots-media` code blocks no longer switches notes from Reading to Edit mode
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.19.19

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Organization Member Management

This release adds a dedicated modal for managing organization memberships, making it easy to add multiple members at once and edit membership details inline.

### New Features

- **Manage Members Modal**: Right-click any organization and select "Manage members..." to open a dedicated member management interface
- **Multi-Select Person Picker**: Add multiple people to an organization at once with checkbox selection, search filtering, and keyboard navigation
- **Inline Membership Editing**: Edit role, rank, status, and dates directly within the member list without opening separate modals
- **File Explorer Integration**: Right-click organization notes in the file explorer to access Edit, Manage Members, and Open in Organizations tab actions

### Fixes

- **Custom organization types not working** (#225): Organizations with custom types were not appearing in the Control Center, and custom types were missing from the Edit Organization modal dropdown.

### Technical Notes

- New `MembershipService` for reading/writing membership data across person notes
- `MultiSelectPersonPickerModal` is a reusable component for future bulk-add scenarios
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.19.18

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### Unified Place Lookup

Look up place information from external geographic databases when creating place notes. Search Wikidata, GeoNames, and OpenStreetMap/Nominatim for place data, then auto-populate coordinates, place type, and administrative hierarchy with a single click.

**Features:**
- "Look up place" button in Create Place modal header
- Source selection chips to choose which databases to query
- Command palette command "Look up place" for standalone searches
- Rate limiting respects API usage policies
- Wikidata and OpenStreetMap work immediately; GeoNames requires free username registration (Settings → Places)

### Bug Fix

- **Crash with nested YAML in frontmatter** (#221): Plugin no longer crashes with `value.startsWith is not a function` when frontmatter contains nested objects in relationship fields.

---

See [CHANGELOG](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md) for full details.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.19.17

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Person Roles in Sources

Track the roles people play in source documents (principal, witness, informant, official, etc.) to support FAN network research and information quality assessment.

### Features

- **Seven canonical role categories:** `principals`, `witnesses`, `informants`, `officials`, `enslaved_individuals`, `family`, `others`
- **Inline YAML notation:** `"[[Person|Person (Role details)]]"` for readability
- **Dynamic block:** `charted-roots-source-roles` renders a role table with person links
- **Context menu:** Right-click on source notes to insert the roles block
- **Modal UI:** Assign roles when linking people via Create/Edit Source modal
- **Sources by Role report:** New Control Center report showing all sources where a person appears, with grouping by role, source, or chronological order

### Example YAML

    principals:
      - "[[John Smith Sr.|John Smith Sr. (Decedent)]]"
    officials:
      - "[[Thomas Brown|Thomas Brown (Administrator)]]"
    enslaved_individuals:
      - "[[Mary]]"
      - "[[Peter]]"

### Bug Fixes

- **Timeline icon baseline alignment** ([#184](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/184)): Event type icons in dynamic timeline blocks now align properly with the text baseline

---

**Full Changelog:** https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md

Community contributors: @ANYroots (original proposal, use cases, terminology), @wilbry (simplified role categories)
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.19.16

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### Added

- **Event type icons for visual views** (#184): New setting to display Lucide icons for event types in timelines and map popups. Choose from text only, icon only, or both.
- **Create event context menu action**: Right-click the Events folder to create events directly.

### Changed

- **Statistics label update** (#216): Renamed "Sex distribution" to "Sex & gender distribution" for clarity.

### Fixed

- **Universe notes counted as people** (#214): Universe notes with `cr_id` no longer appear in People statistics.
- **gender_identity property not recognized** (#215): Now recognized in Sex & Gender Distribution statistics.
- **Places tab error count mismatch** (#213): Data Quality card now correctly displays issue details.
- **Media gallery not displaying after GEDCOM import** (#202): Fixed YAML parsing issues with media wikilinks.
- **Kinship labels persisting during chart navigation** (#195): Labels now properly clear and reappear during tree animations.
- **Data quality not respecting cr_living** (#217): People marked as living are now excluded from "Missing Death Dates".
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.19.15

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### What's new

**Multi-spouse visual cues in family chart** - When viewing a family chart with a person who has multiple spouses, circled numbers (①, ②, etc.) now appear on the connecting lines to show marriage order. This makes it clear who the "hub" person is when spouses are displayed in a row. Enable this by clicking "Show kinship labels" in the chart toolbar. The numbers also appear in exported images (PNG, SVG, PDF).

**Per-spouse marriage metadata** - The Edit Person modal now lets you record marriage details (date, location, status, divorce date) for each spouse individually. Click the calendar icon next to any spouse to expand and enter the information.

### Improvements

- **Settings tab UX**: All sections now start collapsed, and toggling settings no longer resets scroll position
- **Create Person modal UX**: Reduced modal length with inline expansion for optional sections and a sticky footer

### Bug fixes

- Fixed bidirectional relationship normalization incorrectly converting gender-neutral parents and adoptive relationships to mother/father
- Fixed media gallery crash when frontmatter `media` property contains non-string values

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.19.14

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### GEDCOM Media Import (#202)

GEDCOM import now supports media references (OBJE records), bringing it to full parity with Gramps media handling. When importing a GEDCOM file, enable "Media references" in the import wizard to link photos, documents, and other media to your person and event notes.

**Features:**
- Parses top-level OBJE records and inline media on individuals, families, sources, and events
- Resolves external file paths to vault wikilinks (filename-only by default)
- Optional path prefix stripping for users with external media folders
- Live preview in import wizard showing how paths will be converted
- Validates files exist in vault and reports missing media after import
- Adds `media` property to person and event frontmatter

### Bug Fixes

- **Research coverage badge not showing in People tab** (#145): The research coverage badge now correctly appears in the Control Center's People tab when fact-level source tracking is enabled.

- **Create Family wizard creating duplicates for existing people** (#208): When building a family around existing person notes, the wizard now correctly links them instead of creating duplicate notes. The review step now shows separate lists for new notes vs existing notes to link.

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.19.13

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes & Gramps Import Improvements

This release focuses on bug fixes and improvements for users importing data from Gramps.

### What's New

- **Census event type**: Census events from Gramps imports are now recognized as their own event type instead of being lumped into "custom" events. This means census records will display with proper styling, their own icon, and can be filtered or searched separately.

### Bug Fixes

- **Gramps event titles now use your descriptions** (#206): When importing events from Gramps, the plugin now uses your custom event descriptions as the event title. For example, if you named a census event "1880 Census - Living with grandparents" in Gramps, that's what you'll see in Obsidian.

- **Birth/death events on correct timelines** (#183, #207): Birth, death, and similar events imported from Gramps now correctly appear on the right person's timeline. Previously, these events weren't linking properly because they were missing data that identifies who the event "belongs to".

- **Create Family wizard no longer creates duplicates** (#208): The Create Family wizard now correctly handles existing person notes. Previously, when you selected existing people from your vault to add as family members, the wizard would create duplicate notes instead of just linking them.

- **Proof summaries no longer appear as people** (#145): Fixed proof summary notes incorrectly appearing in people lists.

- **Surname drill-down now finds all matches** (#174): When clicking a surname in the Statistics Dashboard, all people with that surname are now shown, including those with explicitly-set surnames.

### Full Changelog

See [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md) for complete details.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.19.12

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Research Workflow Phase 1

### Research Workflow Entity Types (Phase 1)

This release adds foundation support for GPS-aligned (Genealogical Proof Standard) research workflow tracking. Create structured research notes that integrate with the Statistics Dashboard.

**Five new entity types:**
- **Research Project** - Hub for complex, multi-phase research cases
- **Research Report** - Living document analyzing specific research questions
- **Individual Research Note (IRN)** - Synthesis between reports and person notes
- **Research Journal** - Optional daily/session tracking across projects
- **Research Log Entry** - Optional separate notes for queryable research logs

**How to use:**
- Set `cr_type` in frontmatter (e.g., `cr_type: research_project`)
- Or use tags: `#research-project`, `#research-report`, `#irn`, `#research-journal`, `#research-log-entry`

**Statistics Dashboard integration:**
- New "Research entities" section shows counts by type
- Status breakdowns (projects: open/in-progress/completed; reports: draft/review/final/published)
- Private entity tracking

**Properties supported:**
- `status` — Track progress (varies by entity type)
- `private` — Exclude from exports
- `up` — Link to parent in research hierarchy
- `subject` — Link IRN to person being researched
- `related` — Cross-reference related research entities
- `reportTo` — Intended audience for reports

Works with DataView and Bases for querying. See [Research Workflow wiki page](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/wiki/Research-Workflow) for documentation.

**Related:** [#145](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/145)
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.19.11

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### Added

- **Surnames column in People base** ([#174](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/174)): The surnames property is now available as a column in the People base template for sorting and display.

### Fixed

- **Data quality inconsistencies** ([#200](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/200)): "Orphaned people" now correctly excludes those with adoptive parents, step parents, gender-neutral parents, or children. Organizations are no longer counted as people. The "non-biological parents only" metric now includes gender-neutral adoptive parents.

- **Duplicate adopted child nodes in family chart** ([#201](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/201)): When both directions of an adoptive relationship were specified (parent has `adopted_child` and child has `adoptive_parent`), duplicate child nodes appeared in the family chart view.

- **"Add essential place properties" ignores default category setting** ([#199](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/199)): The context menu command now respects the "Default Place Category" setting instead of always defaulting to "real".
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.19.10

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### DNA Match Tracking

Opt-in DNA match tracking for genetic genealogy workflows. Enable via **Settings > Advanced > DNA tracking**:

- **DNA Match person type**: Select "DNA Match" when creating persons to mark them as genetic matches
- **DNA Information fields**: Track shared cM, testing company, kit ID, match type (BKM/BMM/confirmed/unconfirmed), endogamy flag, and notes
- **DNA Match relationship**: New bidirectional relationship type - adding A→B automatically creates B→A
- **DNA badge in person picker**: Shows flask icon and shared cM value for DNA matches
- All DNA features are invisible when the setting is disabled (default: OFF)

### Repair Missing Relationship IDs

New batch operation in **Control Center > Data Quality** to populate missing `_id` fields from resolvable wikilinks. This improves relationship reliability when notes are renamed:

- Detects wikilinks without corresponding `_id` fields (e.g., `father` without `father_id`)
- Resolves wikilinks to their `cr_id` values using the person index
- Preview shows all repairs with warnings for unresolvable wikilinks
- Supports both single-value and array fields

### Bug Fixes

- **Legacy command IDs not working**: Fixed UI buttons in Control Center tabs that were using the old command prefix, causing "Create base" and similar actions to fail silently.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.19.9

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

This release addresses three bugs reported by users.

### Fixed

- **Name components not saving in Edit Person modal** ([#174](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/174)): Name component fields (given name, surnames, maiden name, married names) were not being written to frontmatter when editing existing person notes.

- **GEDCOM import freezing on large imports** ([#193](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/193)): Added periodic event loop yielding during all import phases to prevent UI freezing. Also added safety limits to duplicate filename detection loops and optimized place cache building.

- **Source linking via Edit Person uses wrong folder** ([#196](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/196)): When linking a source via Edit Person modal, the wikilink now uses the file's basename instead of the frontmatter title. Previously, if the filename differed from the title (e.g., due to kebab-case formatting), clicking the link would create a new file instead of opening the existing source.

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.19.8

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Name Components Support

This release adds explicit name component properties in frontmatter for multi-surname cultures and maiden/married name tracking.

### New Properties

| Property | Type | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| `given_name` | string | First/given name(s) - populated from GEDCOM GIVN tag |
| `surnames` | string[] | Surnames - supports Hispanic, Portuguese, and other multi-surname conventions |
| `maiden_name` | string | Birth surname (already existed with aliases) |
| `married_names` | string[] | Married surnames - supports multiple marriages |

### Usage Examples

**Hispanic dual surnames:**
```yaml
name: "José García López"
surnames:
  - García
  - López
```

**Maiden name tracking:**
```yaml
name: "Jane Smith"
maiden_name: "Jones"
```

**Maiden-name-as-primary convention:**
```yaml
name: "Jane Jones"
married_names:
  - "Smith"
  - "Williams"
```

### Integrations

- **Statistics**: Top Surnames now counts all surnames in the array
- **Split Wizard**: Matches against all surname variants (maiden name, married names, explicit surnames)
- **GEDCOM Import**: Writes `given_name` and `surnames` from GIVN/SURN tags
- **GEDCOM Export**: Exports name components to GIVN/SURN tags
- **Create/Edit Person Modal**: Fields for all name component properties

### Non-Breaking Change

Existing name parsing continues to work. Users can optionally add explicit name components for accurate statistics and comprehensive surname matching.

### Bug Fixes

- **Family chart shows wrong person when root is filtered** ([#191](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/191)): When opening a family chart for a person excluded by the folder filter, the chart now shows a helpful notice instead of silently displaying an unrelated entity.

---

**GitHub Issues:** [#174](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/174), [#192](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/192)

**Documentation:** [Name Components Planning Document](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/docs/planning/name-components.md)
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.19.7

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### Per-Map Place Filtering (#153)

Places can now be restricted to specific custom maps within the same universe. This is useful when you have multiple maps covering different regions (e.g., a detailed "North" map and a full continent map) and want certain places to only appear on relevant maps.

**How to use:**
- Add a `maps` property to place notes with an array of map IDs
- Or use the new "Restrict to maps" checkboxes in the Create/Edit Place modal
- When creating a place by right-clicking on a custom map, the current map is auto-selected
- Places without a `maps` property continue to appear on all maps in their universe (backward compatible)

### Bug Fixes

- **Map view not opening from Control Center** (#188): Fixed command ID prefix that prevented the map view from opening when clicked
- **Duplicate nodes in canvas trees with pedigree collapse** (#186): Persons appearing in multiple ancestry paths now display as a single node with edges converging
- **Custom relationship edge colors on canvas trees** (#185): Custom relationships mapped to standard connections now display with their configured color and line style
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.19.6

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### GEDCOM Notes Import (#179)
GEDCOM NOTE tags attached to individuals are now imported. Notes can be embedded in person files or created as separate note entity files.

- Inline notes, multi-line notes (CONT/CONC), and shared NOTE record references
- Toggle in import wizard: Step 3 → Entity types → Notes
- Optional "Create separate note files" for individual note entities

### Large Import Mode (#180)
New performance option for importing large family trees (500+ people). Suspends relationship syncing during import to prevent file system timeouts.

- Toggle in import wizard: Step 3 → Performance

### Flat Membership Format (#181)
Organization memberships now use parallel arrays instead of nested objects, improving compatibility with Obsidian's Properties editor and Dataview queries. A Data Quality check and bulk migration action help convert existing notes.

### Timeline Description Display (#157)
Timelines now show event descriptions for all event types when available. "Census: 1850 Federal Census" instead of "Census of John Smith". Birth and death events continue showing full titles with person names.

### Bug Fixes
- **Edit Organisation overwrites frontmatter** (#182): Edit modal now preserves existing custom properties
- **Membership YAML formatting**: Fixed array serialization format

---

**Full Changelog:** [0.19.4...0.19.5](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/compare/v0.19.4...v0.19.5)
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.19.5

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Settings Consolidation & UI Improvements

This release consolidates all plugin settings into a single location and reorganizes the Control Center for a cleaner user experience.

### Changed

- **Settings consolidated to Plugin Settings** ([#176](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/176)): All settings have been moved from Control Center → Preferences to the standard Obsidian plugin settings location (Settings → Charted Roots). Settings are now organized into 9 sections: Folders, Data & detection, Canvas & trees, Privacy & export, Dates & validation, Sex & gender, Places, Property & value aliases, and Advanced.

- **Statistics Dashboard reorganization**: The "Visual Trees" tab has been renamed to "Trees & reports" and now consolidates all output generation. Both "Generate Reports" (markdown reports) and "Visual Trees" (PDF tree exports) have been moved from Statistics to this tab. The Statistics view is now purely analytical.

### Added

- **Romantic relationship label preference** ([#167](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/167)): New setting lets users choose between "Spouse" or "Partner" terminology throughout the UI. Located in Settings → Charted Roots → Sex & gender. This is a display preference only — frontmatter property names remain unchanged.

- **Edit current note command**: New command "Edit current note" opens the appropriate edit modal (person, place, or event) for the active note. Assign a hotkey in Settings → Hotkeys to quickly edit notes without navigating through the file menu.

### Fixed

- **Gramps import place hierarchy** ([#178](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/178)): Gramps imports now create the full place hierarchy (country, state, county, city) just like GEDCOM imports.

- **Custom relationship family tree integration**: The "Include on family trees" toggle for custom relationship types now works for all mapping options (Foster parent, Adoptive parent, Guardian, Spouse, Child).

### Enhanced

- **Timeline description display for marriage events** ([#157](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/157)): Marriage and engagement events now show description instead of title in timelines, matching other descriptive event types.

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/compare/0.19.3...0.19.4
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.19.4

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### New Features

**Category-Based Place Organization** ([#163](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/163))

Organize your places into category-based subfolders automatically. When enabled, places are stored in folders matching their category (e.g., `Places/Historical/`, `Places/Fictional/`).

- Enable in Settings → Preferences → "Use category-based subfolders"
- New places are automatically routed to the correct subfolder
- Changing a place's category prompts you to move the file
- Custom folder overrides let you define your own folder structure
- Data Quality detects misplaced files with a bulk "Organize places" action

**Timeline Event Description Display** ([#157](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/157))

Timelines now show more informative text for descriptive event types. Instead of generic titles like "Occupation of John Smith", you'll see "Occupation: Farmer"—the actual description that matters.

Affected event types: `occupation`, `residence`, `military`, `education`

**External ID Preservation** ([#175](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/175))

GEDCOM and Gramps imports now store original source IDs in frontmatter (`external_id` and `external_id_source`). When exporting back to GEDCOM, original xref IDs like `@I0001@` are preserved, enabling cleaner round-trip workflows with other genealogy software.

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.19.3

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Partial Date Support

This release preserves date precision throughout the plugin, addressing a long-standing issue where GEDCOM imports would normalize partial dates (like `1850` or `ABT 1878`) to full ISO format, adding false precision.

### What's New

**GEDCOM Import Preserves Precision**
- Year-only dates (`1850`) stay as `1850` instead of becoming `1850-01-01`
- Month+year dates (`MAR 1855`) become `1855-03` instead of `1855-03-01`
- Date qualifiers (`ABT`, `BEF`, `AFT`, `CAL`, `EST`) are now preserved
- Date ranges (`BET 1882 AND 1885`) are now supported

**User-Friendly Display**
- `ABT 1878` displays as "c. 1878"
- `BEF 1950` displays as "before 1950"
- `AFT 1880` displays as "after 1880"
- `BET 1882 AND 1885` displays as "1882–1885"
- `1855-03` displays as "Mar 1855"

**Clean Export Round-Trip**
- GEDCOM, Gramps, and GedcomX exporters correctly handle partial dates and qualifiers
- Data round-trips without losing precision

### Why This Matters

Genealogists often know only the year someone was born, not the exact date. Previously, importing `1850` from GEDCOM would store it as `1850-01-01`, making it look like the person was born on January 1st. This release maintains source fidelity so your research stays accurate.
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.19.2

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## MyHeritage GEDCOM Import Improvements

This patch release improves MyHeritage GEDCOM import compatibility for users with large family trees.

### Fixed

- **MyHeritage GEDCOM whitespace-only line handling** ([#144](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/144)): Fixed preprocessing of MyHeritage GEDCOM files where tab-only lines between continuation fragments caused parse errors. Whitespace-only lines are now skipped entirely, allowing subsequent continuation content to be properly appended to the previous valid GEDCOM line.

### Enhanced

- **Async GEDCOM parsing for large files** ([#144](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/issues/144)): Added async parsing that yields to the event loop periodically, preventing UI freezing when importing large MyHeritage files (800KB+, 40K+ lines). The import wizard now uses async parsing with progress callbacks.

### Internal

- **GEDCOM anonymization script**: Improved `tools/anonymize_gedcom.py` to explicitly strip BOM characters and warn about malformed early lines.

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/compare/0.19.0...0.19.1
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.19.1

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Plugin Rename: Canvas Roots → Charted Roots

This release completes the rename from **Canvas Roots** to **Charted Roots**, better reflecting the plugin's broader genealogical visualization capabilities beyond Obsidian Canvas.

## What's Changed

- **Plugin renamed** to Charted Roots across all code, documentation, UI, and settings
- **Automatic vault migration** — on first load, the plugin migrates:
  - Canvas metadata (`plugin: 'canvas-roots'` → `plugin: 'charted-roots'`)
  - Code blocks (`canvas-roots-timeline`, etc. → `charted-roots-timeline`, etc.)
- **Backward compatibility** — old protocol handlers and command IDs continue to work via dual-registration

## For BRAT Users

If you installed via BRAT using the old repository name, please update your configuration:

1. Open **Settings → BRAT → Beta Plugin List**
2. Remove `banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots`
3. Add `banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots`

The plugin will automatically migrate your vault data on first load.

## For Manual Installation Users

Update your plugin folder from `.obsidian/plugins/canvas-roots/` to `.obsidian/plugins/charted-roots/` if desired. The old folder name will continue to work.

## Migration Notes

- Your settings are preserved — no reconfiguration needed
- The migration runs once automatically and shows a notice with results
- If you have any issues, check the developer console for detailed logs

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-charted-roots/compare/v0.18.32...v0.19.0
releases.name: Charted Roots v0.19.0

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Highlights

**Automatic Wikilink Resolution** - Write `father: "[[John Smith]]"` and it just works. The plugin now automatically resolves wikilinks in relationship fields to `cr_id` values at graph-building time, eliminating the need for manual `_id` field population in most cases. Explicit `_id` fields still take precedence when present, and ambiguous references (multiple files with the same basename) are surfaced in the Data Quality report.

## Added

- **Automatic wikilink resolution** ([#104](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/104)): Relationship fields (father, mother, spouse, children, parents, etc.) now automatically resolve `[[Person Name]]` wikilinks to cr_id values. No more juggling between wikilink and _id fields for basic relationships.

- **Wikidata Place Web Clipper template** ([#166](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/166)): AI-powered template for extracting place data from Wikidata Q-pages. Auto-extracts coordinates, place type, parent place, alternate names, administrative hierarchy, and Wikipedia links.

## Enhanced

- **Staging promotion workflow** ([#165](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/165)): Promotion now auto-assigns `cr_id` to notes missing it, routes notes to correct folders by type (places → Places folder, events → Events folder), and removes Web Clipper metadata. Web-clipped notes work immediately after promotion.

- **Performance optimization** ([#104](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/104)): RelationshipValidator and ProofSummaryService now use the centralized PersonIndexService for cr_id lookups, eliminating duplicate vault scanning.

## Technical Notes

The wikilink resolution feature completes all 4 phases of issue #104:
1. PersonIndexService - centralized cr_id ↔ file index with caching
2. FamilyGraph integration - wikilinks resolve during graph building
3. Data Quality warnings - ambiguous wikilinks flagged in reports
4. Service consolidation - duplicate lookup code eliminated

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.18.32

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
# Bug Fixes

## Fixed

- **Find a Grave Web Clipper templates** ([#155](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/155)): Fixed URL trigger pattern to include HTTPS protocol for auto-selection. Fixed person name extraction to use `.bio-name` CSS selector instead of page title, removing unwanted "Grave - " prefix. Removed hardcoded path configuration to allow user customization.
- **Gramps event names include all participants** ([#156](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/156)): Fixed Gramps import creating event titles with all participants instead of just the principal person. Event names were joining all participants with "and" (e.g., "Birth of Baby and Mother"). Now filters for participants with role="Primary", falling back to the first participant if no role is assigned.
- **Create Place modal missing parent dropdown from Dashboard** ([#158](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/158)): Fixed inconsistent UI in Create Place modal depending on entry point. When opened from Dashboard, the parent place dropdown was missing and showed only a text input. Now passes the required place graph services to enable the dropdown.
- **Dynamic blocks fail with 'value.startsWith is not a function' error** ([#160](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/160)): Fixed timeline and relationships dynamic blocks crashing when config values contain commas inside wikilinks. The config parser was splitting all comma-containing values into arrays, breaking values like `[[Person Name|Alias]]` or `[[Place, City]]`. Now only splits on commas outside wikilink brackets.
- **Import Wizard Preview stuck on 'Parsing file...'** ([#161](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/161)): Fixed Preview step getting stuck showing "Parsing file..." indefinitely. The `isParsing` flag wasn't being cleared before re-render, causing the loading state to persist.
- **Create Place modal doesn't recognize existing parent places** ([#162](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/162)): Fixed modal prompting to create duplicate parent places even when they already exist. Addressed two scenarios: (1) stale place graph cache when creating grandparent hierarchies, (2) `pendingParentPlace` flag not being cleared when selecting from dropdown.

## Added

- **GEDCOM anonymization tool**: Added `tools/anonymize_gedcom.py` script to help users create shareable test files when reporting GEDCOM import issues without exposing sensitive genealogical data. Supports `--keep-dates` and `--keep-places` flags for targeted debugging scenarios. See [Troubleshooting wiki](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/wiki/Troubleshooting#sharing-gedcom-files-for-debugging) for usage instructions.
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.18.31

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Added

- **Ambiguous wikilink detection in Data Quality** ([#104](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/104)): The Data Quality report now detects when wikilinks in relationship fields match multiple files with the same basename. When multiple person notes share the same name (e.g., two "John Smith.md" files in different folders), the system generates warnings suggesting users add `_id` fields (e.g., `father_id`) to disambiguate the references.

## Fixed

- **Infinite preprocessing loop in GEDCOM import** ([#144](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/144)): Fixed an infinite loop that caused repeated preprocessing during GEDCOM import. The import wizard's preview step was triggering concurrent calls to the file parser because re-renders occurred while async parsing was still in progress. Added a guard flag to prevent concurrent parsing attempts.

---

This release completes Phase 3 of the wikilink-to-cr_id resolution feature, adding detection for ambiguous wikilinks that cannot be automatically resolved. Users will now receive clear warnings when relationship wikilinks are ambiguous, with suggestions on how to fix them using explicit `_id` fields.
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.18.30

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes ### Import Wizard Toggle Fix (#154) Fixed a bug where entity type toggles (People, Places, Sources, Events, Dynamic blocks) in the Import Wizard couldn't be turned back on after being toggled off. ### MyHeritage GEDCOM Preprocessing Fix (#144) Fixed "Invalid GEDCOM line format" parse error when importing large MyHeritage GEDCOM files. The v0.18.28 preprocessor was converting `
` tags to actual newlines, which created invalid GEDCOM lines (lines without level numbers). Now replaces `
` with spaces instead, preserving data while maintaining valid GEDCOM structure. --- **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/compare/0.18.28...0.18.29
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.18.29

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## New Feature **MyHeritage GEDCOM Compatibility** ([#144](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/144)) MyHeritage GEDCOM exports can now be imported directly without manual preprocessing. The plugin automatically detects MyHeritage files and fixes: - UTF-8 BOM (Byte Order Mark) that caused parsing failures - Double-encoded HTML entities (`<` → `<`) - `
` tags converted to proper line breaks - Decorative HTML tag stripping Three modes available in **Settings > Data & detection > GEDCOM compatibility mode**: - **Auto** (default): Detect MyHeritage files and apply fixes automatically - **MyHeritage**: Always apply fixes (useful for modified files) - **None**: Disable preprocessing The import results modal now shows what compatibility fixes were applied. ## Bug Fixes - **Special character sanitization for all importers** ([#139](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/139)): Extended name sanitization to Gramps, CSV, and GedcomX importers. Names with special characters (`"`, `()`, `[]`, `{}`) now create proper wikilinks across all import formats. - **Duplicate person filenames during batch import**: Fixed "File already exists" errors when importing multiple people with identical names (e.g., multiple "Son (stillborn)" entries). - **Map view "Link existing place" crash** ([#151](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/151)): Fixed error when using right-click → "Link existing place here" on maps. - **Edit Place modal styling**: Fixed truncated labels and horizontal scrollbar in the Edit Place modal. --- **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.18.28

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## New Features

- **DMS Coordinate Format** (#121): Enter coordinates in degrees-minutes-seconds format (e.g., `33°51'08"N`) in the place creation modal. Opt-in via Settings → Data & detection.

- **Place Context Menu** (#152): Right-click on the Places folder to quickly create a new place note in that location.

- **Link Existing Place to Map** (#151): Right-click on the map to link an existing place note to that location, updating its coordinates automatically.

- **Optional Person Names** (#140): Create placeholder person notes without names—useful when you know relationships but not identities yet. Fill in names later as your research progresses.

- **DNA Match Tracking (Phase 1)** (#126): Lightweight support for genetic genealogy workflows. Includes a DNA match template snippet and a "DNA Matches" view in the People Bases template. Track shared cM, testing company, match type (BKM/BMM), and endogamy flags.

## Bug Fixes

- **Event Edit Preserves Custom Properties** (#150): Editing events no longer deletes custom frontmatter properties added via Templater or manually.

- **BCE Date Sorting** (#146): Events spanning the BCE/AD boundary now sort correctly in timeline codeblocks.

---

See the [full changelog](CHANGELOG.md) for details.
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.18.27

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
This release fixes several bugs affecting timeline display, PDF exports, GEDCOM imports, and staging management.

## Bug Fixes

**Timeline & Date Handling**
- Fixed BCE/AD date sorting in timelines ([#146](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/146)) - Events now sort chronologically across the BCE/AD boundary (e.g., "11 BCE" before "14 AD")
- Fixed missing space between event title and place in timeline blocks ([#146](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/146)) - No more "Marriage of Person Ain Place"

**PDF Exports**
- Fixed Unicode box-drawing characters rendering as blank spaces in PDF exports ([#148](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/148)) - Pedigree tree connectors (├──, └──, │) now display correctly by switching to DejaVu Sans Mono fonts with comprehensive Unicode support
- Fixed raw markdown code block delimiters appearing in PDF exports ([#148](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/148))

**GEDCOM Import**
- Fixed wikilink resolution for names with special characters ([#139](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/139)) - Names like `Jane "Jennie" Smith` now properly link to sanitized filenames

**Staging Manager**
- Fixed entity count showing 0 when clipped notes are present ([#149](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/149)) - All entity types including clipped notes now counted correctly
- Removed confusing "Import Batches" terminology from UI
- Improved visual alignment of entity type breakdown

## Technical Details

Added `build-fonts.js` script to bundle DejaVu Sans Mono fonts into pdfmake's virtual file system, ensuring proper Unicode rendering in PDF exports without requiring external font files.

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.18.26

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
# Web Clipper Integration Phase 1

This release introduces **Web Clipper Integration**, enabling you to capture genealogical data from web sources directly into your Canvas Roots vault using the official Obsidian Web Clipper browser extension.

## New Features

### Web Clipper Integration - Phase 1 ([#128](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/128))

Capture data from obituaries, Find A Grave, FamilySearch, and other genealogical websites into your staging folder for review.

**Key Features:**
- **Auto-detection** - Detects clipped notes containing `clip_source_type`, `clipped_from`, or `clipped_date` properties
- **Dashboard integration** - Shows breakdown like "3 clips (1 new), 1 other" with unread count
- **Toggle filtering** - Filter Staging Manager by [All] [Clipped] [Other] with multi-level filtering
- **User templates** - Works with any Web Clipper templates you create
- **Full documentation** - New [Web Clipper Integration](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/wiki/Web-Clipper-Integration) wiki page with setup guide, template examples, and troubleshooting

Phase 1 focuses on detection and filtering. Future phases will introduce curated templates and enhanced extraction based on community feedback.

## Bug Fixes

- **Timeline event location spacing** - Fixed missing space between event links and location text
- **Filename sanitization** ([#139](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/139)) - Strip parentheses/brackets from filenames to prevent wikilink errors (e.g., `Susan (Sue)` → `Susan Sue.md`)

## Documentation

- Created [Web Clipper Integration](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/wiki/Web-Clipper-Integration) wiki page
- Updated [Data Entry](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/wiki/Data-Entry) with clipping section

## Acknowledgments

Special thanks to @wilbry for testing and UX feedback that shaped this release.

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.18.25

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## What's New

**Staging Management UI** ([#137](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/137))

A dedicated modal for managing staged imports, making it easier to review, organize, and promote imported data before it enters your main family tree.

### Features

- **Batch Organization**: Imports are grouped into timestamped subfolders, each displayed as a collapsible card
- **Entity Breakdown**: See counts by type (people, places, sources, events, organizations) for each batch
- **Expandable File Lists**: Click batch headers to preview individual files before promoting
- **Cross-Import Detection**: Check for duplicates against your main tree using name similarity, date proximity, and gender matching
- **Per-Batch Actions**: Check duplicates, Promote to main tree, or Delete batch
- **Bulk Actions**: Promote all or Delete all staging data
- **Multiple Access Points**: Dashboard (yellow indicator when staging has data), Command palette, or Import Wizard success screen

### Changes

- Moved "Staging isolation" toggle from Data settings to Preferences section
- Renamed staging folder setting for clarity

### Bug Fixes

- **Event edit modal loses person link on save** ([#135](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/135)): Fixed bug where editing an event would silently remove the person link
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.18.24

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## New

### Auto-exclude template folders from note discovery (#136)

Template files containing Templater syntax (like `<% tp.file.title %>`) no longer appear in person/place/event lists or other plugin features.

**How it works:**
- Automatically detects template folders configured in core Templates, Templater, and QuickAdd plugins
- New settings in **Settings > Advanced**:
  - Toggle for auto-detection (enabled by default)
  - Info box showing detected folders
  - Text area for specifying additional folders to exclude

**Also in this release:**
- Built-in Templater template snippets now leave `cr_id` empty - Canvas Roots auto-generates unique IDs when notes are indexed, which is more reliable than timestamp-based generation

### Bug fix: Gramps import hangs during gzip decompression (#134)

- Fixed `.gpkg` and `.gramps` file imports hanging indefinitely when decompressing gzip data
- Added 30-second timeout to prevent indefinite hangs
- Improved logging for decompression operations
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.18.23

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Privacy-Aware Canvas Generation (#102, part of #95)

Generate family tree canvases with privacy protection for living persons:

- **New privacy options** in Tree Wizard for canvas and Excalidraw generation
- **Obfuscated display**: Living persons shown as "Living", "Private", or initials based on your privacy settings
- **Two formats**: Choose 'text' (no file link) or 'file' (keeps clickable link)
- **Preview integration**: See count of privacy-protected persons before generating (e.g., "42 people · 5 privacy-protected")
- **Hidden support**: Use the 'hidden' privacy setting to completely exclude living persons

**Note**: Canvas privacy reduces casual visibility but has limitations—see [Privacy & Security](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/wiki/Privacy-And-Security#canvas-privacy-protection) for details.

## Bug Fixes

- **Adoptive relationships**: Fixed duplicate nodes and missing children when viewing family chart from adoptive parent's perspective (#129)
- **Gramps XML import**: Fixed hang at "Parsing file..." caused by async/await bug in gzip decompression (#130)
- **GEDCOM import**: Fixed wikilinks pointing to wrong person when names are duplicated (#132)
- **Gramps import**: Same wikilink fix applied to .gramps and .gpkg imports (#133)

## Full Changelog

See [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md) for complete details.
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.18.22

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Privacy Protection Enhancements

This release completes privacy phases 3-6 with user-defined private fields, export warnings, and improved discoverability.

### Added

- **Private fields support** (#98): New `private_fields` frontmatter property to mark specific fields as private. Fields listed will be excluded from exports with user confirmation. Common use cases include protecting deadnames (`previous_names`), medical notes, and legal information.

- **Export warnings for private fields** (#99): Warning modal shown before export when private fields are detected. Users can choose to include, exclude, or cancel the export.

- **Privacy feature discoverability** (#100): First-run notice after import when living persons are detected and privacy protection is disabled. Export wizard preview now shows info notice when privacy is disabled. Both notices link directly to privacy configuration settings.

### Fixed

- **Control Center and person picker freezes on large vaults** (#113): Fixed 30+ second freezes on macOS with large vaults by adding modal-level caching for graph services.

- **Relationship calculator asymmetry with unresolved Gramps handles** (#109): Fixed two root causes—unresolved Gramps handles left in `_id` fields and filename collision during relationship update pass.

### Summary

- 3 features added (phases 3-6 of Export Privacy initiative)
- 2 bugs fixed (performance and import reliability)
- Completes issues #98, #99, #100, #109, #113
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.18.21

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Data Quality Improvements

This release focuses on data quality improvements (umbrella issue #114) and fixes a timeline display bug.

### Fixed

- **Timeline block display** (#122): Fixed whitespace collapsing that caused "Residencein" instead of "Residence in"
- **Dashboard data issues count** (#115): Dashboard now shows consistent issue counts with Statistics Dashboard
- **Statistics total mismatch** (#116): Clarified what the "Issues" total represents (missing births + orphans + unsourced events)
- **Sex validation too strict** (#117): Now accepts common synonyms like `male`/`female` in addition to GEDCOM codes (M/F/X/U)
- **Living people calculation** (#118): Uses `livingPersonAgeThreshold` setting (default 100) instead of just checking for no death date
- **Gramps import cr_id formats** (#119): Fixed relationship property updates to respect property aliases

### Added

- **Data quality severity grouping** (#120): Issues are now organized into three categories:
  - **Errors** (red): Date inconsistencies that should be fixed
  - **Data Gaps** (yellow): Missing data that may be unavailable for historical records
  - **Informational** (blue): Neutral metrics like places without coordinates
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.18.20

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes for Gender-Neutral Parents and Property Consistency

This release focuses on fixing issues with gender-neutral parent relationships and ensuring consistent property naming across the plugin.

### Fixed

- **Relationship calculator not traversing gender-neutral parents** ([#109](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/109)) - BFS pathfinding now correctly traverses `parents`/`parents_id` relationships, and parents using gender-neutral fields now have `childrenCrIds` populated.

- **New child notes created with obsolete `child` property** ([#110](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/110)) - Removed automatic addition of empty `child` property; now uses normalized `children` property throughout.

- **Import completion screen shows wrong entity counts** ([#111](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/111)) - Completion screen now shows actual imported counts from results instead of file preview counts.

- **Family Tree canvas separates spouses when grouping siblings** ([#103](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/103)) - Spouses now stay adjacent when siblings are grouped by parent pair.

- **Custom Relationship uses `parent` instead of `parents`** ([#112](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/112)) - Built-in relationship type IDs changed from `parent`/`child` to `parents`/`children`. Family Tree now shows all parents when mixing `father`/`mother` with `parents` property.

- **Person picker search may not work on macOS** ([#113](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/113)) - Improved search input focus and event handling for context menu invocation.

### Upgrade Notes

If you've been using the "Add Custom Relationship" feature to add parent/child relationships, your existing data with `parent` property will continue to work (read compatibility), but new relationships will use the correct `parents` property.
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.18.19

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
Bug fix release addressing issues with couples grouping and gender-neutral parent fields.

## Fixed

- **Couples grouping not detecting all couples** ([#105](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/105)) - "By couples" grouping now correctly groups all couples with `spouse_id` set:
  - Changed from child-parent inference to direct spouse relationship detection
  - Fixes couples without children not being grouped
  - Fixes couples whose children use gender-neutral `parents` field instead of `father`/`mother`

- **Gender-neutral parents not respected** ([#108](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/108)) - Fixed two issues with the gender-neutral `parents`/`parents_id` fields:
  - Bidirectional linker no longer auto-adds `father`/`mother` fields when `parents`/`parents_id` already exist and "Enable inclusive/gender-neutral parent fields" is enabled
  - Edit Person modal now correctly displays existing `parents` relationships when opened via file explorer context menu or Control Center
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.18.18

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Added

### Rename file when person name changes (#107)
When editing a person's name in the Edit Person modal, the plugin now offers to rename the note file to match. If a file with that name already exists, a number is appended (e.g., "John Smith 1.md"). All relationship wikilinks in related notes (parents, spouses, children) are automatically updated to reflect the new filename.

### Canvas grouping for family units (#105)
Visual groups to organize related nodes on canvases with four grouping strategies:
- **None** — No grouping (default)
- **By generation** — Groups people by generation level
- **By couples** — Groups parent pairs who share children
- **By collection** — Groups by the `collection` property

Available in global settings (Preferences → Canvas styling) and per-tree in Tree Wizard. Works for both Canvas and Excalidraw output.

### Sensitive field redaction utilities (#96)
Centralized utilities for filtering sensitive fields (SSN, identity numbers) from exports. Current exporters already protect these fields implicitly via the `PersonNode` interface; these utilities are for future use when working with raw frontmatter.

## Fixed

### Children display property not accumulating (#106)
When adding multiple children via the "Add child" button, the `children` display property now correctly shows all children instead of just the first. Fixed reading from legacy `child` property instead of normalized `children` property.
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.18.17

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Added

- **Pronouns field support** ([#101](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/101))
  - New `pronouns` frontmatter property (free-form string, e.g., "she/her", "they/them")
  - Toggle via Settings → Display → Show pronouns (default: enabled)
  - Displayed in person pickers after name in parentheses
  - Editable via Edit Person modal
  - Included in all report outputs (Markdown, ODT, PDF)

- **Manual living status override** ([#97](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/97))
  - New `cr_living` frontmatter property for explicit control over privacy protection
  - `cr_living: true` — Always treat as living (protected in exports)
  - `cr_living: false` — Always treat as deceased (not protected)
  - "Living status override" dropdown in Edit Person modal when privacy protection is enabled
  - Supported in all export formats: GEDCOM, GEDCOM X, Gramps XML, CSV

## Fixed

- **Sibling ordering in Family Tree canvas** ([#103](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/103))
  - Children from multi-spouse families are now grouped by parent pair instead of interleaved
  - Works around upstream family-chart library layout issue

- **ESLint compliance** — Addressed lint issues for Obsidian plugin review
  - Replaced deprecated `substr()` with `substring()`
  - Added proper type definitions for pdfmake and family-chart libraries
  - Removed unused code (~240 lines)
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.18.16

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Card Style Options (#87)

Choose from 4 card styles in Family Chart view to match your visualization needs:

| Style | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **Rectangle** | Default style with avatar thumbnails and full details (name, dates) |
| **Circle** | Circular avatar cards with name labels below |
| **Compact** | Text-only cards without avatars for denser layouts |
| **Mini** | Smaller name-only cards for high-level overviews |

- Card style persists across Obsidian restarts
- All styles support PNG/PDF export
- Access via **Style** menu → **Card Style** submenu

## Changed:
- Add checkmarks to spacing menu items to show current selection
- Preserve node spacing between reloads
- Birth and death dates now display on separate lines when both are enabled. Cards automatically resize to accommodate the extra line (220×90 instead of 200×70 for Rectangle style).

## Separate Note Files - Phase 4 (#79, #80)

Create standalone note entity files during Gramps import:

- New "Create separate note files" checkbox in import wizard (opt-in)
- Notes created as `cr_type: note` entities in configurable Notes folder
- Create Note modal with note type, title, privacy toggle, and linked entities
- "Canvas Roots: Create note" command in command palette
- Notes tile and templates in Templater modal

## Bug Fixes (#88)

- **Export text overflow** - Fixed text spilling outside card bounds in PNG/PDF exports
- **High Contrast theme** - Fixed white text on bright backgrounds in dark mode; now uses black text for accessibility
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.18.15

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Edit Person Events & Sources

This release adds **Events** and **Sources** sections to the Edit Person modal, allowing you to manage all person-related data from a single interface instead of editing multiple notes separately.

### New Features

**Sources Section**
- Multi-value picker to link source notes with **Link** and **Create** buttons
- Stores as `sources` (wikilinks) and `sources_id` (cr_ids) arrays for reliable linking even after renames
- Color-coded type badges matching the source picker modal style

**Events Section**
- Display events that reference this person with type badges and dates
- **Link** existing events to this person (adds person to event's `persons` array)
- **Unlink** events (removes person from event)
- **Create** new events with this person pre-filled

### Bug Fixes

- **Context menu Edit Person** - Fixed "Plugin not available" error when using Link/Create buttons after opening Edit Person via file context menu
- **Children display** - Fixed children displaying as cr_ids instead of names (#86)
- **Duplicate children_id during Gramps import** - Fixed duplicate values appearing after Gramps .gpkg imports (#84)
- **Deprecated `child` property** - New imports now use `children` (plural) matching v0.18.11 normalization (#85)

### Technical Notes

- Events use **inverse relationships**: event notes contain `persons: ["[[Person]]"]`
- Linking an event from the person modal modifies the **event note**, not the person note
- Sources follow the **dual-storage pattern**: `sources` (wikilinks) + `sources_id` (cr_ids)

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/compare/v0.18.13...v0.18.14
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.18.14

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Gramps Notes Import

Import notes attached to entities in Gramps during XML import:

- **Person notes** appended as "## Notes" section with type headings (e.g., "### Research")
- **Event & place notes** also imported and appended to their respective notes
- **Family notes** attached to marriage/family events
- **Style conversion** preserves bold, italic, strikethrough, underline, superscript, subscript, and links
- **Formatted notes** wrapped in code fences to preserve whitespace
- **Privacy propagation** — if any attached note has privacy flag, `private: true` is set in frontmatter
- Toggle in import wizard to enable/disable notes import

## Bug Fixes

- **Step/adoptive parent canvas positioning** — Fixed step/adoptive parents not appearing on generated canvas trees (#75)
- **Circular relationship freeze** — Added cycle detection to prevent Obsidian from freezing when circular parent-child relationships exist (#83)

## Context Menu Reorganization

Restructured the person note context menu for better organization (#82):

- Renamed "Add relationship..." to "Relationships" submenu
- Moved "Validate relationships" and "Calculate relationship..." into Relationships submenu
- Removed duplicate "More options..." entry
- Added "More" submenu with less frequently used actions
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.18.13

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Bug Fix Release

This release addresses several user-reported issues, with a focus on adoptive parent relationship handling.

### Fixed

**Adoptive Parent Relationships ([#75](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues/75))**

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
This release improves the Post-Import Cleanup Wizard with better feedback during batch operations and full keyboard accessibility, plus standardizes property naming for consistency.

## Cleanup Wizard Phase 4

**Batch Progress Indicators**
- Real-time progress bars during batch operations (Steps 2-6, 10-14)

## Property Naming Normalization

- Preview shows notes that will be updated

**Documentation Updates**

- Deprecation notice added for `child` property

## Fixed
