**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/compare/0.12.0...0.12.1
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.12.1

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Calendarium Integration (Phase 1)

Canvas Roots can now import calendar definitions from the [Calendarium](https://github.com/javalent/calendarium) plugin, making it easier for worldbuilders to use their existing fictional calendars for genealogical events.

**How it works:**
- When Calendarium is installed, a new "Integrations" card appears in Control Center → Preferences
- Enable "Read-only (import calendars)" to see your Calendarium calendars in Canvas Roots
- Imported calendars appear in the Date Systems card under "From Calendarium"
- Select them when creating events with fictional dates

**Key details:**
- Zero configuration required—calendars are imported automatically
- Integration is invisible if Calendarium isn't installed
- Read-only: Canvas Roots reads from Calendarium but doesn't modify it
- Eras, abbreviations, and year directions are preserved

This is Phase 1 of the Calendarium integration. Future phases may include displaying Calendarium events on timelines and bidirectional sync. See the [Calendarium Integration roadmap](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/wiki/Roadmap#calendarium-integration) for details.

## Other Changes

- **Stricter ESLint rules** - Added `await-thenable`, `no-base-to-string`, `no-console`, `no-case-declarations`, and `no-constant-condition` rules with all violations fixed.

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.12.0

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes for GEDCOM Import with Duplicate Names

This release fixes critical bugs in GEDCOM import when handling files that contain multiple people with identical names (e.g., a grandfather and grandson both named "John Smith").

### Fixed

- **Race condition with BidirectionalLinker** - The relationship linker was modifying files during import before GEDCOM IDs could be replaced with internal cr_ids, causing corruption. The linker is now suspended during import.

- **Regex substring matching** - Shorter GEDCOM IDs (e.g., `I2`) were incorrectly matching within longer IDs (e.g., `I27`), producing corrupt IDs like `jvc-874-coq-7457`. Replacements are now sorted by length with proper boundary assertions.

- **Missing children_id replacement** - The import Phase 2 was not replacing `children_id` values, leaving raw GEDCOM IDs in parent records.

- **Duplicate name corruption** - Post-import relationship sync was matching people by filename rather than cr_id, causing data from one person to incorrectly merge with another who shared the same name.

- **Corrupt cr_id detection** - Data quality checks now validate cr_id format and flag invalid values as errors.

### Testing

A new test file `gedcom-sample-duplicates-27.ged` is included with 27 people across 4 family branches, featuring:
- Grandfather/grandson with same name (John Smith)
- Two unrelated people with same name (Mary Johnson)  
- Two people with same name AND same birth date (William Brown)
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.11.9

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### Fixes

- **Base templates: columns not visible by default** - Fixed an issue where Obsidian Bases templates for Events, Sources, Organizations, and Places did not display columns by default. The templates had `sort` (which controls sorting direction) but were missing `order` (which controls visible columns). Added `order` arrays to the first view in each template specifying which columns to display.

- **Descendant tree canvas export missing edges** - Fixed a bug where descendant tree canvas exports had no connecting arrows between person cards. The `buildDescendantTree()` function was creating edges with `type: 'child'` which were filtered out by the canvas generator (which skips child edges to avoid duplicates). Changed edge type to `'parent'` so edges are properly included in the export.
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.11.8

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
### Added

- **Context menu for person links in Parent claim conflicts** - Right-click on person names in the Parent claim conflicts table to open in new tab or new window.

### Fixed

- Various PR Review code improvements and fixes
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.11.7

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Fixed

- Fixed GEDCOM import relationship ID replacement: all relationship fields (`father_id`, `mother_id`, `spouse_id`, `children_id`) now correctly replace temporary GEDCOM IDs with permanent `cr_id` values during import, including for people with duplicate names (which previously failed due to filename suffix handling) and for `children_id` arrays (which were previously missing from the replacement logic).

## Changed

- **PR review compliance** - Fixed 27 floating promises, lexical declaration in case block, `element.style.visibility` usage, removed `async` from 4 methods without `await`, added defensive object handling in YAML serialization.

- **Code cleanup** - Removed unused template exports, replaced `Vault.delete()` with `FileManager.trashFile()`, removed unused variables and functions.
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.11.5

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Obsidian plugin review fixes (Round 11) and bug fixes.

### Changed

- Updated Control Center Guide tab with data quality guidance
- Added `visibleProperties` to both Events and People base templates - While Obsidian doesn't read this on load (it's write-only for UI state), it documents the recommended columns
- Improved UX with expanded descriptions - All four "Create base" buttons (People, Events, Places, Sources) now explain that users need to click "Properties" to enable additional columns after creating
- Renamed Sources button - Changed from "Create base" to "Create Sources base" for consistency
- **PR review compliance** - Addressed all required and optional items from Obsidian plugin review:
  - Replaced direct `style.x =` assignments with `style.setProperty()` for CSP compliance
  - Wrapped async event handlers with `void (async () => {...})()` pattern to handle floating promises
  - Removed unused imports across the codebase

### Fixed

- Fixed the Events base template - Rewrote it to use correct Obsidian Bases syntax (filters arrays, order arrays instead of sort objects)
- Fix people base template showing non-person notes
- **Person picker showing non-person notes** - Fixed the "Select person" modal (used when linking Father/Mother/Spouse in Create Person) incorrectly listing place, event, and source notes alongside person notes
- **Timeline callout vertical line alignment** - Fixed the vertical line in markdown timeline exports not aligning with the dot markers
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.11.4

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## GEDCOM Import Data Quality Preview

This release introduces a **pre-import data quality analysis** that catches issues before any files are created in your vault.

When importing a GEDCOM file, Canvas Roots now analyzes the data and shows a preview modal if it detects:
- **Date issues** – Death before birth, future dates, events occurring before birth or after death
- **Relationship issues** – Gender/role mismatches (female listed as father), parent younger than child
- **Reference issues** – Pointers to records that don't exist in the file
- **Data completeness** – Missing names, unknown sex, no dates recorded

### Place Name Standardization During Import

The preview modal includes a **Places tab** where you can standardize place name variants **before** files are created:
- Choose canonical forms for country names (e.g., "USA" vs "United States of America")
- Standardize state abbreviations (e.g., "CA" vs "California")
- Your choices affect both file names and frontmatter property values

This ensures consistent data from the start, avoiding cleanup work after import.

## Control Center UI Improvements

**Actions cards consistency** – All entity tabs (People, Places, Events) now have a consistent layout:
- Actions card appears first with create, template, and base creation options
- Each tab now includes "Create [Entity] base" for quick Obsidian Base setup
- Events tab renamed "Event notes" card to "Actions" for consistency

**New actions added:**
- People tab: "Create People base"
- Places tab: "Create place note", "Create Places base", "Templater templates"
- Events tab: "Create Events base", "Templater templates"

## Post-Import Place Tools

**Standardize place name variants** (Places tab) – New data quality tool for normalizing abbreviations in existing data:
- Country variants: "United States of America", "United States", "US" → "USA"
- US state abbreviations: "California" → "CA", "New York" → "NY"

**Standardize place types** – Convert generic types like "locality" (common in GEDCOM imports) to standard types (city, town, village).

## Documentation

New **[Data Quality](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/wiki/Data-Quality)** wiki page documenting all data quality tools, batch operations, and best practices.

## Testing

New comprehensive GEDCOM edge case test file with 50+ intentional data quality issues for stress testing the import process.
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.11.3

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## 0.11.2 - Data Quality & Settings UX

### Parent Conflict Resolution

New dedicated card in the People tab for resolving conflicting parent claims—when multiple people claim the same child. The table shows each conflict with both claimants' names and cr_ids, with one-click "Keep 1" / "Keep 2" buttons for quick resolution.

### Settings UX Overhaul

Major improvements to both Plugin Settings and the Control Center Preferences tab:

- **Search**: Filter settings by name or description
- **Collapsible sections**: Plugin Settings now organized into logical groups
- **Sliders**: Numeric settings (spacing, node dimensions) use sliders with reset buttons
- **Folder autocomplete**: Folder settings suggest existing vault folders as you type
- **Cross-navigation**: Links between Plugin Settings and Preferences for easy discovery
- **Less duplication**: Canvas layout and folder settings consolidated in Preferences only

### GEDCOM Import

- **GEDCOM import now adds `cr_type: person`** to imported person notes for consistent note type detection

### Fixes

- **GEDCOM import nested arrays for wikilinks** - Fixed YAML serialization writing `[[place]]` as nested arrays; wikilink values are now properly quoted
- **Map view not showing markers** - Fixed map not recognizing flat coordinate properties (`coordinates_lat`, `coordinates_long`) written by geocoding; now supports nested, flat, and legacy coordinate formats
- **Places tab crash with non-string place values** - Fixed TypeError when place properties contain arrays or objects instead of strings
- **Family chart view only showing ancestors** - Fixed issue where the interactive family chart only displayed the direct ancestral line instead of the complete tree
  - Chart now properly shows descendants, siblings, and in-laws
  - Root cause: missing bidirectional children relationships in data transformation
- Bidirectional relationship validation now correctly handles parent sex (male→father, female→mother)
- Fixed false positives where spouses with shared children were incorrectly flagged
- Batch fix operations no longer get reverted by automatic bidirectional linking

### Breaking Changes

The default primaryTypeProperty is now `cr_type` instead of `type` to avoid conflicts with other plugins, and the base template filter now includes `cr_type = "person"`. Existing vaults using `type` will continue to work. If you have existing Bases files created before this release, delete and recreate them to apply the updated filter.
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.11.2

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
# v0.11.1 - Data Quality Enhancements

This release focuses on improving data quality tools with enhanced batch operations for relationship validation, value normalization, and better organization of data quality features across the Control Center.

## New Features

### Bidirectional Relationship Validation
Detect and fix one-way relationship inconsistencies where relationships aren't properly reciprocated.

- **Finds missing reciprocal links**: Parent lists child but child doesn't list parent, spouse A lists B but B doesn't list A
- **Supports all relationship formats**: Works with both simple (`spouse`, `children`) and indexed (`spouse1`, `spouse2`) properties
- **Preview modal**: Search, filter by type, and sort before applying changes
- **Smart validation**: Won't overwrite existing parent fields to prevent data loss

**Location**: Control Center > People tab > Batch operations

### Impossible Dates Detection
Preview-only validation tool to identify logical date errors that require manual review.

Detects:
- Birth after death
- Unrealistic lifespans (>120 years)
- Parent born after child
- Parent too young at child's birth (<10 years)
- Posthumous births (>12 months after father's death, any time after mother's death)

**Handles multiple date formats**: ISO (YYYY-MM-DD), partial dates (YYYY-MM, YYYY), circa dates, date ranges

**Location**: Control Center > People tab > Batch operations

## Improvements

### Value Alias System for Sex Normalization
The "Normalize sex values" batch operation now uses the flexible value alias system instead of hardcoded M/F logic.

- **Respects user configuration**: Uses value aliases from Control Center > Schemas > Value aliases
- **Supports worldbuilders**: Can normalize to custom values (e.g., "H" → "hermaphrodite")
- **Smart normalization**: Only normalizes values with configured mappings, skips canonical values
- **Backward compatible**: Built-in M/F mappings work out of the box for genealogists

### Reorganized Data Quality Tools
Improved discoverability and reduced confusion about where to find data quality features.

**Data Quality tab**:
- New "Quick Start" card with navigation links to People, Places, and Schemas tabs
- Renamed sections: "Vault-wide analysis" and "Cross-domain batch operations"
- Clearer explanatory text for each section

**People and Places tabs**:
- Navigation guidance with clickable links to Data Quality tab
- Removed duplicate operations (consolidated to Data Quality tab)
- Domain-specific tools remain for convenience

**Places tab**:
- Updated batch operations styling to match Obsidian settings pattern
- Right-aligned action buttons for consistency

## Documentation

All changes documented in [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md).

## Related Features

This release builds on:
- [Value Aliases (v0.9.4)](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/releases/tag/0.9.4) - Custom terminology for property values
- [Property Aliases (v0.9.3)](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/releases/tag/0.9.3) - Map custom property names to canonical fields
- [GEDCOM Import v2 (v0.10.1)](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/releases/tag/0.10.1) - Enhanced import with data quality tools

## Installation

Update via Obsidian's Community Plugins settings, or download manually from the [releases page](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/releases).
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.11.1

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
# Canvas Roots v0.11.0 - Export v2

## Major Release: Complete Export Overhaul

This release represents a **complete rewrite of all export functionality** with full entity support, round-trip fidelity, and a modern user interface.

## What's New

### Full Entity Export
Export now includes **everything**, not just person records:

- **Events**: All life events (birth, death, marriage, residence, education, military, etc.) with dates, places, and citations
- **Sources**: Source notes with repositories, citations, and confidence levels
- **Places**: Complete hierarchy with coordinates and place types
- **Custom Relationships**: Godparent, witness, guardian, and other custom relationships as GEDCOM ASSO records

### Redesigned Export UI

- **Statistics Preview**: Real-time count of records to export, updates as you adjust filters
- **Entity Toggles**: Choose which entity types to include (people, events, sources, places)
- **Output Options**: Download to system or save to vault folder
- **Progress Modal**: Full-screen tracking with detailed phase information
- **Last Export Info**: See when you last exported and what was included

### Property & Value Alias Integration

**Critical fix**: Exporters now respect your custom property names and values. If you've configured `dob` as an alias for `birth_date`, or use `M`/`F` for sex values, they'll be correctly resolved across all formats.

### Round-Trip Fidelity

Exports now preserve **all data** imported via GEDCOM Import v2:
- Event dates with precision modifiers
- Source citations with page numbers
- Place hierarchy with coordinates
- Privacy protection settings

## Affected Formats

All four export formats upgraded with full entity support:
- GEDCOM 5.5.1
- GEDCOM X
- Gramps XML
- CSV

## Before & After

| Feature | v0.10.x | v0.11.0 |
|---------|---------|---------|
| Events exported | ❌ | ✅ All types |
| Sources exported | ❌ | ✅ With citations |
| Places exported | ❌ | ✅ With hierarchy |
| Custom relationships | ❌ | ✅ As ASSO records |
| Property aliases | ❌ | ✅ All formats |
| Export statistics | ❌ | ✅ Real-time |
| Save to vault | ❌ | ✅ Yes |

## Upgrade Notes

**No breaking changes** - existing workflows continue to work. New default includes all entity types (can be disabled via toggles).

**Recommended**: Try exporting with all entities enabled to see your complete dataset preserved.

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/compare/v0.10.20...v0.11.0
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.11.0

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
# Unified Property and Value Configuration

This release introduces a redesigned alias configuration experience in the Preferences tab, making it dramatically easier to discover and configure property and value aliases.

## Highlights

### Unified Property Configuration UI

The new interface replaces the modal-based workflow with an inline, Obsidian-native experience that shows **all** available properties and values you can alias—not just the ones you've already configured.

**Property Aliases** (55 total)
- 27 Person properties (name, born, died, birth_place, relationships, etc.)
- 20 Event properties (date, event_type, persons, place, sources, etc.)
- 8 Place properties (name, coordinates, parent_place, etc.)

**Value Aliases** (31 canonical values across 4 fields)
- Event type: 13 values (birth, death, marriage, residence, occupation, etc.)
- Sex: 4 values (male, female, nonbinary, unknown)
- Place category: 6 values (real, historical, legendary, mythological, etc.)
- Note type: 8 values (person, place, event, source, organization, etc.)

### Key Features

- **Collapsible sections** organized by entity/field type with lazy rendering for performance
- **Search and filter** across property names, descriptions, and common aliases
- **Alias count badges** showing how many aliases you've configured for each section
- **Inline editing** with auto-save on blur—no modals required
- **All sections collapsed** by default for a cleaner, less overwhelming initial view
- **Improved validation** that only triggers when you finish typing, not on every keystroke

### Why This Matters

Previously, you had to know the canonical property names before you could create aliases. Now you can **browse** all available properties, read their descriptions, and see common alternative names—making the plugin's flexibility far more discoverable.

## What's New

The new interface provides a single, unified location for all alias configuration, replacing the previous table-based approach with a more discoverable, browseable design inspired by the Tasks plugin's configuration UI.

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/compare/v0.10.18...v0.10.19
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.10.19

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## v0.10.18 - Property Alias Support Across All Note Creation

This release completes property alias support across the entire plugin, ensuring users with custom property names get a consistent experience everywhere.

### Highlights

**Full Property Alias Support** - All note creation and editing modals now respect your configured property aliases. Whether you're creating a person, place, event, source, or organization note, Canvas Roots will write properties using your custom names instead of the canonical defaults. This also extends to template-based place generation and event note properties.

**Edit Event Context Menu** - Right-click any event note in the file explorer to quickly open it in the edit modal.

**Improved Duplicate Detection** - Fuzzy name matching catches more variations when identifying potential duplicates, handling minor spelling differences and character variations.

### Bug Fixes

- Fixed Control Center showing "0% events have dates" for users with property aliases
- Fixed fictional dates not being recognized in statistics
- Fixed event type dropdown category headers being selectable
- Fixed Family Chart not centering correctly on initial load
- Fixed crash when place name frontmatter contains wikilinks
- Fixed bulk geocode writing nested coordinates instead of flat properties
- Fixed place statistics and referenced places cards showing `cr_id` instead of place names
- Fixed merge duplicates false positives for places with common prefixes
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.10.18

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Data Enhancement Pass: Generate place notes

This release introduces the "Generate place notes" batch operation, a new tool for automatically creating place notes from place references found in your person and event notes.

### New Features

- **Generate place notes** - New batch operation in Control Center → Data Quality
  - Scans person and event notes for place references in frontmatter
  - Previews places to create with search, sort, and pagination
  - Creates place notes with proper hierarchy linking
  - Updates plain text references to wikilinks
  - Individual "Create" buttons for selective generation

- **Progress tracking** - Real-time progress bar during bulk generation with cancel support

- **Paginated results** - After generation, view all created/matched places in a searchable, sortable table

- **Edit integration** - Each result row has an Edit button to open the Edit Place modal for immediate refinement
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.10.17

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## 0.10.16 - Place Management & Data Quality Improvements

### New Features

- **Flatten nested properties modal**: New batch operation in Data Quality tab to migrate legacy nested YAML (e.g., `coordinates: { lat, long }`) to flat properties (`coordinates_lat`, `coordinates_long`). Scans all Canvas Roots notes with preview before applying.

- **Place name normalization**: Create Missing Places modal now expands US state abbreviations (TX → Texas) and converts "Co" to "County", with original names saved as aliases.

- **Parent hierarchy auto-linking**: New place notes automatically link to existing parent places by parsing hierarchical names.

### Bug Fixes

- **Data Analysis showing person issues for places**: Fixed place notes incorrectly appearing in Data Quality analysis with person-specific issues like "No birth date" or "No parents defined"

- **Place statistics showing IDs**: Fixed "Most common birth/death places", "Migration patterns", and "Referenced places" displaying `cr_id` values instead of place names

- **Merge Duplicates false positives**: Fixed places with common prefixes (e.g., "San Mateo", "San Francisco") being incorrectly grouped as duplicates

- **Bulk geocode nested coordinates**: Fixed geocoding service writing legacy nested `coordinates:` format instead of flat properties

- **Bulk geocode cancel message**: Fixed false "cancelled" message when clicking Done after completion

- **Place reference matching**: Fixed GEDCOM-imported places not matching existing place notes; coordinate lookup now uses multi-strategy approach for hierarchical names

- **GEDCOM place type inference**: Improved US location type detection during import

### Improvements

- **GEDCOM Import v2: Place wikilinks**: When importing GEDCOM files with "Create place notes" enabled, person notes now reference places via wikilinks (e.g., `birth_place: "[[Dublin, Ireland]]"`) instead of plain strings. This enables Obsidian's backlinks and graph view to show place-person connections. Existing notes with plain place strings can be upgraded via the upcoming [Data Enhancement Pass](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/wiki/Roadmap#data-enhancement-pass) feature.

- **Batch preview modal enhancements**: Added search, filter, and sort controls to all batch operation preview modals
  - Search by person name
  - Filter by field type (when multiple fields exist)
  - Sort alphabetically (A→Z / Z→A toggle)
  - Scrollable table showing all entries (no more truncation)

- **Sex normalization - unrecognized values**: Preview now shows non-standard sex values (e.g., "hermaphrodite" for worldbuilding) with "(unrecognized - no change)" indicator, styled distinctly to clarify they won't be modified
- **Sex normalization clarity**: Added explanation distinguishing biological sex (used in genealogical records) from gender identity
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.10.16

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## 0.10.15 - Place Duplicate Detection & Import Improvements

This release improves duplicate place detection and GEDCOM import handling for US state abbreviations.

### Highlights

**Smarter Duplicate Detection**
- **New Pass 5**: Detects state abbreviation variants as duplicates (e.g., "Abbeville SC" ↔ "Abbeville South Carolina")
- **Improved Pass 4**: Administrative divisions (County, Parish, etc.) are now grouped separately from settlements, preventing incorrect grouping of "Abbeville County" with "Abbeville" (the city)

**GEDCOM Import Normalization**
- US state abbreviations are now automatically expanded during import:
  - `Abbeville, SC, USA` → `Abbeville, South Carolina, USA`
  - `Abbeville SC` → `Abbeville, South Carolina`
- Context-aware type inference prevents mislabeling cities as counties when sibling county notes exist

### Why This Matters

Many GEDCOM files contain inconsistent place formatting—some entries use "SC" while others use "South Carolina" for the same state. Previously, this created duplicate place notes that required manual cleanup. Now:

1. **New imports** automatically normalize state names, preventing duplicates from being created
2. **Existing duplicates** are detected by the merge modal's new Pass 5, making cleanup easier

### Full Changelog

See [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md) for complete details.
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.10.15

- **Collections Tab: Families Table** - Converted families list to paginated table format, consistent with other entity tables

### Documentation

releases.description:
## Timeline Export Improvements

This release adds Markdown timeline export, a unified export interface, and additional Excalidraw styling options.

### New: Markdown Timeline Export

Export your event timelines to four text-based formats:
- **Vertical timeline** - Styled callouts with year columns, colored dots, and event cards
- **Condensed table** - Compact markdown table with date, event, people, place, and sources columns
- **Simple list** - Bullet list grouped by year, maximum compatibility
- **Dataview query** - Dynamic timeline that updates automatically as you add events (requires Dataview plugin)

### Unified Export Card

The Events tab now features a single "Export timeline" card that consolidates all three export formats:
- **Canvas** - Native Obsidian canvas with linked nodes
- **Excalidraw** - Hand-drawn style diagrams
- **Markdown** - Text-based formats (new!)

Options dynamically show/hide based on your selected format, keeping the interface clean while providing full control.

### Excalidraw Styling Options

Full control over the hand-drawn diagram appearance:
- **Drawing style**: Architect (clean), Artist (natural), Cartoonist (rough)
- **Font**: 7 fonts including Virgil, Excalifont, Comic Shanns, Helvetica, Nunito, Lilita One, and Cascadia
- **Font size**: Adjustable from 10-32px
- **Stroke width**: 1-6px line thickness
- **Fill style**: Solid, Hachure (diagonal lines), Cross-hatch
- **Stroke style**: Solid, Dashed, Dotted
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.10.13

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## v0.10.12 - Duplicate Place Detection & Merge Modal Enhancements

This release introduces a powerful tool for cleaning up duplicate place notes after GEDCOM imports, along with significant UX improvements to the merge workflow.

### Highlights

**Merge Duplicate Place Notes**
- New tool to find and consolidate duplicate place notes
- Detects duplicates by matching name + parent place name
- Also detects duplicates by normalized `full_name` (catches "Hartford, CT" vs "Hartford, CT, USA")
- Suggests the most complete note as canonical based on scoring algorithm
- Merging updates all person references, re-parents child places, and moves duplicates to trash

**Merge Modal UX**
- Sort by most/fewest duplicates, or alphabetically
- Filter to show pending, has metadata, or has coordinates groups
- Rename the canonical file after merge (great for removing "-2" suffixes)
- Right-click open button for "Open to the right" or "Open in new window"
- Character count badges show body content length
- Full name display from GEDCOM import
- Help link to documentation

**Standardize Place Names UX**
- Clear explanation of which frontmatter fields will be updated
- Dynamic impact preview shows exactly what will change
- Button labels now show reference counts

### New Command
- `Merge duplicate place notes` - Find and merge duplicate place notes
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.10.12

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## GEDCOM Import Improvements, Place Tools & Base Templates

This release focuses on improving GEDCOM import quality, enhancing place variation detection, and adding centralized access to Obsidian Bases templates.

### New Features

- **Guide Tab: Base Templates Card**: New card providing quick access to create Obsidian Bases for all entity types—People, Places, Events, Organizations, and Sources—with one-click creation.

- **Data Quality Tab: Base Type Dropdown**: The "Create base" option now supports all five entity types via a dropdown selector.

- **New Commands**: Added `Create places base template` and `Create events base template` commands for quick access.

- **Geocode Place Context Menu**: Right-click any place note to look up coordinates via OpenStreetMap. Uses the note title and parent place for accurate geocoding.

- **Enhanced Place Variation Detection**: The "Find variations" tool now detects places with the same name but different hierarchy formats:
  - "Greene County, Tennessee, USA" ↔ "Greene County Tennessee"
  - "Scotland County, North Carolina" ↔ "Scotland County North Carolina USA"

### GEDCOM Import Fixes

- **Property Alignment**: Place notes now use `parent_place` (wikilink) and `parent_place_id` (cr_id reference) matching the Place model
- **String Normalization**: Handles messy GEDCOM data with leading commas, extra spaces, and empty hierarchy parts
- **Dedup Cache**: Now recognizes both `type` and `cr_type` properties when checking for existing places
- **Place Type Detection**: Added heuristics to infer place types (county, state, country, etc.) from naming patterns

### Notes

These changes should reduce duplicate place creation during GEDCOM imports. However, source GEDCOM files with significant data quality issues (inconsistent formatting, missing commas, typos like "USA??") may still require manual cleanup using the "Find variations" and "Build hierarchy" tools.
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.10.11

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Bug Fix: Place Hierarchy from GEDCOM Import

This release fixes a critical bug where place parent-child relationships were not being properly loaded from GEDCOM-imported data.

### Fixed

- **Place Hierarchy Not Loading**: GEDCOM-imported places were appearing as orphans (935 out of 1039 in one test case)
  - Root cause: GEDCOM importer writes `parent: "[[ParentName]]"` but the place graph service only checked for `parent_place` and `parent_place_id`
  - Now supports all three property names: `parent`, `parent_place`, and `parent_place_id`
  - Added proper wikilink-to-ID resolution in a second pass after all places are loaded

### Impact

If you've imported places from GEDCOM and noticed:
- High orphan place counts in Place Statistics
- Max hierarchy depth of 1 (flat structure)
- Places not showing parent relationships

This fix should resolve those issues. You may need to re-import your GEDCOM or the hierarchy should load correctly on next cache refresh.
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.10.10

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Control Center Improvements

This release enhances the Control Center with click-to-edit tables and a new interactive map preview.

### Click-to-Edit Tables
All entity tables now support click-to-edit:
- **People**: Click any row to open the person edit modal
- **Events**: Click any row to open the event edit modal
- **Places**: Click any row to open the place edit modal
- **Organizations**: Click any row to open the organization edit modal

Each table also has an open note button in the actions column for quick file access.

### Maps Tab: World Map Preview
- Interactive Leaflet world map preview showing real world geography
- Place markers displayed at their geographic coordinates
- Shows count of places with coordinates
- Click anywhere on the map to open the full interactive map view

### New Status Cards
- Events card showing total count and breakdown by event type
- Sources card showing total count and breakdown by source type

### Table Filtering and Sorting
All entity tables now feature consistent filtering, sorting, and pagination:
- **People**: Filter by dates/links, sort by name/birth/death
- **Places**: Filter by category/coordinates, sort by name/people/category
- **Sources**: Filter by type/confidence/media, sort by title/date/type
- **Organizations**: Filter by type/members, sort by name/type/members

### Bug Fixes
- Fixed source and event notes incorrectly appearing in the People tab
- Fixed Events tab statistics not detecting `cr_type` notes
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.10.9

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## v0.10.8 - Complete cr_type Migration

This release completes the `cr_type` migration that was started in v0.10.2.

### What Changed

All note creation and user-facing documentation now uses the namespaced `cr_type` property instead of the generic `type` property. This avoids potential conflicts with other plugins that may use the `type` field.

**Updated locations:**
- Essential properties in Control Center Guide tab
- All Templater template snippets (person, place, source, event, organization, proof summary)
- Service files that create events, sources, organizations, and schemas
- GEDCOM importer (events, sources, and places)
- Create Map modal and image-map-manager
- Empty state messages across all tabs

### Other Improvements

- Added link to wiki Templater Integration guide in the template snippets modal for advanced user script setup

### Backwards Compatibility

Notes using `type` property will continue to work - the detection system checks `cr_type` first, then falls back to `type`.

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/compare/v0.10.7...v0.10.8

releases.description:
## Settings Consolidation & Bug Fixes

This release improves the organization of folder settings and fixes a crash in the Status tab.

### Changed

- **Settings Consolidation**: Reorganized folder settings for better discoverability
  - Added Events, Organizations, Timelines, and Schemas folders to Plugin Settings
  - Created new "Advanced" section for staging isolation and folder filtering options
  - Added explanatory info boxes in both Plugin Settings and Preferences tab
  - Import/Export tab now shows folder summary with link to Preferences for configuration

### Fixed

releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.10.7

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## v0.10.6 - Bug Fix & Add cr_id Context Menu

### Bug Fix

**Bidirectional relationship sync now preserves existing frontmatter**

Extended the fix from v0.10.5 to also cover bidirectional relationship sync operations. Previously, when adding parents, spouses, or children via the context menu, the sync operation could corrupt existing list properties containing wikilinks (e.g., `[[Gaeleri]]` becoming `[[[Gaeleri]]]`).

Files fixed:
- `src/core/bidirectional-linker.ts` - Now uses `processFrontMatter` API
- `src/core/relationship-manager.ts` - Now uses `processFrontMatter` API

### New Feature

**"Add cr_id" context menu action**

A new context menu action that adds only a `cr_id` to notes, without adding all essential properties. This is useful when you want to make a note trackable by Canvas Roots without adding placeholder fields.

- Appears alongside "Add essential properties" in all context menus
- Available for single files, multi-file selection, and folders
- Detects note type and uses appropriate prefix:
  - Person notes: `abc-123-def-456`
  - Place notes: `place_abc-123-def-456`
  - Event notes: `event_abc-123-def-456`
- Skips notes that already have a `cr_id`

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/compare/v0.10.5...v0.10.6
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.10.6

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## v0.10.5 - Bug Fix & Templater Documentation

### Bug Fix

**"Add essential properties" now preserves existing frontmatter**

Fixed a critical bug where the "Add essential properties" context menu action would corrupt existing list properties containing wikilinks. For example, `[[Gaeleri]]` would incorrectly become `[[[Gaeleri]]]`.

The fix uses Obsidian's `processFrontMatter` API, which safely modifies only the specified properties without re-serializing the entire frontmatter.

### Documentation

**Templater Integration Guide**

Added comprehensive documentation for using the [Templater](https://github.com/SilentVoid13/Templater) plugin with Canvas Roots:

- Explains the `cr_id` format (`abc-123-def-456`)
- Provides both inline template snippets and reusable user script approaches
- Includes complete example templates for Person, Place, Event, and Source notes
- Tips for folder-specific template automation

[Read the Templater Integration guide](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/wiki/Templater-Integration)

The Guide tab in Control Center now links to this documentation.

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/compare/v0.10.4...v0.10.5

releases.description:
## v0.10.3: Type Customization

Full type manager for Events, Sources, Organizations, Relationships, and Places. Create, edit, hide, and customize types and categories with user-defined names.

### Type Managers

Each Control Center tab now includes a "Manage types" card:

| Tab | Types | Features |
|-----|-------|----------|
| Events | 22 built-in event types | Custom types, categories (Core, Extended, Narrative), icon/color |
| Sources | 6 built-in source types | Custom types, categories, description |
| Organizations | 11 built-in org types | Custom types, categories |
| Relationships | 17 built-in relationship types | Custom types, color, line style |
| Places | 15 built-in place types | Custom types, categories, hierarchy level |

### Features

**Type Management:**
- Create custom types with full customization
- Override built-in types (change name, description, icon, color)
- Hide types from dropdowns while preserving existing notes
- Reset customized built-in types to defaults
- Delete user-created types

**Category Management:**
- Create custom categories to group related types
- Rename built-in categories to match your terminology
- Reorder categories with sort order field
- Hide unused categories (built-in or custom)
- "Show all" button to restore hidden categories

**Place Type Hierarchy:**
- Hierarchy levels (0-99) determine valid parent-child relationships
- Categories organize the UI independently of hierarchy
- Quick level presets for common positions

### Use Cases

- Rename "birth" to "nameday" for fantasy world-building
- Add "coronation" and "succession" event types for dynasty tracking
- Create "Land Records" source type for property research
- Hide unused relationship types like "apprentice" or "mentor"
- Add custom place categories like "Bodies of Water"

---

**Full Changelog:** See [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.10.3

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## v0.10.2 - Flexible Note Type Detection

**New Standard: `cr_type` Property**

Canvas Roots now uses `cr_type` as the default type property for new installations, avoiding conflicts with other plugins that use `type` (Templater, Dataview, etc.).

**Detection Methods (checked in order):**
1. `cr_type` property (new default)
2. `type` property (legacy fallback)
3. Tags (`#person`, `#place`, `#event`, etc.)

**Migration Tool**

A new Data Quality check flags notes using the legacy `type` property. Use the "Migrate legacy type property" batch operation to convert `type` → `cr_type` across your vault.

**Backwards Compatibility**
- Existing users automatically keep `type` as primary
- Both properties are always checked
- No immediate migration required
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.10.2

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## GEDCOM Import v2

This release introduces a major upgrade to GEDCOM importing with support for creating multiple note types, progress tracking for large imports, and flexible filename options.

### New Features

**Enhanced GEDCOM Import**
- Create **event notes** from GEDCOM events (births, deaths, marriages, and 30+ other event types)
- Create **source notes** from GEDCOM `SOUR` records with full metadata
- Create **hierarchical place notes** that parse `City, County, State, Country` structure
- Per-note-type toggles: choose which note types to create
- Option to skip people notes if you already have them in your vault

**Filename Format Options**
- Three formats: Original (`John Smith.md`), Kebab-case (`john-smith.md`), Snake_case (`john_smith.md`)
- "Customize per note type" toggle for fine-grained control over each note type

**Import Progress Modal**
- Visual feedback during large imports (2k+ people)
- Phase indicator, progress bar, and running statistics

**Smart Place Duplicate Detection**
- Case-insensitive matching prevents duplicate place notes
- Updates existing places instead of creating duplicates

### Property Alias Improvements

**GEDCOM Alignment: `sex` is now the canonical property**

- The internal canonical property has been renamed from `gender` to `sex` to align with GEDCOM 5.5.1 standard (which uses the `SEX` tag)
- **Backwards compatible**: Existing notes with `gender` continue to work - the plugin checks `sex` first, then falls back to `gender`
- Both `sex` and `gender` are available in the property alias dropdown, so users can:
  - Keep using `gender` if preferred
  - Track both properties separately (biological sex vs. gender identity)
  - Map custom property names to either

**New canonical properties available for aliasing:**
- `type` - For flexible note type detection
- `sex` - GEDCOM-aligned sex/gender field

### Bug Fixes

**People Tab Performance**
- Fixed crash when viewing People tab with large imports (2k+ people)
- Added pagination (100 at a time) and removed expensive badge calculations

**GEDCOM Analysis Performance**
- Fixed freeze when selecting large GEDCOM files
- Optimized algorithm from O(n×m) to O(n+m) with pre-built family lookup index

### Changed

- Numbering system modal no longer appears automatically after import (added "Skip" button)

---

**Full Changelog:** [0.10.0...0.10.1](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/compare/v0.10.0...v0.10.1)
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.10.1

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Chronological Story Mapping Release

This release completes the **Chronological Story Mapping** roadmap item, adding comprehensive [event and timeline management](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/wiki/Events-And-Timelines) for genealogists, worldbuilders, and writers.

### Highlights

- **Event Notes** — New `type: event` notes with 22 built-in event types across Core (birth, death, marriage, divorce), Extended (residence, occupation, military, etc.), and Narrative (plot_point, flashback, climax, etc.) categories
- **Timeline Views** — Person timelines, family timelines (aggregating events for a person + spouses + children), and place timelines (events at a location over time)
- **Timeline Export** — Export to Canvas or Excalidraw with horizontal, vertical, or Gantt layouts; color-code by event type, category, or confidence
- **Groups/Factions** — Tag events with groups for filtering by nation, faction, or organization
- **Sort Order Computation** — Automatic topological ordering from `before`/`after` relationships for Obsidian Bases sorting
- **Source Event Extraction** — Extract events from source notes with smart suggestions based on source type

### New Features

**Event Management**
- Create Event Modal for manual event creation
- 22 built-in event types with custom type support
- Relative ordering with `before`/`after` constraints when exact dates are unknown
- Events Base template with 20 pre-configured views

**Timeline Views**
- Person Timeline — Calendar badge on person list items expands to show all events
- Family Timeline — Users badge shows aggregate events for person + spouses + children
- Place Timeline — View events at a location over time with family presence analysis
- Global Timeline — Filter and browse all events with gap analysis

**Timeline Export**
- Three layout styles: Horizontal, Vertical, Gantt (by date and person)
- Color schemes: Event type, Category, Confidence, Monochrome
- Filter by person, event type, or group/faction
- Include before/after relationship edges
- Per-canvas style overrides preserved during regeneration

**Context Menus**
- Person list items: Create event, export timeline
- Person note files: Create event, export timeline
- Source rows: Extract events
- Folders: New events base from template

### Documentation

- [Events & Timelines](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/wiki/Events-And-Timelines) — Complete feature documentation
- [Frontmatter Reference](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/wiki/Frontmatter-Reference) — Event note properties

### 🙏 Acknowledgments

Thanks to our user research participants who helped shape this feature with valuable feedback on timeline visualization, relative ordering, and export workflows.

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/compare/v0.9.4...v0.10.0
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.10.0

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
# Value Aliases

Use custom property values without editing your notes.

This release extends the [Property Aliases](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/wiki/Roadmap#property-aliases-) feature (v0.9.3) to support custom property **values**. Worldbuilders and users with existing vaults can now use their own terminology for event types, gender, and place categories without modifying existing notes.

## What's New

**[Value Aliases](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/wiki/Roadmap#value-aliases)** — Map custom values to Canvas Roots canonical values:

- **Event types**: Map `nameday` → `birth`, `coronation` → `custom`, etc.
- **Gender**: Map `m` → `male`, `f` → `female`, `nb` → `nonbinary`
- **Place categories**: Map `canon` → `fictional`, `apocryphal` → `disputed`

Configure in **Control Center → Preferences → Aliases**. The card now has two sections:
- **Property names**: Your property names → Canvas Roots fields (existing feature)
- **Property values**: Your values → canonical values (new)

### How It Works

| Scenario | Behavior |
|----------|----------|
| Reading notes | Canonical values checked first, then aliases |
| Unknown event types | Gracefully resolve to `custom` |
| Creating/importing notes | Uses your aliased values |

### Example

If your worldbuilding vault uses `nameday` instead of `birth`:

1. Add a value alias: `nameday` → `birth` (Event type)
2. Canvas Roots recognizes `event_type: nameday` as a birth event
3. Birth events appear with the correct icon on maps and timelines
4. GEDCOM imports write `nameday` to preserve your terminology

### Canonical Values Reference

| Field | Values |
|-------|--------|
| Event type | `birth`, `death`, `marriage`, `burial`, `residence`, `occupation`, `education`, `military`, `immigration`, `baptism`, `confirmation`, `ordination`, `custom` |
| Gender | `male`, `female`, `nonbinary`, `unknown` |
| Place category | `real`, `historical`, `disputed`, `legendary`, `mythological`, `fictional` |

### Other Changes

- Added "Nested properties' issue category for detecting non-flat frontmatter in Control Center > Data Quality tab. Scans for nested YAML structures.
- Place edit modal now writes flat properties
  - `coordinates_lat` and `coordinates_long` instead of nested `coordinates: { lat, long }`
  - `custom_coordinates_x`, `custom_coordinates_y`, `custom_coordinates_map` instead of nested `custom_coordinates: { x, y, map }`
  - Update function also removes any legacy nested properties when updating
- Add bases folder setting to configure where base files are created
- Standardized on Gender terminology with nonbinary support
  - Changed person modal from "Sex" to "Gender" with all four options
  - Added nonbinary color support to canvas generator (yellow)
  - Added nonbinary color support to tree preview
  - Updated data quality validation to accept all canonical genders

### Documentation

- [Settings and Configuration](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/wiki/Settings-And-Configuration) — Updated with Value Aliases section
- [Frontmatter Reference](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/wiki/Frontmatter-Reference) — Updated with canonical values tables
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.9.4

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Property Aliases

Use your own property names without renaming frontmatter! Canvas Roots now supports [property aliases](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/wiki/Settings-And-Configuration#property-aliases) that let you map custom property names to Canvas Roots fields.

### What's New

[Property Aliases](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/wiki/Settings-And-Configuration#property-aliases) - Map custom frontmatter property names to Canvas Roots fields
- Configure aliases in Control Center → Preferences → Property Aliases
- Supports all person note properties: identity, dates, places, relationships
- Read resolution: canonical property first, then falls back to aliases
- Write integration: imports create notes with aliased property names
- Essential Properties card displays aliased property names when configured
- Bases templates generated with aliased property names

**Settings & Configuration Wiki Page** - New comprehensive documentation covering all settings

### How It Works

If your vault uses `birthdate` instead of `born`:

1. Add an alias: `birthdate` → `born`
2. Canvas Roots reads your property and treats it as the birth date
3. When importing GEDCOM/CSV, new notes use your aliased property names
4. Bases templates reference your aliased property names

Your frontmatter stays unchanged—Canvas Roots adapts to you.

### Supported Properties

| Category | Properties |
|----------|------------|
| Identity | `name`, `cr_id`, `gender`, `nickname`, `maiden_name` |
| Dates | `born`, `died` |
| Places | `birth_place`, `death_place` |
| Relationships | `father`, `mother`, `spouse`, `child` (and `_id` variants) |
| Other | `occupation`, `universe`, `image`, `sourced_facts`, `relationships` |

### Screenshots

![Property Aliases Settings](docs/images/canvas-roots-control-center-prefs-tab.png)

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Evidence Visualization Release

This release transforms Canvas Roots into a GPS-aligned research platform with fact-level source tracking, proof summaries, and visual conflict indicators.

### Highlights

- **Fact-Level Source Tracking**: Track which specific facts (birth, death, marriage, etc.) have source citations using the new `sourced_facts` property
- **Proof Summary Notes**: Document your reasoning with structured proof notes linking evidence to conclusions
- **Research Gaps Report**: Quickly identify under-researched facts across your tree
- **Canvas Conflict Markers**: See at a glance which people have unresolved source conflicts (⚠️ indicator)
- **Enhanced Source Indicators**: Coverage percentage now shown alongside source count (📎 3 · 75%)

### New Features

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `sourced_facts` property | Per-fact source arrays on person notes |
| Source quality classification | Primary, Secondary, Derivative ratings per Evidence Explained |
| Proof summary notes | `type: proof_summary` with evidence linking and confidence levels |
| Research Gaps Report | Data Quality tab shows unsourced facts with priority ranking |
| Source Conflict Detection | Identifies proof summaries with conflicting evidence |
| Canvas conflict markers | ⚠️ N indicator at top-left of person nodes |
| Research coverage overlay | 📎 N · % with color coding by coverage level |

### Settings

| Setting | Default | Description |
|---------|---------|-------------|
| `trackFactSourcing` | false | Enable fact-level source tracking |
| `factCoverageThreshold` | 6 | Number of facts for 100% coverage |
| `showResearchGapsInStatus` | true | Show research gaps in Status tab |

### For Serious Genealogists

This release aligns Canvas Roots with the [Genealogical Proof Standard (GPS)](https://www.bcgcertification.org/ethics-standards/gps/). You can now:

1. Track exactly which facts have evidence
2. Rate source quality (primary/secondary/derivative)
3. Document proof arguments with confidence levels
4. Identify and resolve conflicting evidence
5. See research status at a glance on your canvas

All evidence features are **opt-in** via the `trackFactSourcing` setting - casual users won't see any changes.

### Full Changelog

See [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md) for complete details.
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.9.0

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Evidence & Source Management Release

This release introduces **source indicators** on generated family trees, helping you visualize research quality at a glance.

### New Features

#### Source Indicators on Generated Trees
- Display badges like "📎 3" on tree nodes showing how many source notes link to each person
- **Color coding**: Green badges for well-documented ancestors (3+ sources), yellow for partially documented (1-2 sources)
- Nodes without sources show no badge, making research gaps immediately visible
- Toggle in Settings → Canvas Roots → Canvas styling → "Show source indicators"

### Documentation

- **New wiki page**: [Evidence & Sources](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/wiki/Evidence-And-Sources) - comprehensive guide to source management
  - Source note schema with 13 source types (census, vital_record, photograph, etc.)
  - Linking sources to people via wikilinks
  - Sources Bases template with 17 pre-configured views
  - Best practices for organizing source notes

- Updated [Tree Generation](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/wiki/Tree-Generation) with source indicators documentation
- Updated [Frontmatter Reference](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/wiki/Frontmatter-Reference) with source property names

### How It Works

1. Create source notes with `type: source` in frontmatter
2. Link sources to people using wikilinks in the source note body (e.g., `[[John Smith]]`)
3. Enable "Show source indicators" in settings
4. Generate a family tree - badges appear automatically

This feature helps identify which ancestors need more research and celebrates well-documented individuals in your tree.

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/compare/v0.7.0...v0.8.0

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
# Canvas Roots v0.7.0 - World-Building Suite

This release introduces three major features designed for world-builders, fiction writers, and anyone tracking complex non-genealogical relationships.

## New Features

### Custom Relationships
Extended relationship types beyond standard family links for tracking godparents, guardians, mentors, and more.

- **12 built-in relationship types** across 4 categories:
  - Legal/Guardianship: Guardian/Ward, Adoptive Parent/Child, Foster Parent/Child
  - Religious/Spiritual: Godparent/Godchild, Mentor/Disciple
  - Professional: Master/Apprentice
  - Social: Witness (symmetric)
- **Relationships Tab** in Control Center for management and statistics
- **Colored canvas edges** for custom relationships on tree visualizations
- **Context menu integration**: Right-click any person note to add relationships

### Fictional Date Systems
Custom calendars and eras for world-building projects.

- **Built-in presets**: Middle-earth (First/Second/Third Age), Westeros (AC/BC), Star Wars (BBY/ABY), Generic Fantasy
- **Era definitions** with name, abbreviation, and epoch year
- **Date parsing** for formats like "TA 2941" or "283 AC"
- **Age calculation** within calendar systems
- **Universe-scoped** calendar systems
- **Date Systems card** in Canvas Settings tab for configuration

### Organization Notes
Track non-genealogical hierarchies such as noble houses, guilds, corporations, and military units.

- **8 organization types**: Noble House, Guild, Corporation, Military Unit, Religious Order, Political Entity, Educational, Custom
- **Organization hierarchy** with parent/child relationships
- **Person memberships** with roles and temporal data (from/to dates)
- **Organizations Tab** in Control Center with statistics and type management
- **Obsidian Bases integration**: Pre-configured template with 17 views
- **Commands**: Create organization note, Open organizations tab, Create organizations base template

## Changes

- **Status Tab**: Renamed "Relationships" card to "Family links" to distinguish from custom relationships
- **Tab Reorganization**: Merged Staging tab content into Import/Export tab

## Removed

- **Advanced Tab**: Retired from Control Center
  - Logging settings moved to plugin's native Settings tab
  - "Create base template" button moved to Data Quality tab

## Documentation

- New wiki pages: [Custom Relationships](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/wiki/Custom-Relationships), [Fictional Date Systems](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/wiki/Fictional-Date-Systems), [Organization Notes](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/wiki/Organization-Notes)
- Updated [Frontmatter Reference](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/wiki/Frontmatter-Reference) with organization and membership properties

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/compare/v0.6.3...v0.7.0
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.7.0

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## What's New in v0.6.3

### Schema Validation

Define custom validation schemas to enforce data consistency across your person notes. Schemas help catch missing fields, invalid data types, and logical inconsistencies.

**Key Features:**

- **Schema Notes**: New note type (`type: schema`) with validation rules in a JSON code block
- **Schemas Tab**: Dedicated Control Center tab for creating, editing, and managing schemas
- **UI-First Design**: Create schemas entirely through the modal interface—no manual JSON editing required
- **Flexible Scopes**: Apply schemas to all people, specific collections, folders, or universes
- **Property Validation**:
  - Required properties
  - Type checking (string, number, date, boolean, enum, wikilink, array)
  - Enum validation with allowed values
  - Number range validation (min/max)
  - Wikilink target validation (verify linked notes have correct type)
- **Conditional Requirements**: Make properties required based on other field values
- **Custom Constraints**: Write JavaScript expressions for cross-property validation (e.g., "death date must be after birth date")
- **Data Quality Integration**: Schema violations appear in the Data Quality tab

**New Commands:**
- `Canvas Roots: Open schemas tab`
- `Canvas Roots: Validate vault against schemas`

**Context Menu Actions:**
- Person notes: "Validate against schemas"
- Schema notes: "Edit schema", "Validate matching notes"

### Example Schema

```yaml
---
type: schema
cr_id: schema-date-validation
name: Date Validation
applies_to_type: all
---
{
  "requiredProperties": ["name"],
  "properties": {
    "born": { "type": "date" },
    "died": { "type": "date" }
  },
  "constraints": [
    {
      "rule": "!died || born",
      "message": "Cannot have death date without birth date"
    }
  ]
}
```

### Documentation

- [Schema Validation Wiki](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/wiki/Schema-Validation)
- [Frontmatter Reference](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/wiki/Frontmatter-Reference#schema-note-properties)

---

**Full Changelog:** https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md

## v0.6.2 - Maps Tab & Control Center Improvements

This release adds a dedicated **Maps Tab** to the Control Center, consolidating all map visualization and custom map management into one place. The Guide tab has been streamlined, the Status tab now shows comprehensive vault statistics, and tab ordering has been improved.

### Maps Tab

A new tab in the Control Center for managing maps and visualizations:

- **Open Map View** - Quick access button with coordinate coverage stats
- **Custom Maps Gallery** - Visual thumbnail grid of all your custom map images
  - Image previews with name and universe badges
  - Hover to reveal Edit and Menu buttons
  - Click to open directly in Map View
- **Visualizations** - Migration diagrams and place network tools
- **Map Statistics** - Coordinate coverage, map counts, and universe list

### Custom Map Management

Full create/read/update/delete operations for custom map notes:

- **Create Map** - New modal with image picker, bounds configuration, and universe assignment
- **Edit Map** - Update existing map properties
- **Duplicate Map** - Clone maps with auto-generated unique IDs
- **Export/Import JSON** - Share map configurations between vaults
- **Delete Map** - Remove maps with confirmation

### Status Tab Enhancements

The Status tab now provides a comprehensive vault overview:

- **Places card** - Total places, places with coordinates, breakdown by category
- **Custom Maps card** - Total maps count and list of universes
- **Canvases card** - Total canvas files in vault

### Guide Tab Overhaul

The Guide tab has been streamlined from 19 cards to 5 focused sections:

- Collapsible reference sections for Person, Place, and Map note properties
- Task grid for quick navigation to common features
- Integrated wiki links for detailed documentation
- Clear 3-step "Getting Started" workflow

### Other Changes

- **Quick Actions Tab removed** - Features moved to Tree Output and Advanced tabs
- **Data Quality Tab repositioned** - Now appears after Collections for better workflow

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## What's New

**Interactive Map View** - A Leaflet.js-powered map for visualizing where your family members (or fictional characters) lived, traveled, and died.

### Map Features
- **Color-coded markers** for birth, death, marriage, burial, and life events (residence, occupation, education, military, immigration, religious, custom)
- **Marker clustering** for dense areas
- **Migration paths** (birth → death) with directional arrows and person name labels
- **Journey paths** connecting all life events chronologically
- **Time slider animation** - see "who was alive when?" across generations
- **Heat map layer** for geographic concentration
- **Place search**, fullscreen mode, and mini-map
- **Split view** for side-by-side map comparison
- **GeoJSON and SVG export**

### Custom Image Maps for World-Builders
- Use your own map images for fictional worlds or historical maps
- **Pixel coordinate system** (L.CRS.Simple) - place markers using image pixel coordinates
- **Interactive alignment** (Edit Mode) - drag corners to align your map image
- **Universe-based filtering** - switch between different fictional worlds

### Events System
- New `events` array in person frontmatter for tracking life events beyond birth/death/marriage
- Supported event types: residence, occupation, education, military, immigration, baptism, confirmation, ordination, custom
- Full import/export support (GEDCOM, GEDCOM X, Gramps XML, CSV)

### Also in This Release
- **GEDCOM X export** (JSON format)
- **Gramps XML export**

## Documentation
- [User Guide: Interactive Map View](docs/user-guide.md#interactive-map-view)
- [Architecture: Leaflet Maps Plan](docs/architecture/leaflet-maps-plan.md)
- [Frontmatter Schema: Events Array](docs/reference/frontmatter-schema.md)

## Upgrading
No breaking changes. Existing vaults will work without modification.

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## v0.5.2 - Geographic Features

This release introduces comprehensive geographic/place-based features for Canvas Roots, enabling users to create place notes, track where people were born, died, married, and lived, and visualize migration patterns.

### New Features

#### Place Notes System
- **Place note schema** with support for hierarchical relationships (city → state → country)
- **Six place categories**: real, historical, disputed, legendary, mythological, fictional
- **Universe support** for organizing fictional/mythological places
- **Historical names** tracking for places that changed names over time
- **Coordinates** support for real-world and custom map systems

#### Place Statistics (Control Center)
- Overview statistics: total places, coordinate coverage, orphan detection
- Breakdown by category with associated person counts
- Most common birth/death places ranking
- Migration pattern detection (birth → death location flows)
- Place hierarchy issue detection

#### Place Management Actions
- Create missing place notes from person references
- Build hierarchy wizard for orphan places
- Standardize place name variations
- View alphabetical place index with person counts

#### Visualizations (D3-based)
- **Network/Schematic View**: Places as nodes sized by population, with tree and radial layouts
- **Migration Flow Diagram**: Arc diagram showing movement patterns between places
- Time period filtering with century presets
- Collection (family branch) filtering
- Hierarchy level aggregation

#### UX Improvements
- Searchable parent place picker grouped by place type
- Manual coordinate entry with validation
- Quick-create places from person notes via context menu
- Auto-create parent place workflow
- Custom place types beyond built-in options
- Geocoding lookup via Nominatim API
- Places Base template with 14 pre-configured views

#### Control Center Updates
- Renamed "Data entry" tab to "People" for clarity
- New Create Person modal with relationship pickers (father, mother, spouse)
- Unlinked place badges with create buttons in person list
- Dedicated places folder setting
- Place-based tree filtering (birth, death, marriage, burial locations)

### Coming Soon
- Phase 4: Full map support with Leaflet.js and OpenStreetMap integration

## v0.5.1 - Canvas Navigation & Import Format Expansion

This release enhances canvas navigation for split trees and adds support for two new import formats.

### Canvas Navigation Enhancements

- **Navigation portal nodes** - Split canvases now include clickable portal nodes that link related canvases together, making it easy to navigate between segments of a large family tree
- **Master overview canvas** - The Split Canvas Wizard can now generate an overview canvas with a grid layout showing links to all split canvases, providing a central hub for navigation

### New Import Formats

- **GEDCOM X (JSON)** - Import genealogical data from FamilySearch and other sources that export in the GEDCOM X JSON format
- **Gramps XML** - Import family trees directly from Gramps genealogy software's native XML format

Both new formats are available in the Import/Export tab and support the full staging workflow for reviewing data before creating person notes.

### Improvements

- Format dropdown in Import/Export tab now dynamically shows only formats that support the selected direction (GEDCOM X and Gramps XML are import-only)
- Updated Guide tab with documentation for all new features

releases.description:
## Canvas Navigation & Organization

This release introduces the **Split Canvas Wizard**, a powerful tool for breaking large family trees into manageable, interconnected canvases.

### Split Canvas Wizard

Access via canvas context menu → Canvas Roots → Split canvas wizard

**Six split methods:**

- **By generation** - Split every N generations (configurable)
- **By branch** - Separate paternal/maternal ancestor lines
- **Single lineage** - Extract direct line between two specific people
- **By collection** - One canvas per user-defined collection
- **Ancestor + descendant pair** - Create linked ancestor and descendant canvases for the same root person
- **By surname** - Extract all people sharing a surname, even without established family connections

**Surname split features:**
- Scrollable list of surnames sorted by frequency
- Multi-surname selection
- Options to include spouses and match maiden names
- Create separate canvas per surname or combine into one

**Full wizard workflow:**
- Preview expected canvas count and people before generating
- Generate canvas files directly from the wizard
- Completion screen shows success/error status with list of created files
- Configuration summary for reference

### Guide Tab

The Control Center's Guide tab now includes documentation for the Split Canvas Wizard, explaining all six split methods and how to access them.

### What's Next

Future enhancements will add navigation nodes linking between related canvases and master overview canvases with links to detailed views.

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/compare/v0.4.0...v0.5.0
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.5.0

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## v0.4.0 - Import Cleanup & Merge Tools

This release introduces a comprehensive staging workflow for safely processing GEDCOM imports, along with powerful merge tools for consolidating duplicate records and data quality analysis.

### New Features

**Staging Workflow**
- Configure a dedicated staging folder that's automatically isolated from your main tree
- Import GEDCOM or CSV files to staging for review before incorporating into your main data
- Staging folder excluded from tree generation, duplicate detection, relationship sync, and vault statistics

**Cross-Import Duplicate Detection**
- Detect duplicates between staging imports and your existing main tree
- Side-by-side comparison modal for reviewing potential matches
- Mark matches as "Same person" or "Different people" with persistent resolution tracking

**Merge Wizard**
- Combine duplicate records with field-level conflict resolution
- Choose which value to keep for each field (name, dates, places, etc.)
- "Both" option for array fields to combine spouses/children from both records
- Automatic relationship reconciliation updates all references to the merged record
- Available from both duplicate detection and cross-import review modals

**Data Quality Tools**
- Quality score (0-100) based on data completeness and consistency
- Issue detection across 5 categories: date inconsistencies, relationship problems, missing data, format issues, orphan references
- 15+ specific issue types detected (birth after death, circular references, unrealistic ages, etc.)
- Filter issues by category and severity (error/warning/info)
- Batch normalization: standardize date formats to YYYY-MM-DD
- Batch normalization: standardize gender values to M/F
- Batch normalization: clear orphan parent references
- Preview changes before applying any batch operation

**Staging Tab in Control Center**
- View staging subfolders with person counts and modification dates
- Promote individual subfolders or all staging to main tree
- Delete staging subfolders you no longer need
- Quick statistics for staging area

**Data Quality Tab in Control Center**
- Visual quality score and completeness statistics
- Scrollable issue list with clickable person names for navigation
- Category and severity filters for focused review
- Batch operation buttons with preview modals

**Combined Import/Export Tab**
- Unified interface replaces separate GEDCOM and CSV tabs
- Format dropdown (GEDCOM/CSV) and Direction dropdown (Import/Export)
- Inline folder configuration section—set up people folder, staging folder, and isolation settings without leaving Control Center

**Folder Filtering**
- Control which folders Canvas Roots scans for person notes
- Exclusion list mode: ignore specific folders (templates, archive, etc.)
- Inclusion list mode: only scan specified folders

### Typical Workflow

1. Import `old-family-tree.ged` to staging subfolder `smith-2024`
2. Open Staging tab → "Review matches with main tree"
3. For each match: Same person → Merge, or Different people → will be promoted
4. Click "Promote subfolder" to move unique people to main
5. Duplicates were already merged—your main tree now has consolidated data
6. Open Data Quality tab to identify and fix any remaining issues

### Full Changelog

See [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md) for complete details.

## v0.3.0 Release: Interactive Family Chart View

This release introduces the **Interactive Family Chart View** — a new way to explore and edit your family trees directly within Obsidian.

### What's New
- **Interactive visualization**: Pan, zoom, and navigate family trees with smooth animations
- **Click-to-explore**: Click any person to center the view or open their note
- **Built-in editing**: Add, modify, and delete relationships directly in the chart
- **Undo/Redo support**: Full edit history for confident editing
- **Bidirectional sync**: Changes automatically update your markdown notes
- **Export to PNG or SVG**: Save high-quality images of your family tree

Your view settings (color scheme, spacing, zoom level) are preserved across sessions.
