releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
# Canvas Roots v0.2.9

This release adds significant new features for genealogists and world-builders: reference numbering systems, lineage tracking, relationship history with undo, privacy protection for GEDCOM exports, and folder statistics.

## Fixed

- **Context Menu Submenu Nesting**: Fixed bug where submenu items appeared as siblings instead of children
  - "Generate Canvas tree" and "Generate Excalidraw tree" now correctly appear inside "Generate tree" submenu
  - Root cause: Obsidian's `Menu.addItem()` returns the parent menu for chaining, not the submenu created by `.setSubmenu()`

## New Features

### Reference Numbering Systems
Assign standard genealogical reference numbers to family members:
- **Ahnentafel** - Ancestor numbering (1=self, 2=father, 3=mother, 4=paternal grandfather...)
- **d'Aboville** - Descendant numbering with dot notation (1, 1.1, 1.2, 1.1.1...)
- **Henry System** - Compact descendant numbering without dots (1, 11, 12, 111...)
- **Generation** - Relative generation depth (0=self, -1=parents, +1=children)

Access via command palette or right-click context menu on person notes. Numbers are stored in frontmatter and automatically available in Bases views.

### Lineage Tracking
Compute and track multi-generational lineages from root persons:
- Patrilineal (father's line), matrilineal (mother's line), or all descendants
- `lineage` array property supports multiple lineage memberships
- Suggested lineage names based on surname (e.g., "Smith Line")
- Filter and analyze descendant lines in Bases views

### Relationship History & Undo
Track and reverse relationship changes made through Canvas Roots:
- History modal showing all changes with timestamps
- Statistics by change type (add parent, add spouse, add child, etc.)
- One-click undo for any change
- Configurable retention period with automatic cleanup

### Privacy Protection
Optional privacy controls for living persons in GEDCOM exports:
- Configurable birth year threshold (default: 100 years)
- Exclude or anonymize personal details for potentially living persons
- Maintain family structure while protecting PII

### Folder Statistics
View comprehensive statistics about person notes in any folder:
- Data completeness metrics
- Relationship health reports
- Family structure analysis (gender distribution, generation depth)

### Enhanced Bases Template
Expanded from 16 to 22 pre-configured views:
- By lineage, By generation number
- Ahnentafel ordered, d'Aboville ordered, Henry ordered
- Without lineage

## Other Improvements
- Multi-vault deploy script support
- Improved error handling for Base template creation
- Bases plugin detection with confirmation modal

## Full Changelog
See [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md) for complete version history.

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
## Canvas Roots v0.2.3-beta - UI Consolidation & Workflow Improvements

This release streamlines tree generation workflows, adds powerful bulk property management, and introduces alternative layout algorithms for different visualization needs.

---

## UI Consolidation & Quick Tree Generation

### Tree Output Tab (Renamed from "Tree Generation")
- **Clearer naming** that reflects both generation and export capabilities
- **New Export Tree section** with step-by-step Excalidraw export instructions
- All tree-related output operations now consolidated in one place

### "Generate Tree" Context Menu
Right-click any person note to access the new **"Generate tree"** submenu with two streamlined options:

**"Generate Canvas tree"**
- Opens Tree Output tab with full control
- Configure tree type, generations, layout algorithm, styling
- Perfect when you need precise control

**"Generate Excalidraw tree"** ⚡ *New instant generation*
- One-click tree generation with sensible defaults
- Full tree, 5 generations, automatic file naming
- File format: `Family Tree - [Person Name].excalidraw.md`
- No configuration needed - ideal for quick exports

**Design Philosophy**: Canvas for full control, Excalidraw for speed. Choose the workflow that fits your need.

---

### Interactive Tree Preview

**Interactive tree preview** with pan/zoom controls, color schemes, and export capabilities.

**Key Features:**
- **Real-time SVG preview**: See your family tree layout before generating the canvas
- **Interactive controls**: Pan, zoom, and zoom-to-fit buttons for easy navigation
- **Color scheme options**: Switch between Gender, Generation, and Monochrome coloring
- **Hover tooltips**: View person details (name, birth/death dates) on hover
- **Export functionality**: Save preview as PNG (high-resolution) or SVG (vector format)

The tree preview appears in the Control Center's "Tree Output" tab after configuring your tree parameters. This allows you to verify layout, spacing, and relationships before committing to canvas generation.

**Usage:**
1. Configure tree settings in the Tree Generation tab
2. Switch to Tree Output tab to see the preview
3. Use pan/zoom controls to explore the layout
4. Change color schemes to visualize different data aspects
5. Export as PNG or SVG for external use
6. Click "Generate to canvas" when satisfied with the layout

This feature is particularly useful for large family trees (50+ people) where you want to verify the layout before generating the final canvas.

---

## Essential Properties Feature

Quickly prepare person notes with all essential properties for optimal plugin functionality.

### "Add Essential Properties" Context Menu
- Right-click any markdown file (single or multi-select)
- Choose **"Canvas Roots: Add essential properties"**
- Adds all **9 essential properties** if missing:
  - `cr_id` - Unique identifier (auto-generated)
  - `name` - Person's name (uses filename if blank)
  - `born` - Birth date
  - `died` - Death date
  - `father` - Father relationship
  - `mother` - Mother relationship
  - `spouses` - Spouse relationships (array)
  - `children` - Children relationships (array)
  - `group_name` - Family group name

### Smart Features
✅ **Non-destructive** - Only adds missing properties, preserves existing data  
✅ **Smart visibility** - Menu only appears when properties are missing  
✅ **Multi-file support** - Bulk process multiple notes at once  
✅ **Dual storage aware** - Recognizes both wikilink (`father`) and ID (`father_id`) properties  
✅ **Progress feedback** - Shows summary: "3 updated, 2 already complete"

### Use Cases
- Prepare blank notes before data entry
- Upgrade legacy person notes
- Batch process imported or incomplete notes
- Ensure consistency across your family tree

---

## Complete Person Notes by Default

All person note creation now includes essential properties automatically.

### What's Changed
**Data Entry tab**: All created notes include 9 essential properties (even if empty)  
**GEDCOM imports**: Imported notes now have complete property sets with empty values for missing data  
**Consistency**: Uniform structure across manual and imported notes

### Benefits
- New notes won't show "Add essential properties" action
- Better Bases queries and Dataview compatibility
- Clearer structure for manual data entry
- No guessing about which properties to include

---

## Alternative Layout Algorithms

Choose from **four specialized layout algorithms** to visualize family trees in different ways:

### New Layouts

**Compact** - 50% tighter spacing for large trees
- Ideal for families with 50+ people
- Reduces canvas size while maintaining readability

**Timeline** - Chronological positioning by birth year
- X-axis: Birth year (shows who lived when)
- Y-axis: Generation number
- Intelligently estimates positions for missing birth dates from relatives
- Auto-fallback to generation-based layout when no dates available

**Hourglass** - Focus on one person's complete lineage
- Root person centered at Y=0
- Ancestors positioned above (negative Y)
- Descendants positioned below (positive Y)
- Each generation horizontally centered

### Standard (Default)
- Traditional family-chart layout with proper spouse handling
- Best for most family trees

### Enhanced Features
✅ Canvas filenames include layout type: `Family Tree - Name (timeline).canvas`  
✅ Layout type stored in canvas metadata for regeneration  
✅ Comprehensive documentation in Control Center Guide tab  

Select your preferred layout in the **Tree Output** tab's "Layout algorithm" dropdown.

---

## Technical Improvements

- Enhanced dual storage system checks in menu visibility logic
- Improved GEDCOM import data completeness with essential properties
- Refactored person note creation for consistency across all creation paths
- Updated Control Center tab naming throughout codebase

---

## Installation

**Via BRAT (Recommended)**:
1. Open Command Palette (Ctrl/Cmd + P)
2. Run: `BRAT: Check for updates to installed beta plugins`
3. BRAT will detect and install v0.2.3-beta

**Manual Update**:
1. Download `main.js`, `styles.css`, and `manifest.json` from this release
2. Replace files in `.obsidian/plugins/canvas-roots/`
3. Reload Obsidian (Ctrl/Cmd + R)

---

## Documentation

- [CHANGELOG](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md) - Complete version history
- [User Guide](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/blob/main/docs/user-guide.md) - Full workflow documentation
- [Roadmap](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/blob/main/docs/roadmap.md) - Planned features

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/compare/v0.2.2-beta...v0.2.3-beta
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.2.3-beta

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
# Canvas Roots v0.2.2-beta

This release brings bidirectional relationship syncing, enhanced GEDCOM import/export capabilities, and powerful new Obsidian Bases integration features for genealogy research and analysis.

## Major Features

### Bidirectional Relationship Sync

**Automatic relationship updates across all linked notes:**
- When you add a parent to a person, they automatically appear in that parent's children
- When you add a spouse, the relationship is automatically reciprocated
- When you add a child, they automatically get the correct parent link
- Works with indexed spouse properties (`spouse[0]`, `spouse[1]`, etc.)
- Automatic file watcher detects external edits and syncs relationships
- Post-GEDCOM import sync ensures all relationships are bidirectional
- **Relationship deletion sync** - removing a relationship updates both notes
- Relationship snapshots initialized on plugin load for change detection

### Enhanced GEDCOM Import/Export

**GEDCOM Export Enhancements:**
- Birth and death places now exported (`PLAC` tags)
- Occupation data exported (`OCCU` tags)
- Gender information exported
- Better support for genealogy software compatibility

**GEDCOM Import Enhancements:**
- **Pre-import validation** with detailed error and warning reporting
- **New Import Results Modal** showing comprehensive import summary with validation issues
- Import birth/death places, occupation, and gender data
- Improved child relationship extraction from family records
- Enhanced error handling with clearer user feedback
- Better data quality reporting (malformed data detection)

### Obsidian Bases Integration Improvements

**Six New Relationship Query Views:**
- **Single Parents** - People with children but no spouse
- **Childless Couples** - Married people without children
- **Multiple Marriages** - People with multiple spouses
- **Sibling Groups** - Grouped by parents with counts
- **Root Generation** - People without parents (structural tree roots)
- **Marked Root Persons** - People explicitly marked as research anchor points

**Root Person Marking Feature:**
- Mark people as "root persons" for lineage tracking
- Context menu action: "Mark as root person" / "Unmark as root person"
- Crown icon for easy identification
- Comprehensive documentation in Control Center Guide tab
- Supports multiple root persons for tracking different lineages

### Property Improvements

**Breaking Change - Automatic Migration:**
- Renamed `collection_name` property to `group_name` for semantic consistency
- Automatic migration runs on plugin load - existing data preserved
- UI terminology now matches property names throughout

## Documentation Updates

- New "Marking root people" section in Guide tab
- Updated all documentation to use `group_name` terminology
- Roadmap updated with completed features and future advanced features under consideration

## Compatibility

- Requires Obsidian 1.7.2 or higher
- Automatic property migration for `collection_name` → `group_name`
- GEDCOM 5.5.1 format maintained for maximum compatibility

## Notes

This is a beta release. Please report any issues on GitHub.
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.2.2-beta

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
# Release Notes for 0.2.1-beta

## Bug Fixes

### Excalidraw Export

- Fixed Excalidraw export format compatibility - exports now display correctly in the Excalidraw plugin
- Corrected opacity value from 0-1 scale to 0-100 scale
- Added missing required fields: frameId, rawText, autoResize, lineHeight, elbowed
- Added coordinate normalization to ensure exported elements are visible in viewport
- Added proper block reference IDs to text elements for Obsidian integration
- Person Picker UX
- Fixed person picker displaying cryptic cr_id values instead of birth/death dates
- Added proper Date object to string conversion for Obsidian's YAML date parsing
- Updated Control Center root person display to show dates with fallback to cr_id
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.2.1-beta

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
# Canvas Roots v0.2.0-beta

## Beta Release

Canvas Roots has transitioned from alpha to beta, reflecting the maturity and stability of its core feature set. All essential genealogical workflows are now production-ready.

### Why Beta?

- **Core features complete**: GEDCOM import/export, tree generation, canvas styling, and collections are fully functional
- **Production-ready**: Robust error handling, comprehensive validation, and proven stability
- **Real-world tested**: Alpha testing confirmed core workflows are solid and reliable
- **Feature-complete foundation**: Ready for everyday genealogical research in Obsidian

### What's Included in v0.2.0-beta

All features from v0.1.4-alpha plus improved stability:

**GEDCOM Support:**
- Full round-trip GEDCOM 5.5.1 import/export
- UUID preservation for reliable data integrity
- Collection metadata support
- Marriage data preservation

**Tree Generation:**
- Automated genealogical layouts (ancestors, descendants, full trees)
- Zero-overlap positioning
- Multi-family detection
- Collection-based filtering

**Canvas Integration:**
- Native Obsidian Canvas nodes
- Comprehensive styling options
- Non-destructive regeneration
- Context menu integration

**Data Management:**
- Collections with auto-detection
- Obsidian Bases compatibility
- Dual storage system (wikilinks + cr_id)
- YAML-first data model

### Upgrade Notes

- This is a smooth transition from alpha - no breaking changes
- All data from v0.1.x is fully compatible
- Simply install and use as before

### What's Next?

Future releases will focus on enhancements:
- Reference numbering systems (Ahnentafel, etc.)
- Person detail panels and interactive previews
- Privacy features for living people
- Advanced relationship modeling

---

**Installation**: Download `canvas-roots-v0.2.0-beta.zip`, extract to your vault's `.obsidian/plugins/canvas-roots/` directory, and enable the plugin in Settings.

**Note**: This is a beta release - suitable for production use. Core features are stable, but please report any issues on [GitHub](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues).
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.2.0-beta

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
# Canvas Roots v0.1.4-alpha

## GEDCOM Export

This release adds comprehensive GEDCOM export functionality, enabling users to export their family tree data to the industry-standard GEDCOM 5.5.1 format for sharing with other genealogy software or creating backups.

### Key Features

- **GEDCOM 5.5.1 format generation** with complete header and trailer
- **Individual record export** with name, sex, birth/death dates and places
- **Family record extraction** from parent-child and spouse relationships
- **UUID preservation** using custom `_UID` tags for round-trip compatibility
- **Collection code preservation** (`_COLL` and `_COLLN` tags) for organized exports
- **Marriage metadata export** including dates and locations from SpouseRelationship data
- **Sex inference** automatically determines M/F from father/mother relationships
- **Collection filtering** for selective export of specific family groups
- **Control Center UI** with export configuration options
- **Context menu integration** on folders ("Export GEDCOM from this folder")
- **Browser download** with proper `.ged` file extension
- **Comprehensive documentation** in the Guide tab

### Export Methods

1. **Control Center → GEDCOM tab**: Configure export options and export all people or filter by collection
2. **Folder context menu**: Right-click any folder → "Export GEDCOM from this folder" for quick exports

### Round-Trip Compatibility

Exported GEDCOM files can be re-imported back into Canvas Roots while preserving:
- Person UUIDs (via `_UID` custom tag)
- Collection assignments (via `_COLL` and `_COLLN` custom tags)
- All relationship data and metadata

This enables workflows like: Canvas Roots → GEDCOM → external genealogy software → GEDCOM → Canvas Roots, maintaining data integrity throughout.
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.1.4-alpha

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
# v0.1.3-alpha - Collections & Groups (Complete)

This release completes the Collections & Groups system with advanced analytics, visualization, and UI improvements.

## New Features

### Collections & Groups System (All Phases Complete)

**Organization & Browsing:**
- **Auto-detected family groups** with customizable group names via `collection_name` property
- **User-defined collections** for manual organization via `collection` property  
- **Context menu actions**: "Set group name" and "Add to collection"
- **Collections tab** with three browse modes:
  - All people (complete vault list)
  - Detected families (auto-detected groups)
  - My collections (user-defined collections)

**Advanced Features (Phase 3):**
- **Collection-based node coloring**: Hash-based consistent colors for multi-collection canvases (Canvas Settings → Node coloring → Collection)
- **Collection overview canvas**: Generate master canvas showing all collections with grid layout, visual connections, and statistics
- **Analytics dashboard**: Comprehensive statistics with data quality metrics
  - Total people, collections, families, and user collections
  - Average collection size with largest/smallest highlights
  - Data completeness percentages (birth dates, death dates, gender)
  - Relationship metrics (parents, spouses, children, orphaned people)
  - Cross-collection metrics (connections, bridge people, top connections)
  - Date range analysis (earliest/latest years, span)

**Cross-Collection Features:**
- **Connection detection** showing bridge people who link different collections
- **Collection filtering** in tree generation (works with all tree types)
- **Bridge person identification** with relationship counts and visual indicators
- Displays top 3 bridge people per connection with "+X more" notation

**Security & Privacy:**
- Log export obfuscation to protect PII (secure by default)
- Pattern-based PII detection (names, dates, UUIDs, paths)
- Toggle to export unobfuscated logs when needed

**Per-Canvas Style Settings (New):**
- Override global style settings for individual canvases
- 7 customizable style options (node coloring, arrows, edge colors, spouse settings)
- Set during tree generation or edit via context menu
- Style overrides stored in canvas metadata
- Preserved during canvas regeneration
- Fully backward compatible

### UI Polish

Refactored Control Center interface to use Obsidian's native `Setting` component throughout, providing better consistency with Obsidian's design language:

- **Canvas Settings tab**: Native dropdowns for arrow styles and color schemes, proper input controls for spacing/sizing
- **Data Entry tab**: Clean horizontal layout for person creation form
- **Tree Generation tab**: Streamlined configuration with native slider for generations, dropdowns for tree type and direction
- **Collections tab**: Replaced radio buttons with dropdown for browse mode
- **Advanced tab**: Native controls for log level and export directory

All tabs now feature the standard Obsidian horizontal layout (labels on left, controls on right) with automatic responsive behavior and better accessibility. Reduced code by 319 lines while improving consistency.

### Documentation
- Comprehensive Guide tab explaining groups vs collections with advanced features section
- New Collections & Groups section in user guide
- Complete architecture documentation (All Phases 1-3 implemented)

## Improvements
- Renamed "collection name" to "group name" in UI for better clarity
- Removed unreliable debug information card from Advanced tab

## What's Inside
This release implements the complete Collections architecture (Phases 1-3), providing powerful organization tools for genealogists managing multiple family lines and world-builders organizing fictional dynasties or factions.

## Learn More
- **Collections architecture**: [docs/architecture/collections.md](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/blob/main/docs/architecture/collections.md)
- **User guide**: [docs/user-guide.md](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/blob/main/docs/user-guide.md#collections--groups)
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.1.3-alpha

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
# Canvas Roots v0.1.2-alpha

Context menu enhancements for streamlined family tree management.

## New Features

### Person Note Context Menu
Right-click any person note to access:
- **Add relationship** - Quick submenu to add parent, spouse, or child relationships using the person picker
- **Validate relationships** - Check for broken `cr_id` references and missing bidirectional links
- **Find on canvas** - Search and jump to any canvas containing this person

### Folder Context Menu
Right-click any folder to:
- **Set as people folder** - One-click configuration of your default people folder
- **Import GEDCOM to this folder** - Pre-select folder and open GEDCOM import
- **Scan for relationship issues** - Batch validation of all person notes in the folder tree

### Canvas File Context Menu
Right-click canvas files (in file explorer or tab) to:
- **Regenerate canvas** - Re-run layout algorithm with current relationship data
- **Show tree statistics** - View person count, generation depth, and edge count

## Platform Support
All context menu features support both desktop (nested submenus) and mobile (flat menus with "Canvas Roots:" prefix).

## What's Next
Collections management with custom naming and advanced organization features.

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/compare/v0.1.1...v0.1.2
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.1.2-alpha

## Canvas Roots 0.1.1-alpha

Minor alpha release with Obsidian Bases integration and documentation improvements.

### New Features

**Obsidian Bases Template**
- Added "Create Base template" command to Command Palette
- New "Data Tools" card in Quick Actions tab with one-click template creation
- Pre-configured Base template with 6 views:
  - All Family Members, Living Members, Deceased Members
  - Recently Added, Missing Parents, Incomplete Data
- Includes calculated formulas for lifespan, age, and date formatting
- Template creates `family-members.base` in vault root

### Documentation Changes

- Clarified alpha vs. planned features in README
- Softened Bases integration language to reflect compatibility (not yet fully tested)
- Removed view-specific language ("table view" → general compatibility)

### Technical Changes

- Updated minimum Obsidian version to 1.7.2 (required for Bases support)
- Added BASE_TEMPLATE constant with complete template definition

### Installation

Install via [BRAT](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat):
1. Install BRAT from Community Plugins
2. Run command: `BRAT: Add a beta plugin for testing`
3. Enter: `https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots`

### Documentation

- [Known Limitations](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/blob/main/docs/known-limitations.md)
- [Bases Integration Guide](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/blob/main/docs/bases-integration.md)
- [Security Policy](SECURITY.md) with guidance on PII handling, data privacy, and security best practices for genealogical research

---

**Note:** This is an alpha release. Core features (GEDCOM import, tree generation, canvas styling) are stable and tested. See [Known Limitations](https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/blob/main/docs/known-limitations.md) for details on planned features.
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.1.1-alpha

releases.author: banisterious
releases.description:
# Canvas Roots v0.1.0-alpha

> **Alpha Release:** This is an early alpha release. Core features are stable and well-tested, but some advanced features are not yet implemented. See [Known Limitations](docs/known-limitations.md) for details.

## First Alpha Release

Canvas Roots is now available for alpha testing! This plugin automatically generates genealogical family trees on Obsidian Canvas with specialized layout algorithms designed for complex family relationships.

## Core Features (Stable & Tested)

### GEDCOM Import
- ✅ Import GEDCOM 5.5.1 files with exceptional error handling
- ✅ 100% success rate on malformed data with graceful degradation
- ✅ Dual storage system (wikilinks + `cr_id`) for robust relationships
- ✅ Enhanced import feedback showing data quality issues
- ✅ Perfect preservation of special characters and edge cases

### Tree Generation
- ✅ Zero-overlap layouts using family-chart genealogical algorithms
- ✅ Support for ancestors, descendants, and full family trees
- ✅ Multi-family detection with helpful user notices
- ✅ Fast performance (0.005s layout, 0.4s import for small trees)
- ✅ Generation limit controls and spouse inclusion options

### Canvas Styling
- ✅ Gender-based node coloring (green/purple) or generation-based
- ✅ Customizable edge colors and arrow styles (directed, bidirectional, undirected)
- ✅ Separate styling for parent-child vs. spouse relationships
- ✅ JSON Canvas 1.0 specification compliant

### Canvas Management
- ✅ Regenerate existing canvases with updated data and settings
- ✅ Preserve tree metadata (type, root person, generations)
- ✅ Non-destructive updates with layout direction switching
- ✅ Recent trees history for quick navigation

## Testing Results

**Baseline Test (11 people):**
- Zero overlapping nodes ✅
- Import: 0.45s, Layout: 0.005s ✅
- Perfect visual quality ✅

**Malformed GEDCOM Test (13 people, 10+ edge cases):**
- 100% import success rate ✅
- All edge cases handled gracefully ✅
- Zero crashes ✅
- Production-ready error handling ✅

## Installation

### Using BRAT (Recommended)

1. Install [BRAT](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat) from Community Plugins
2. Run command: `BRAT: Add a beta plugin for testing`
3. Enter: `https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots`
4. Enable Canvas Roots in Settings → Community Plugins

### Manual Installation

1. Download `canvas-roots.zip` from this release
2. Extract to `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/canvas-roots/`
3. Reload Obsidian (Ctrl/Cmd + R)
4. Enable in Settings → Community Plugins

## Documentation

- **[README](README.md)** - Overview, features, workflow
- **[Known Limitations](docs/known-limitations.md)** - What's working vs. planned
- **[Development Guide](docs/development.md)** - For contributors
- **[Technical Specification](docs/specification.md)** - Complete feature roadmap

## Known Limitations

Not yet implemented (planned for future releases):
- Collections management (folder-based organization)
- GEDCOM export (import-only in alpha)
- Reference numbering systems (Dollarhide-Cole, Ahnentafel)
- Person detail panel
- D3 tree view preview

See [Known Limitations](docs/known-limitations.md) for complete details and workarounds.

## Reporting Issues

Please report bugs at: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/issues

Include:
- Plugin version (0.1.0-alpha)
- Obsidian version
- Console errors (Ctrl/Cmd + Shift + I)
- Exported logs from Control Center → Status tab

## Acknowledgments

- [family-chart](https://github.com/donatso/family-chart) library for genealogical layout algorithms
- Obsidian community for feedback and testing

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/banisterious/obsidian-canvas-roots/commits/v0.1.0-alpha
releases.name: Canvas Roots v0.1.0-alpha
